Cardiology

571 Questions

Q1

Pulsus bisiferiens is seen in all, EXCEPT:

  • A. Aortic regurgitation + Aortic stenosis
  • B. Aortic regurgitation (NBE, AIIMS)
  • C. Hypertrophic cardiomyopathy
  • D. Tetralogy of Fallot
Q2

Which of the following statements about Pulsus Bisiferiensis not true:** (NBE)

  • A. It is seen in aortic regurgitation
  • B. It is best felt in brachial artery
  • C. It has one peak in systole and one in diastole
  • D. It has a percussion wave and a tidal wave
Q3

Bisfiriens pulse is seen in all Except:** (PGI Dec 05)

  • A. AS + AR
  • B. AR
  • D. TOF
Q4

Pulsus Bisiferiens is best felt in:** (NBE)

  • A. Carotid artery
  • B. Brachial artery
  • C. Popliteal Artery
  • D. Femoral artery
Q5

Pulsus paradoxus is seen in all except :** (AIIMS June 98)

  • A. IPPV
  • B. COPD
  • C. Cardiac Tamponade
  • D. Constrictive pericarditis
Q6

Pulsus paradoxus is present in all Except:** (PGI Dec 05)

  • A. Emphysema
  • B. Pulmonary embolism
  • C. Hypovolemic shock
  • D. Hypertrophic cardiomyopathy
Q7

True about pulsus paradoxus is —** (PGI June 98)

  • A. Arm-tongue circulation time is increased
  • B. ↑ Stroke volume
  • C. Seen in constrictive pericarditis
  • D. ↑ HR
Q8

Pulsus paradoxus is seen in (select three correct options)—** (PGI June 06)

  • A. Cardiac tamponade
  • B. Constrictive pericarditis
  • C. HOCM
  • D. AR
  • E. Severe asthma
Q9

Pulsus alternans occurs in –** (PGI-June 98)

  • A. Constrictive pericarditis
  • B. Viral myocarditis
  • C. Hypokalemia
  • D. MI
Q10

All of the following statements about Pulsus Bigeminus are true, except:** (NBE)

  • A. Must be distinguished from Pulsus Alternans
  • B. Is a sign of digitalis toxicity
  • C. Compensatory pause is absent
  • D. Rhythm is Irregular
Q11

Pulsus Bigeminus is seen in therapy with:** (NBE)

  • A. Digitalis
  • B. Beta Blockers
  • C. ACE Inhibitors
  • D. Calcium Channel Blockers
Q12

Water hammer pulse is seen in-** (AIIMS May 07)

  • A. Aortic stenosis
  • B. Aortic regurgitation
  • C. Aortic stenosis and Aortic regurgitation
  • D. Mitral regurgitation
Q13

Double apical impulse is seen in –** (PGI Dec-98)

  • A. MR
  • C. AS
  • D. MS
Q14

Pulse pressure is:** (AIIMS May 93)

  • A. 1/3 diastolic + 1/2 systolic B.P.
  • B. 1/2 diastolic + 1/3 systolic B.P.
  • C. Systolic – Diastolic B.P.
  • D. Diastolic + 1/2 systolic B.P.
Q15

Wide pulse pressure may be seen in all Except:** (AI 1991)

  • A. Aortic Regurgitation
  • B. PDA
  • C. Complete Heart Block
  • D. Congestive Heart failure
Q16

Wide pulse pressure is seen in all except:** (NBE)

  • A. PDA
  • B. Aortic stenosis
  • C. Aortic Regurgitation
  • D. A.V. malformation
Q17

Pulse deficit of more than 10 per minute is seen in:** (NBE)

  • A. Ventricular Premature Contraction (VPC)
  • B. Bradyarrhythmias
  • C. Atrial Fibrillation (AF)
  • D. Ventricular Fibrillation (VF)
Q18

Pulse deficit may be seen in all of the following, Except :** (NBE)

  • D. Tachyarrhythmias
Q19

All of the following statements about Pulse deficit in Atrial Fibrillation are true, except:** (NBE)

  • A. Usually more than 10/minute
  • B. Increases with faster ventricular rates
  • C. May be absent in digitalis intoxication
  • D. Radial pulse rate may be more than ventricular pulse rate
Q20

All of the following phases of the jugular venous pulse and their causes are correctly matched, except: (AI 2002)

  • A. ‘c’ wave - onset of atrial systole
  • B. ‘a-x’ descent - atrial relaxation
  • C. ‘v-y’ - emptying of blood from right atrium into right ventricle
  • D. ‘y-a’ ascent - filling of the right atrium from the vena cava
Q21

C’ Wave in Jugular Venous Pulse (JVP) waveform is due to:** (NBE)

  • A. Atrial contraction
  • B. Tricuspid bulging
  • C. Systolic filling of atrium
  • D. Atrial Relaxation
Q22

Which of the following is the correct statement regarding findings in JVP:** (AI 2002)

  • A. Cannon wave : Complete heart block
  • B. Slow vy descent : Tricuspid regurgitation
  • C. Giant c wave : Tricuspid stenosis
  • D. Increased JVP with prominent pulsations : SVC obstruction
Q23

Giant ‘a’ waves in JVP occur in all except:** (AI 1996)

  • A. Junctional rhythm
  • B. Pulmonary hypertension
  • C. Tricuspid regurgitation
  • D. Complete heart block
Q24

In the JVP, ‘a’ waves are absent in :** (AI 1994)

  • A. Atrial fibrillation
  • B. Mitral stenosis
  • C. Tricuspid atresia
  • D. Sick sinus syndrome
Q25

‘C’ wave in JVP is due to-** (AIIMS Nov 07)

  • B. Tricuspid valve bulging into right atrium
  • C. Right atrial filling
  • D. Rapid ventricular filling
Q26

C wave in JVP indicates:** (AI 2009)

  • B. Bulging of tricuspid valve
  • C. Ventricle systole
Q27

Typical JVP finding in cardiac tamponade –** (PGI-June-2000)

  • A. Absent ‘Y’ descent
  • B. Prominent ‘a’ wave
  • C. Absent ‘a’ wave
  • D. Prominent ‘Y’ wave
Q28

Frog sign is best related to:** (NBE P)

  • A. Sinus Arrest
  • B. PSVT
  • C. VT
  • D. PVC
Q29

Erb’s Point in cardiology refers to:** (NBE)

  • A. Right 2nd intercostal space
  • B. Left 2nd intercostal space
  • C. Right 3rd intercostal space
  • D. Left 3rd intercostal space
Q30

S2 is best appreciated in:** (NBE)

  • A. 3rd left intercostal space
  • B. 2nd right intercostal space
  • C. 4th left intercostal space
  • D. 5 left intercostal space
Q31

P2 is best appreciated in :** (NBE)

  • A. 2nd left intercostal space
Q32

All of the following are true about S1, except:** (AI 1991)

  • A. Lower frequency than S2
  • B. Caused by closure of mitral valve
  • C. Heard at the end of ventricular systole
  • D. Better heard with diaphragm of stethoscope
Q33

First heart sound is soft in all, except:** (AIIMS Dec 95)

  • A. Short PR interval
  • B. Ventricular septal defect
  • C. Mitral regurgitation
  • D. Calcified valve
Q34

Causes of soft S1 include:** (PGI June 97)

  • A. Long standing severe MS
  • B. Obesity
  • C. Mitral Regurgitation
  • D. Pleural effusion
  • E. All of the above
Q35

All of the above Loud S1 is caused by (select two correct options):** (PGI Dec 04)

  • A. Calcified mitral valve.
  • B. MVP
  • C. Short Pr interval
  • D. Tachycardia
  • E. Dilatation or widening of mitral valve after valvotomy
Q36

Loud S1 in Mitral stenosis is caused by:

  • A. Prolonged flow through mitral valve
  • B. 1st degree heart block (PGI June 99)
  • C. Calcification of the valve
  • D. Immobilization of valve
Q37

Reverse splitting of first heart sound in heard in (select two correct options):** (PGI June 04)

  • A. RBBB
  • B. LBBB
  • C. Tricuspid stenosis
  • E. Atrial myxoma
Q38

Paradoxical splitting of second heart sound is seen in?** (NBE)

  • B. ASD
  • C. LBBB
  • D. VSD
Q39

Wide-split second heart sound is seen in (select two options)—** (PGI Dec 02)

  • A. ASD
  • C. PDA
  • D. MR
  • E. PS
Q40

Wide-splitting of S2 is seen in (select three correct options):** (PGI Dec 06)

  • B. MR
  • D. PS
  • E. LBBB
Q41

Fixed splitting of S2 may be seen in all except:** (PGI June 95)

  • A. Pulmonary embolism
  • B. PS
  • C. ASD
  • D. LBBB
Q42

Wide fixed split S2 occurs in:** (AIIMS May 93)

  • A. VSD (ventricular septal defect)
  • C. ASD (Atrial septal defect)
  • D. Coarctation of aorta
Q43

Reverse splitting of S2 is seen in all except (select two options):** (PGI Dec 05)

  • A. LBBB
  • B. WPW type A
  • C. LV pacing
  • D. Systemic hypertension
  • E. Post-stenotic dilatation in AS
Q44

Single second heart sound is seen in (select 3 options):** (PGI Dec 04)

  • A. TOF
  • B. Pulmonary arterial hypertension
  • C. Pulmonary atresia
  • D. Corrected TGA
  • E. Severe pulmonary stenosis
Q45

Loud pulmonary component of second heart sound heard in (select two options) –** (PGI June 04)

  • A. Pulmonary hypertension
  • B. TOF
  • C. Eisenmenger’s syndrome
  • D. Pulmonary stenosis
  • E. AS
Q46

Third heart sound is seen in all except :** (AIIMS Dec 98)

  • A. Athletes
  • C. Constrictive pericarditis
  • D. LVF
Q47

All of the following statements about third Heart sound (S3) are true, except:** (AI 2010)

  • A. Occurs due to rapid filling of the ventricles during atrial systole
  • B. Seen in in Constrictive Pericarditis
  • C. Seen in Atrial Septal Defect (ASD)
  • D. Seen in Ventricular Septal Defect (VSD)
Q48

True about third heart sound is –** (PGI Dec-98)

  • A. Absent in Chr. Constrictive pericarditis
  • B. Absent in aortic aneurysm
  • C. Absent in MS
  • D. Normal physiologically in Athletes
Q49

Which of the following is true about fourth heart sound ‘S4’ :** (AI 07)

  • A. Can be heard by the unaided ear
  • B. Frequency is greater than 20 Hz
  • C. Heard during ventricular filling phase
  • D. Heard during ventricular ejection phase
Q50

S4 is seen in all of the following, Except:** (AIIMS May 94)

  • A. Thyrotoxicosis
  • B. Acute MI
  • C. Atrial fibrillation
Q51

S4 is not seen in :** (AIIMS May 95)

  • A. Ventricular aneurysm
  • B. Mitral regurgitation
  • D. Hypertension
Q52

All of the following heart sounds occur shortly after S2 except:** (AI 2003)

  • A. Opening snap
  • B. Pericardial knock
  • C. Ejection click
  • D. Tumor plop
Q53

Following is true regarding opening snap :** (AIIMS Nov 02)

  • A. It is a high-pitched diastolic sound.
  • B. It s due to opening of stenosed aortic valve.
  • C. It indicates pulmonary arterial hypertension.
  • D. It precedes the aortic component of second heart sound.
Q54

All of the following are diastolic sounds, Except:** (PGI- Dec-06)

  • A. S3
  • B. S4
  • C. Opening snap
  • D. Ejection click
Q55

Midsystolic click is characteristic feature of:** (NBE)

  • A. MVP
  • B. MS
  • C. AR
  • D. PDA
Q56

Which of the following statements is true about Click and murmur of MVP after squatting:** (NBE)

  • A. Click is delayed and murmur is prolonged
  • B. Click occurs early and murmur is prolonged
  • C. Click is delayed and murmur is shortened
  • D. Click occurs early and murmur is shortened
Q57

Systolic thrill in left 2nd and 3rd intercostals space may be seen in all of the following, Except:** (AI 2009)

  • A. Subpulmonic VSD
  • B. Pulmonic stenosis
  • C. Ebstein’s anomaly
  • D. Pink TOF
Q58

An early systolic murmur may be caused by all of the following except:** (AI 2003)

  • A. Small ventricular septal defect
  • B. Papillary muscle dysfunction
  • D. Aortic stenosis
Q59

All of the following murmurs may be heard in patients with aortic regurgitation except :** (AIIMS Nov 02)

  • A. High-pitched decrescendo diastolic murmur.
  • B. Soft, low pitched mid distolic rumbling murmur.
  • C. Mid-systolic ejection flow murmur
  • D. Pansystolic murmur.
Q60

Mid-diastolic Murmur with presystolic accentuation is typically seen in:** (NBE)

  • A. Mitral stenosis
  • B. Mitral Regurgitation
  • C. Aortic stenosis
  • D. MVP
Q61

What is false in relation to Carey Coombs Murmur?** (AIIMS Nov 06)

  • A. Delayed Diastolic Murmur
  • B. Seen in Rheumatic Fever
  • C. Associated with AR
  • D. Low Pitched Murmur
Q62

Carey coomb murmur is seen in –** (NBE)

  • A. Severe mitral stenosis
  • B. Acute rheumatic carditis
  • C. Pure aortic regurgitation
  • D. Severe pulmonary HT
Q63

Rytand’s murmur is seen in –** (NBE)

  • A. A-V Block
  • B. Mitral Stenosis
  • C. Aortic Stenosis
  • D. Aortic Regurgitation
Q64

Which of the following best relates to Cabot-Locke murmur:** (NBE)

  • A. Hyperthyroidism
  • B. Anemia
  • C. Pericarditis
  • D. Endocarditis
Q65

Pericardial friction rub is best heard at:** (NBE)

  • A. Apex
  • B. Left lower sternal border
  • C. Right sternal border
  • D. Left second intercostal space
Q66

Pericardial knock is usually present in:** (NBE)

  • A. Cardiac Tamponade
  • B. Constrictive Pericarditis
  • C. Restrictive Cardiomyopathy
  • D. RVMI
Q67

A new systolic murmur after Acute myocardial infarction may be due to all of the following Except: (AIIMS May 1993)

  • A. Complete heart block
  • B. Rupture of Interventricular septum
  • C. Papillary muscle dysfunction
  • D. Ischemic cardiomyopathy
Q68

In which of the following condition cardiac murmur is increased on standing position?** (NBE)

  • A. Hypertrophic obstructive cardiomyopathy
Q69

Which of the following murmurs increase with Valsalva maneuver?** (AIIMS Nov 2010)

  • B. VSD
  • D. HOCM
Q70

A young patient presents with a systolic murmur at the apex. The murmur increases on both handgrip and valsalva maneuver. Which of the following conditions is most likely?** (AI 04)

  • A. HOCM
  • B. AS
  • C. MVP
Q71

Which of the following murmurs increase with valsalva maneovre:** (AIIMS May 09)

  • B. HOCM
Q72

Continuous murmur is found in all, EXCEPT:** (AIIMS May 93)

  • A. Mitral stenosis with mitral regurgitation
  • B. Patent ductus arteriosus
  • C. Rupture of sinus of Valsalva
  • D. Systemic arteriovenous (AV) fistula
Q73

Continuous murmur is seen in all the following except:** (AI 1995)

  • A. Aortic sinus of valsalva rupture
  • B. Coarctation of Aorta
  • C. AV malformations
  • D. Peripheral pulmonary stenosis
Q74

A continuous murmur is heard in all of the following conditions except:** (AIIMS May 05)

  • A. Ventricular septal defect with aortic regurgitation
  • C. Coronary arteriovenous fistula
  • D. Venous hum
Q75

Nobel Price for invention of Electrocardiogram was awarded to:** (NBE)

  • A. Hewlet
  • B. Judkin
  • C. Einthoven
  • D. White
Q76

Normal Axis of the heart is best described as being between:** (NBE)

  • A. -30 to +90
  • B. +30 to -90
  • C. -30 to -90
  • D. +90 to ±180
Q77

Normal heart axis is:** (NBE)

  • A. -90 to + 90°
  • B. -90 to + 180°
  • C. -30 to +90°
  • D. -90 to 180°
Q78

-30 TO -60 degree axis deviation indicates:** (NBE)

  • A. Left Axis Deviation
  • B. Right Axis Deviation
  • C. Extreme Right Axis Deviation
  • D. Normal Cardiac Axis
Q79

-30 TO -60 degree axis deviation is seen in:** (NBE)

  • A. Left Ventricle Hypertrophy
  • B. Right Ventricle Hypertrophy
  • C. Left Posterior Fascicular Block
  • D. Lateral wall Myocardial Infarction
Q80

Left Axis Deviation is seen as:** (NBE)

  • A. Positive in Lead I and Positive in Lead II
  • B. Positive in Lead I and Negative in Lead II
  • C. Negative in Lead I and Negative in Lead II
  • D. Negative in Lead I and positive in Lead II
Q81

P wave is due to:** (AI 99)

  • A. Atrial depolarization
  • B. Atrial repolarization
  • C. Ventricular depolarization
  • D. Ventricular repolarization
Q82

The normal P wave is inverted in lead :** (NBE)

  • A. LI
  • B. LII
  • C. aVF
  • D. aVR
Q83

The normal P wave is biphasic in lead:** (NBE)

  • A. V1
Q84

Absent P Wave is seen in:** (NBE)

  • A. Atrial Fibrillation
  • B. Cor-pulmonale
  • C. Mitral Stenosis
  • D. COPD
Q85

Pseudo-P-Pulmonale is typically seen in:** (NBE)

  • A. Hypokalemia
  • B. Hyponatremia
  • C. Hypocalcemia
  • D. Hypercalcemia
Q86

A patient in regular rhythm presents with absent P waves on ECG. Leads II, III and AVF reveal a Saw- Tooth Pattern. Which of the following is the most likely diagnosis:** (NBE)

  • B. Atrial Flutter with Variable Block
  • C. Atrial Flutter with Fixed Block
  • D. Multifocal Atrial Tachcardia
Q87

A wide and notched P wave is typically seen in:** (NBE)

  • A. Mitral Stenosis
  • B. Cor-Pulmonale
  • C. COPD
  • D. Pulmonary embolism
Q88

QRS complex indicates-** (AI 98)

  • A. Atrial repolarization
  • B. Atrial depolarization
  • C. Ventricular repolarization
  • D. Ventricular depolarization
Q89

A QRS duration between 100 and 120 milliseconds suggests all of the following, Except:** (NBE)

  • A. Normal
  • B. Left anterior Fascicular Block
  • C. Left posterior Fascicular Block
  • D. Left Bundle Branch Block
Q90

Wide QRS complex [≥ 0.12 seconds] may be seen in all of the following EXCEPT:** (NBE)

  • A. Hyperkalemia
  • B. Wolf Parkinson White Syndrome
  • C. Ventricular Tachycardia
  • D. Left Anterior Fascicular Block
Q91

Wide QRS complex is typically seen in:** (NBE)

  • A. Bundle Branch block
  • B. Sick sinus sydrome
  • C. Mobitz Type I block
  • D. Mobitz Type II block
Q92

Low QRS voltage on ECG indicates:** (NBE)

  • B. Pericardial effusion
  • C. Cor pulmonale
  • D. Infective endocarditis
Q93

The ECG of a 40 year old male was recorded using standard bipolar limb leads. The sum of voltage of the three standard leads was found to be 5 millivolts. This indicates:** (AIIMS May 05)

  • A. A normal heart
  • B. Right ventricular hypertrophy
  • C. Left ventricular hypertrophy
  • D. Increased cardiac muscle mass
Q94

Low QRS voltage on ECG with left ventricular hypertrophy on Echocardiography suggests a diagnosis of:** (NBE)

  • A. Pericardial effusion
  • B. Cardiac Amyloidosis
  • C. Corpulmonale
Q95

In LVH, SV1 + RV 6 is more than ________ mm?** (NBE)

  • A. 25
  • B. 30
  • C. 35
  • D. 40
Q96

Tall T waves on ECG are seen in:** (NBE)

  • B. Hypokalemia
  • C. Hypercalcemia
  • D. Hypocalcemia
Q97

ST elevation is seen in all of the following conditions except :** (AIIMS 2002)

  • A. Myocardial infarction
  • B. Coronary artery spasm
  • D. Ventricular aneurysm
Q98

All of the following may cause ST segment elevation on EKG, except :** (AI 2005)

  • A. Early repolarization variant.
  • B. Constrictive pericarditis.
  • C. Ventricular aneurysm.
  • D. Prizmetal angina.
Q99

What is diagnostic of fresh myocardial infarction in ECG :** (AIIMS May 95)

  • A. QT interval prolongation
  • B. P mitrale
  • C. ST segment elevation
  • D. ST segment depression
Q100

Reciprocal changes in ECG in patients with inferior wall myocardial infarction are seen in which leads-

  • A. Lead I & aVL
  • B. Lead II
  • C. Lead III
  • D. Lead aVF
Q101

QT prolongation is seen in all, except :** (AIIMS June 2000)

  • A. Hypothermia
  • B. Digitalis toxicity
  • D. Romanowand syndrome
Q102

QT interval is shortened in:** (AI 95)

  • A. Hypocalcaemia
  • D. Hyperkalemia
Q103

Hypercalcemia in ECG is diagnosed by:** (PGI Dec-99)

  • A. Increased QT interval
  • B. Decreased QT interval
  • C. Increased PR interval
  • D. Tall T waves
Q104

Hypocalcemia is characterized by all of the following features except-** (AI 03)

  • A. Numbness and tingling of circumoral region
  • B. Hyperactivity tendon reflexes
  • C. Shortening of Q-T interval in ECG
  • D. Carpopedal spasm
Q105

All are ECG changes in hypokalemia, except:** (AIIMS Dec 97)

  • A. U wave
  • B. ST segment sagging
  • C. T-wave flattening or inversion
  • D. QT interval prolongation
Q106

Which of the following represents the earliest ECG Change in a patient with Hypokalemia:** (NBE)

  • A. Pseudo-P-Pulmonale
  • B. Flattening of T wave
  • C. Development of U wave
  • D. Fusion of TU Waves
Q107

The following ECG findings are seen in Hypokalemia:** (AI 2007)

  • A. Increased PR interval with ST depression
  • B. Increased PR interval with peaked T wave
  • C. Prolonged QT interval with T wave inversion
  • D. Decreased QT interval with ST depression
Q108

All of the following are the electrocardiographic features of Hyperkalemia, except-** (AIIMS May 04)

  • A. Prolonged PR interval
  • B. Prolonged QT interval
  • C. Sine wave patterns
  • D. Loss of P waves
Q109

All of the following may occur due to hyperkalemia, except:** (AI 06)

  • B. Prolonged QRS interval
  • C. Prolonged QT interval
  • D. Ventricular asystole
Q110

All of the following electrocardiographic findings may represent manifestations of digitalis intoxication, except:** (AIIMS Nov 99)

  • A. Bigeminy
  • B. Junctional tachycardia
  • C. Atrial flutter
  • D. Atrial tachycardia with variable block
Q111

Osborne waves in ECG are seen in:** (NBE Repeat)

  • A. Hypothyroidism
  • B. Hypothermia
  • D. Hypokalemia
Q112

Epsilon wave is seen in:** (NBE)

  • A. Brugada syndrome
  • C. WPW syndrome
  • D. Arrhythmogenic right ventricular dysplasia
Q113

All of the following conditions are more commonly associated with LBBB pattern than RBBB Pattern on ECG, Except:** (AIIMS 2013)

  • A. Acute MI
  • B. Aortic valve disease
  • C. Lev Disease
  • D. Ashman Phenomenon
Q114

Idiopathic degeneration of the proximal bundle branch fibres is known as:** (AIIMS 2013)

  • A. Lev’s Disease
  • B. Lenegre’s Disease
  • C. Ashman Phenomenon
  • D. Brugada Syndrome
Q115

Brugada Syndrome is associated with:** (AIIMS 2013)

  • A. Left Bundle Branch Block
  • B. Left Anterior Fascicular block
  • C. Left Posterior Fascicular block
  • D. Right Bundle Branch Block
Q116

Which of the following arrhythmias is most frequent in Holiday heart syndrome:** (NBE)

  • A. Atrial Flutter
  • B. Atrial Fibrillation
  • C. Ventricular Premature Depolarization
  • D. Ventricular Tachycardia
Q117

Holiday heart syndrome is the term used for cardiotoxicity produced by:** (NBE)

  • A. Alcohol
  • B. Tobacco
  • C. Cocaine
  • D. Amphetamine
Q119

Following ECG manifestations are seen in acute pericarditis except -

  • A. ST segment elevation with upward concavity in V2 -6
  • B. ST depression in aVR
  • C. PR Depression
  • D. Prolongation of QRS complexes
Q120

In left sided massive pneumothorax, ECG shows all, except:** (AIIMS May 94)

  • A. Left axis deviation
  • B. Absent R wave
  • C. Pathological Q waves
  • D. Precordial T wave inversion
Q121

Which of the following ECG changes is least likely in a patient with left pneumothorax :** (AIIMS Nov. 2000)

  • A. Inversion of T wave
  • B. Left axis deviation
  • C. Small R wave
  • D. Electrical alternans
Q122

Atrial fibrillation may occur in all the following conditions, except-** (AIIMS May 03)

  • B. Hypothyroidism
  • C. Dilated cardiomyopathy
Q123

Most common in Arrhythmia in ICU Patients is:** (NBE)

  • C. PSVT
  • D. NPAT
Q124

A chronic alcoholic develops palpitations suddenly after alcohol binge. Which of the following arrhythmia is most commonly associated with alcohol binge in the alcoholics?** (AIIMS Nov 01)

  • A. Ventricular fibrillations
  • B. Verticular premature contractions
  • D. Atrial fibrillation
Q125

Which of the following agents should not be used for oral anticoagulation in high risk patients with Atrial Fibrillation:

  • A. Wafarin
  • B. Dabigatran
  • C. Rivoraxaban
  • D. Clopidogrel
Q126

All of the following are true about Atrial Fibrillation, Except:** (NBE)

  • A. ↑ risk of thromboembolism
  • B. digoxin for treatment
  • C. anticoagulation not required
  • D. aspirin in low risk patients
Q127

A person with mitral regurgitation and atrial fibrillation presents with syncope. On examination the person has a heart rate of 55. What is the most probable cause?** (AI 2007)

  • A. Digitalis toxicity
  • B. Incomplete heart block
  • C. Stroke
  • D. Subarachnoid Haemorrhage
Q128

In a patient with chronic atrial fibrillation with a regular beat of 60/min, the most probable cause is: (AIIMS May 01)

  • A. Sleep
  • C. Sino nodal block
  • D. Hypothyroidism
Q129

Drug of choice in PSVT is :** (AI, AIIMS, NBE)

  • A. Amiodarone
  • B. Lignocaine
  • C. Quinidine
  • D. Adenosine
Q130

Drug of choice for maintenance therapy in PSVT IS:** (NBE)

  • C. Verapamil
Q131

Ventricular tachycardia is defined as:** (NBE)

  • A. ≥ 3 consecutive VPCs at a rate >100/min
  • B. ≥ 5 consecutive VPCs at a rate >150/min
  • C. ≥ 3 consecutive VPCs at a rate >200/min
  • D. ≥ 5 consecutive VPCs at a rate >250/min
Q132

Sustained VT is defined as VT that persists for:** (NBE)

  • A. 10 seconds
  • B. 15 seconds
  • C. 20 seconds
  • D. 30 seconds
Q133

All of the following features can differentiate between ventricular tachycardia and supraventricular tachycardia except:** (AIIMS Nov 98)

  • A. QRs < 0.14 seconds
  • B. Ventricular rate > 100/min
  • C. Variable first heart sound
  • D. Relieved by carotid sinus massage
Q134

In a patient with wide-complex tachycardia, the presence of all of the following in the ECG indicates ventricular tachycardia except:** (AIIMS Nov 03)

  • A. Atrioventricular dissociation
  • B. Fusion beats
  • C. Typical right bundle branch block
  • D. Capture beats
Q135

Board complex tachycardia, due to ventricular tachycardia is suggested by all except:** (NBE)

  • A. Fusion Beats
  • B. AV dissociation
  • C. Capture Beats
  • D. Termination of tachycardia by carotid Sinus massage
Q136

Broad complex tachycardia due to ventricular tachycardia is suggested by all of the following except: (NBE)

  • D. Triphasic QRS in V1
Q137

Ventricular Tachycardia is represented by all, Except:** (PGI 1996)

  • A. Wide QRS complex
  • D. Fusion beats
  • E. Typical RBBB QRS complex
Q138

Which of the following ECG features are not seen in patients with ventricular tachycardia:** (NBE)

  • A. Bizarre QRS complexes
  • B. Presence of AV dissociation [fusion beats]
  • C. Prolonged duration of QRS complexes
  • D. P-Pulmonale
Q139

Which of the following statements about Premature Ventricular Beats is false:** (PGI 1997)

  • A. Sequential depolarization of ventricles
  • B. Wide, Bizzare, Notched QRS complexes
  • C. Prevalence decreases with age
  • D. Palpitations is a common presenting feature
Q140

All of the following are features of Premature Ventricular Complexes, Except:** (AI 1993)

  • B. Absent P wave
  • C. Complete compensatory pause
  • D. Prolonged PR interval
Q141

Accelerated Idioventricular Rhythm (AIVR) is the most common arrhythmia associated with:** (AI 2010)

  • A. Dilated cardiomyopathy
  • B. Myocardial Reperfusion
  • C. Digitalis Intoxication
  • D. Myocarditis
Q142

Which of the following is not true about Torsades de pointes?** (NBE)

  • A. Type of supraventricular tachycardia
  • B. Polymorphic QRS complexes
  • C. Prolonged QT interval on ECG
  • D. Pathological U waves
Q143

Feature of Torsade de pointes is:** (AIIMS Dec 97)

  • B. Short QRS complex
  • C. Prolonged QTc interval
  • D. Short QTc interval
Q144

Congenital long QT syndrome can lead to:** (AIIMS May 2003)

  • B. Polymorphic ventricular tachycardia
  • C. Acute myocardial infarction
  • D. Recurrent supraventricular tachycardia
Q145

Torsade-de-pointes is caused by :** (PGI June 99)

  • A. Hypermagnesemia
  • B. Metabolic acidosis
  • C. Hypomagnesemia
  • D. Metabolic alkalosis
Q146

The most common accessory pathways leading to WPW syndrome is:** (NBE)

  • A. Left free wall
  • B. Posteroseptal
  • C. Right free wall
  • D. Anteroseptal
Q147

Bundle of KENT is involved in which of the following disease?** (NBE)

  • A. WPW syndrome
  • B. Tako-Tsubo cardiomyopathy
  • C. Arrhythmogenic Right Ventricular Dysplasia
Q148

Type B pre excitation in WPW syndrome is caused by:** (NBE)

  • A. Right Bundle Branch Block
  • B. Right sided accessory pathway
  • C. Left sided accessory pathway
  • D. Left bundle Branch block
Q149

The most common reentrant tachycardia associated with WPW syndrome is:** (PGI 2012)

  • A. Orthodromic AV reentry
  • B. Antidromic AV reentry
  • C. Rapidly conducting AF
  • D. Ventricular tachycardia
Q150

All are ECG findings in Wolf Parkinson’s white syndrome, except:** (AIIMS Feb 97)

  • A. Narrow QRS complexes
  • B. Normal QT interval
  • C. Slurred and tall QRS
  • D. Short PR interval
Q151

All of the following statements about WPW syndrome are true, Except:** (NBE)

  • A. More common in females
  • B. Lt ventricular aberrant is commonly seen
  • D. Heart is structurally normal
Q152

The drug of choice in patients with Wolff-Parkinson-White syndrome with atrial fibrillation is-** (AIIMS Nov 03)

  • B. Procainamide
Q153

A Patient with short PR interval and Delta waves on ECG presents to the emergency department with Atrial Fibrillation and rapid ventricular rate. He is hemodynamically stable. All of the following agents may be used in the management, except:** (AIIMS 2013)

  • A. Verapamil
  • C. Ibutilide
  • D. Amiodarone
Q154

Which of the following is the treatment of choice for Atrial Fibrillation in patients with WPW Syndrome who are hemodynamically unstable:** (AIIMS 2013)

  • A. Procainamide
  • B. Digoxin
  • C. DC Cardioversion
  • D. Radiofrequency Ablation of the bypass tract
Q155

Radiofrequency Ablation of the bypass tract is done for:** (AIIMS June 98)

  • A. Ventricular tachycardia
  • C. WPW
  • D. Atrial tachycardia
Q156

Sinus Bradycardia is defined as heart rate of?** (NBE)

  • A. Less than 40/min
  • B. Less than 50/min
  • C. Less than 60/min
  • D. Less than 70/min
Q157

All of the following are features of Mobitz Type I block, except:** (AI-1992)

  • A. Constant PR interval
  • B. Normal QRS morphology
  • C. Regular Atrial Rhythm
  • D. Atrial rate > ventricular rate
Q158

Constant PR interval is seen in:** (PGI-1997)

  • A. First degree block
  • B. Second degree – Mobitz type I block
  • C. Second degree – Mobitz type II block
  • D. Third degree block
Q159

All of the following may be seen in Mobitz Type II second degree Heart block, except:** (NBE)

  • A. Change in QRS complex morphology
  • B. Atrial rate more than ventricular rate
  • C. Constant PR interval
  • D. No constant Relationship between P wave and QRS complex
Q160

True statement regarding Atrioventricular block with atrial tachycardia is:** (AIIMS Nov 98)

  • A. Seen in WPW syndrome
  • B. Is a complication of pacemaker
  • C. Can occur in normal person occasionally.
  • D. Seen in Digitalis toxicity with K+ depletion.
Q161

All of the following are associated with Atrioventricular Block, except:** (NBE)

  • B. Hypermagnesemia
  • C. Hypothyroidism
  • D. Cushing's Syndrome
Q162

Most common cause of Acquired Complete Heart Block in children is** (NBE)

  • A. Cardiac Surgery
  • B. Acute Rheumatic Fever
  • C. Drug Overdose
Q163

In the treatment of severe bradycardia, all of the following can be best modality of treatment except:** (AIIMS Nov 05)

  • A. Atropine
  • B. Pacing
  • C. Isoproterenol
  • D. Diltiazem
Q164

All of the following are true about Right Ventricular Infarcts, Except:** (NBE)

  • A. Nocturia
  • B. Hepatomegaly
  • C. Ascites
  • D. Orthopnea
Q165

All the following are features of right sided heart failure, Except:** (AI 2009)

  • A. Increased PCWP
  • B. Pulsatile liver
  • C. Increased JVP
  • D. Positive hepatojugular reflex
Q166

Increased hydrostatic pressure in left sided congestive heart failure causes -** (NBE)

  • A. Raised JVP
  • B. Congestive Hepatomegaly
  • C. Pedal Edema
Q167

All of the following are true about Right Ventricular Infarcts, Except** (NBE)

  • C. Ascitis
  • D. Normal JVP
Q168

Which of the following is not a major Framingham criteria in CHF:** (AIIMS Nov 2008)

  • A. Cardiomegaly
  • B. Paroxysmal nocturnal dyspnea
  • C. S3 gallop
  • D. Hepatomegaly
Q169

Left ventricular Hypertropy is caused by all, Except:** (AIIMS Nov 09)

  • A. M.S (Mitral stenosis)
  • B. A. S (Aortic Stenosis)
  • C. M.R (Mitral Regurgitation)
  • D. A. R (Aortic Regurgitation)
Q170

CCF is associated with increase in all the following except:** (AI 2007)

  • A. Right atrial mean pressure
  • B. Serum Sodium
  • C. Urea
  • D. Nor epinephrine
Q171

Positive hepatojugular reflux is found in conditions except:** (AI 2009)

  • A. Tricuspid regurgitation
  • B. Right heart failure
  • C. Decreased after load
Q172

Positive Hepatojugular Reflux is found in all of the following conditions, except:** (AIIMS May 2011)

  • A. Tricuspid Regurgitation
  • B. Precapillary Pulmonary Hypertension
  • C. Right Ventricular Infarction
  • D. Decreased After-load
Q173

Enlarged Pulsatile liver with ascitis is typically seen in:** (AIIMS May 2011)

  • A. Tricuspid Regurgitation (TR)
  • B. Mitral Regurgitation (MR)
  • C. Mitral stenosis
  • D. Pulmonary stenosis (PS)
Q174

Left atrial filling pressure closely approximates-** (AIIMS May 93)

  • A. Pulmonary capillary wedge pressure
  • B. Central venous pressure
  • C. Intrapleural pressure
  • D. Intracranial pressure
Q175

A Patient comes with sudden respiratory distress. On examination, bilateral basal crepts are present over chest suggestive of pulmonary edema. Alveolar wedge pressure is normal. The likely cause is-** (AIIMS June 2000)

  • A. Narcotic overdose
  • B. Congestive heart failure
  • C. Myocardial infarction
  • D. Cardiogenic shock
Q176

Normal PCWP with pulmonary oedema is seen in-** (AIIMS June 98)

  • A. Left atrial myxoma
  • B. High altitude
  • C. Pulmonary vein obstruction
  • D. Pulmonary artery obstruction.
Q177

Pulmonary edema associated with normal PCWP is observed. Which of these is not a cause:** (AI 01)

  • A. High altitude
  • B. Cocaine overdose
  • C. Post cardiopulmonary bypass
  • D. Bilateral renal artery stenosis
Q178

Assays of which of the following biomarkers is most commonly used in the diagnosis of Heart failure

  • A. Brain Natriuretic Peptide (BNP)
  • B. Atrial Natriuretic Peptide (ANP)
  • C. Endothelin- 1 (ET-1)
  • D. Adrenomedullin
Q179

Which of the following biomarkers has been shown to correlate best with pulmonary vascular resistance in patients with heart failure:** (NBE)

  • D. Endothelin- 2 (ET-2)
Q180

Which of the following biomarkers is released from ventricular myocytes:** (NBE)

  • C. Endothelin1
  • D. Endothelin2
Q181

All of the following are used in the initial management of acute life threatening cardiogenic pulmonary edema, except:** (AI 2012)

  • A. Digoxin
  • B. Morphine
  • C. Furosemide
  • D. Positive Pressure Ventilation
Q182

All of the following medications may be used in congestive cardiac failure, Except** (AIIMS Nov 09)

  • A. Spironolactone
  • B. Nitrates
  • C. Nesiritide
  • D. Trimetazidine
Q183

All of the following are true about starting beta-blocker therapy in cases of CHF, Except** (NBE)

  • A. They should be initiated at the effective doses
  • B. They should be gradually increased over weeks
  • C. Special precautions should be taken in NYHA class III & IV
  • D. Carvedilol and Metoprolol are the preferred drugs
Q184

Treatment of choice for patients with Congestive Heart Failure and an ejection fraction of < 40 percent is:** (NBE)

  • A. ACE + Beta Blocker
  • B. ACE-I + Diuretic
  • C. ACE-I+ ARB
  • D. ACE-I + CCB
Q185

All of the following medications have shown to lower mortality in patients with CHF, Except:** (NBE)

  • A. ACE – Inhibitors
  • C. Calcium Channel blockers
  • D. Spironolactone
Q186

In a patient with chronic congestive cardiac failure, all of the following drugs prolong survival except :** (AIIMS May 04)

  • A. Metoprolol
  • B. Carvedilol
  • C. Enalapril
  • D. Digoxin
Q187

All of the following statements about digitalis are true except :** (AI 1997)

  • A. Excretion is mainly renal
  • B. Oral absorption is good
  • C. Actively metabolized in liver
  • D. Lipid soluble
Q188

Digoxin toxicity is enhanced by all, except:** (AIIMS Dec 97)

  • A. Quinidine
  • D. Hepatic dysfunction
Q189

All of the following may precipitate digitalis toxicity except :** (AI 1997)

  • A. Hypocalcemia
  • C. Hypomagnecemia
Q190

Treatment of digoxin over dose includes administration of all of the following except :** (AI 1997)

  • A. Potassium
  • C. Phenyton
  • D. Hemodialysis
Q191

Following are used in treatment of digitalis toxicity except-** (AI 95)

  • C. Fabparticles
Q192

All the following are radiological features of Chronic Cor pulmonale except :** (AI 1996)

  • A. Kerley B lines
  • B. Prominent lower lobe vessels
  • C. Pleural effusion
  • D. Cardiomegaly
Q193

Which of the following is the least common cause of True Valvular Mitral Stenosis** (NBE)

  • A. Cor triatrium
  • B. Rheumatic fever
  • C. Systemic lupus erythematosus
  • D. Congenital MS
Q194

Area of mitral orifice in adults is:** (AIIMS Dec 94)

  • A. 6-8 cm²
  • B. .5-2 cm²
  • C. 4-6 cm²
  • D. 1-4 cm²
Q195

Mitral stenosis is associated with :** (AI 1997)

  • A. Right ventricular hypertrophy
  • B. Left ventricular hypertrophy
  • C. Left axis deviation
  • D. QRS complex
Q196

Mid diastolic Murmur with presystolic accentuation is typically seen in:** (NBE)

Q197

All of the following may be seen as severity of mitral stenosis increases Except** (NBE)

  • A. Pulsatile liver
  • B. Atrial fibrillation
  • C. Opening snap delayed from S2
  • D. Length of murmur is prolonged
Q198

Severity of mitral stenosis is assessed by:** (AI 1998)

  • A. Loud opening snap
  • B. Length of murmur
  • C. Loud S1
  • D. Splitting S2
Q199

Which clinical sign is suggestive of elevated pulmonary wedge presure in mitral stenosis?** (NBE)

  • A. Loud OS
  • B. Loud S1
  • C. Mid-diastolic murmur
  • D. Short A2 –OS interval
Q200

Double Density sign in Mitral Stenosis is a sign of enlargement of:** (NBE)

  • A. Right Atrium
  • B. Left Atrium
  • C. Left Auricle
  • D. Right Auricle
Q201

Absolute Contraindication for Percutaneous Balloon Mitral Valvotomy include the following except

  • A. Presence of pulmonary hypertension
  • B. Left atrial mobile thrombus
  • C. Severe mitral regurgitation
  • D. Bilateral Commissural calcification
Q202

Mitral valve replacement is recommended in patients with** (NBE)

  • A. Moderate MS in NYHA class II
  • B. Moderate MS in NYHA class III
  • C. Severe MS in NYHA class II
  • D. Severe MS in NYHA class III
Q203

Severity of Mitral Regurgitation may be judged by** (NBE)

  • A. Intensity of murmur
  • B. Duration of murmur
  • C. Left ventricular S3
  • D. Loud S1
Q204

The severity of mitral regurgitation is decided by all of the following clinical findings except:

  • A. Presence of mid-diastolic murmur across mitral valve.
  • B. Wide split second heart sound.
  • C. Presence of left ventricular S3 gallop.
  • D. Intensity of systolic murmur across mitral valve.
Q205

A 59 year old man with severe myxomatous mitral regurgitation is asymptomatic, with a left ventricular ejection fraction of 45% and an endsystolic diameter index of 2.9 cm/m2. The most appropriate treatment is** (AI 2005)

  • A. Mitral valve repair or replacement
  • B. No treatment
  • C. ACE inhibitor therapy
  • D. Digoxin and diuretic therapy
Q206

A young asymptomatic female is observed to have a Midsystolic Click on routine examination. Valves are likely to show** (AI 2010)

  • A. Myxomatous degeneration
  • B. Aschoff bodies
  • C. Calcific degeneration
  • D. Ruptured chordae tendinae
Q207

Barlow's syndrome refers to** (NBE)

  • A. Bicuspid Aortic Stenosis
  • B. Atrial Myxoma
Q208

All of the following statements about mitral valve prolapse are true except:** (AIIMS Nov 93)

  • A. It is more common in females
  • B. Most patients are symptomatic
  • C. It has a benign clinical course
  • D. Transient cerebral ischemic is a know complication
Q209

True regarding Mitral valve prolapse syndrome is all, EXCEPT-** (AIIMS Nov 93)

  • A. MORE common in female
  • B. Mostly symptomatic
  • C. High incidence of arrhythmia
  • D. Transient cerebral ischemia can occur
Q210

All of the following are true for mitral valve prolapse, except-** (AI 06)

  • A. Transmission may be as an autosomal dominant trait
  • B. Majority of the case present with features of mitral regurgitation
  • C. The valve leaflets characteristically show myxomatous
  • D. The diseases is one of the common cardiovascular manifestations of Marfan's Syndrome
Q211

All of the following are true about Mid-late systolic click of MVP, EXCEPT** (NBE)

  • A. Non-ejection systolic click
  • B. May be multiple
  • C. Produced by tensioning of slack chords tendineae
  • D. Timing of click does not alter with changes in volume of LV
Q212

Which of the following CONGENITAL Heart DISEASES is associated with MVP** (NBE)

  • A. Ostium primum ASD
  • B. Ostium secundum ASD
  • C. VSD
Q213

A 26 yr old asymptomatic woman is found to have arrhythmias and a systolic murmur associated with midsystolic clicks; which investigation would you use** (AI 2001)

  • A. Electrophysiological testing
  • B. Tc scan
  • C. Echocardiography
  • D. Angiography
Q214

A female patient Chandani develops chest pain which is not associated with exercise and chest auscultation shows multiple non ejection clicks. The investigation which is used to diagnose the disease is :** (AIIMS June 99)

  • A. Echocardiography
  • B. Pyrophosphate scan
  • C. Thallium 201 scan
  • D. ECG
Q215

Angina pectoris and Syncope are most likely to be associated with:** (AI 1994)

  • D. Tricuspid stenosis
Q216

A 63 year old man present with a triad of angina, syncope and congestive heart failure. Which of the following valvular heart lesion can be suspected :** (AIIMS Nov 05)

  • B. Tricuspid regurgitation
  • D. Aortic regurgitation
Q217

Which of the following valvular heart diseases is most commonly associated with sudden death:

  • A. Aortic Stenosis
Q218

In severe aortic stenosis true finding is :** (AIIMS Nov 98)

  • A. Late systolic ejection click.
  • B. Heaving with outward apex
  • C. ST segment changes in ECG
  • D. Loud S2
Q219

Which of the following physical signs is seen in a patient with severe aortic stenosis:** (AIIMS 2002)

  • B. Diastolic rumble
  • C. Holosystolic murmur
  • D. Delayed peak of systolic murmur
Q220

All are features of aortic stenosis except** (NBE)

  • A. Commonly associated with a bicuspid aortic valve
  • B. Presence of ejection systolic murmur
  • C. Presence of Pulsus Tardus
  • D. LV outflow pressure gradient of 2-4 mm Hg
Q221

Calcification of the aortic valve is seen in :** (AI 95)

  • C. Marfan's syndrome
  • D. Hurler's syndrome
Q222

Sub-valvular Aortic Stenosis is known to be associated with all of the following, except:** (NBE)

  • C. Tricuspid Valve Atresia
  • D. Ventricular Septal Defect
Q223

The most common complication of Sub-valvular Aortic Stenosis is:** (NBE)

  • D. Pulmonary Regurgitation
Q224

AS is called severe when the aortic valve area is** (NBE)

  • A. <1.5 cm²
  • B. <1.0 cm²
  • C. <0.5 cm²
  • D. <0.2 cm²
Q225

Acute Aortic regurgitation is seen in all the following except:** (AI 1994)

  • A. Marfan's syndrome
  • B. Acute myocardial infarction
  • C. Bacterial endocarditis
  • D. Ankylosing spondylitis
Q226

Aortic regurgitation does NOT occur in :** (AIIMS Sept 96)

  • B. Marfan's syndrome
  • C. Rheumatic heart disease
Q228

A 35 year old athlete has height 184 cm., arm span 194 cm., pulse rate 64/min., BP 148/64 mm Hg. Chest auscultation reveals long diastolic murmur over right 2nd intercostal space on routine examination. The probable diagnosis is :** (AIIMS June 99)

  • A. Aortic regurgitation
  • B. Atrial septal defect
  • C. Ebstein anomaly
Q229

A young basketball player with ht 188cm & arm span 197cm has a diastolic murmur best heard in second right intercostal space, likely cause of murmur is** (AI 2001)

  • A. AS
  • B. Coarctation of aorta
Q230

Duroziez's sign is seen in** (NBE)

  • B. Tricuspid Regurgitation
  • D. Pericardial effusion
Q231

LVH is commonly seen with :** (AI 1999)

  • A. Pure mitral stenosis
  • B. ASD with fossa-ovalis
  • C. Aortic incompletance
  • D. Carcinoid syndrome
Q232

Which of the following is contraindicated in the treatment of acute severe aortic regurgitation** (NBE)

  • A. Intravenous Diuretics
  • B. Sodium Nitroprusside
  • C. Intraaortic Balloon Counterpulsation
  • D. Surgery
Q233

Peripheral pulmonary stenosis may occur at the following sites :** (AI 97)

  • A. Supravalvular
  • B. Valvular
  • C. Subvalvular
  • D. All of the above
Q234

Peripheral pulmonic stenosis is associated with (select two options):** (PGI June 02)

  • A. Subaortic stenosis
  • B. Takayasu's arteritis
  • C. William syndrome
  • D. Coarctation of syndrome
  • E. Rubella
Q235

In pulmonary stenosis the interval between first heart sound (S1) and Systolic ejection click:** (NBE)

  • A. Increases as the severity of PS increases
  • B. Decreases as the severity of PS increases
  • C. Remains unchanged
  • D. Any of the above
Q236

Ejection click of pulmonary stenosis is better heard in** (NBE)

  • A. Inspiration
  • B. Expiration
  • C. Patient bending forward
  • D. Patient lying in left lateral position
Q237

The most common cause of tricuspid regurgitation is secondary to –** (AI-03)

  • A. Rheumatoid heart disease
  • B. Dilatation of right ventricle
  • C. Coronary artery disease
  • D. Endocarditis due to intravenous drug abuse
Q238

Hepatomegaly with liver pulsation indicates:** (AI 2009)

  • A. TR
  • C. Pulmonary hypertension
Q239

Vitum's Sign is seen in** (NBE)

Q240

Carvallo's sign is seen in** (NBE)

Q241

Carvallo's Sign representing a Diastolic Murmur that increases on inspiration is seen in** (NBE)

  • A. Tricuspid Stenosis
Q242

All of the following are risk factors for atherosclerosis except :** (AI 06)

  • A. Increased waist - hip ratio
  • B. Hyperhomocysteinemia
  • C. Decreased fibrinogen levels
  • D. Decreased HDL levels
Q243

Best predictor for future risk of cardiovascular events, amongst the following is:** (AI 2009)

  • A. hs CRP
  • B. Lipoprotein 'a'
  • C. Homocysteine
  • D. Interleukin 6
Q244

Which of the following is the best marker to predict future cardiac events** (DNB June 2012)

  • B. Homocystine
  • C. Interleuken-6
  • D. (Option not fully visible/clear in crop, likely missing)
Q245

The amino acid which is associated with atherosclerosis is :** (AIIMS May 2006)

  • A. Lysine
  • B. Homocysteine
  • C. Cysteine
  • D. Alanine
Q246

The risk of coronary artery disease is associated with an elevated level of** (NBE)

  • A. Cysteine.
  • B. Cystine.
  • C. Homocysteine.
  • D. Selenocysteine.
Q247

The protein which has structural homology with plasminogen and is responsible for myocardial infarction and stroke** (NBE)

  • A. HDL
  • B. LP(a)
  • C. LDL
  • D. Homocysteine
Q248

Risk factors for coronary artery disease (CAD) (select two options)–** (PGI June 01)

  • A. High HDL
  • B. Low LDL
  • C. Increased homocysteine levels
  • D. Decreased fibrinogen levels
  • E. Increased lipoproteins
Q249

Predisposing factors for coronary artery disease include, all Except:** (PGI Dec 02)

  • A. Homocysteinemia
  • B. ↑ Lipoprotein B (Lipoprotein A)
  • C. ↑ Fibrinogen
  • D. ↑ plasminogen activator inhibitors 1
Q250

Raised serum level of lipoprotein-(a) is a predictor of** (AI 03)

  • A. Cirrhosis of liver
  • B. Rheumatic arthritis
  • C. Atherosclerosis
  • D. Cervical cancer
Q251

Which of the following increases the susceptibility to coronary artery disease :** (AI 03)

  • A. Type V hyperlipoproteinaemia
  • B. Von willebrands disease
  • C. Nephrotic syndrome
  • D. Systemic lupus erythematosus
Q252

In an old patient, the best indicator of probability of developing cardiovascular disease can be calculated by–** (AIIMS May 02)

  • A. LDL/HDL ratio.
  • B. Triglycerides
  • C. Total cholesterol
  • D. Serum LDL
Q253

Most important predictor of coronary artery disease is:** (AIIMS May 09)

  • A. VLDL
  • B. LDL
  • C. Chylomicron
  • D. LDL/HDL
Q254

An association of which of the following bacteria has been suggested with atherosclerosis:** (NBE)

  • A. Staphylococcus Aureus
  • B. Streptococcus Pneumoniae
  • C. Chlamydia Pneumoniae
  • D. Aspergillus Fumigatus
Q255

The most important modifiable risk factor for atherosclerosis is:** (NBE)

  • A. Hyperlipidemia
  • B. Diabetes mellitus
  • C. Cigarette Smoking
Q256

According to the ATP III Classification Desired levels of total cholesterol is:** (NBE)

  • A. < 250
  • B. < 200
  • C. < 100
  • D. < 40
Q257

All of the following dietary goals are recommended for patients with high risk of coronary heart disease, Except:** (AI 2011)

  • A. LDL cholesterol < 100 mg/dl
  • B. Saturated fat < 7 % of total calories
  • C. Salt restriction < 6 gm/day
  • D. Avoid Alcohol
Q258

Which of the following dietary interventions has shown to reduces mortality in patients with coronary heart disease.–** (AIIMS May 07)

  • A. High Fibre diet
  • B. Steral Esters
  • C. Potassium supplements
  • D. Omega 3 polyunsaturated fatty acids
Q259

A patient with hypertriglyceridemia is treated with Omega 3 polyunsaturated fatty acids. Treatment with omega-3- polyunsaturated fatty acids, will have the following effect on lipid profile:** (AIIMS Nov 06)

  • A. Increased LDL and Decreased total cholesterol
  • B. Decreased LDL and Decreased total cholesterol
  • C. Increased LDL and Increased total cholesterol
  • D. Decreased LDL and Increased total cholesterol
Q260

Which of the following statements about Atherosclerosis is true:** (AIIMS May 2011)

  • A. Intake of Unsaturated Fatty Acids is associated with decreased risk
  • B. Extent of lesions in veins is similar as that in arteries
  • C. Thoracic Aorta is more commonly involved than abdominal aorta
  • D. Hypercholesterolemia alone does not increase the risk of atherosclerosis per se
Q261

Which of the following is not true about metabolic syndrome?** (NBE)

  • A. It is also called Syndrome X
  • B. Acanthosis Nigricans may be seen
  • C. Insulin resistance (IR)is a characteristic feature
  • D. Type-A- Insulin Resistance has autoantibodies against thereceptor
Q262

According to the NCEP-ATP III Definition of Metabolic Syndrome, all of the following criteria for 'Men' are correct, EXCEPT** (NBE)

  • A. Waist circumference > 102 cm
  • B. Triglyceride level ≥150 mg/dL
  • C. HDL cholesterol <40 mg/dL
  • D. Blood pressure ≥140 mmHg systolic
Q263

WHO Rose Questionnaire is used for** (NBE)

  • A. Alcohol Addiction
  • B. Angina Assessment
  • C. DVT Assessment
  • D. Arrhythmia Assessment
Q264

In stable angina:** (AIIMS Nov 03)

  • A. CK-MB is elevated
  • B. Troponin I is elevated
  • C. Myoglobin is elevated
  • D. The levels of cardiac markers remain unchanged.
Q265

Episodes of Stable Angina Pectoris typically last for** (NBE)

  • A. Less than 1 min
  • B. 2-5 minutes
  • C. 5-10 minutes
  • D. > 10 minutes
Q266

Wellen's syndrome suggests:** (NBE)

  • A. Stable angina
  • B. Unstable angina
  • C. Prinzmetal angina
  • D. Ludwig angina
Q267

Which of the following is not true about Prinzmetal's Variant Angina–** (NBE)

  • A. Severe ischemic pain at rest with ST segmentelevation
  • B. Focal spasms are common in right coronaryartery
  • C. Nitrates and calcium channel blockers are themainstay of management
  • D. Aspirin should be administered as soon as the diagnosis is made
Q268

All of the following statements about Universal Definition of Myocardial Infarction are true, Except: (AIIMS Nov 2011)

  • A. Sudden, unexpected Cardiac death with symptoms of Ischemia.
  • B. Elevation of cardiac biomarkers with new regional wall motion abnormality
  • C. Three times increase in Troponin levels after Percutaneous Coronary Intervention (PCI)
  • D. Three times increase in Troponin levels after Coronary Artery Bypass Grafting (CABG)
Q269

All of the following arteries are common sites of occlusion by a except:** (AIIMS May 2005)

  • A. Left anterior descending
  • B. Right coronary artery
  • C. Circumflex coronary artery
  • D. Marginal artery
Q270

Most common site of myocardial infraction is:** (AI 98)

  • A. Anterior wall of left ventricle
  • B. Posterior wall of left ventricle
  • C. Posterior wall of right ventricle
  • D. Inferior wall of left ventricle
Q271

A 60 year old man presents with chest pain with last 6 hours and is diagnosed as acute myocardial infarction. Angiography showed involvement of anterior descending branch of left coronary artery. The most probable site of infarct is :** (AIIMS May 01)

  • A. Anterolateral wall
  • B. Posterior wall
  • C. Interventricular septum
  • D. Right ventricle
Q272

The most common site for myocardial infarction is :** (AI 96)

  • A. Left atrium
  • B. Left ventricle
  • C. Right atrium
Q273

The most common site of Myocardial infarction is:** (AI 94)

  • A. Anterior wall
  • B. Interventricular septum
  • C. Posterior wall
  • D. Inferior wall
Q274

ECG is poor in detecting ischemia in areas supplied by which of the following vessels:** (AI 2011)

  • A. Left Anterior Descending (LAD)
  • B. Left Circumflex (LCx)
  • C. Left Coronary Artery (LCA)
  • D. Right Coronary Artery (RCA)
Q275

Which of the following ECG leads is most sensitive in detecting intraoperative myocardial ischemia (PGI)

  • A. Lead I
  • C. Lead V1
  • D. Lead V2
  • E. Lead V5
Q276

A 70 year old male patient presented to the emergency department with pain in epigastrium and difficulty in breathing for 6 hours. On examination, his heart rate was chest examination was normal. The patient has been taking omeprazole for gastroesophageal reflux disease for last 6 months. What should be the initial investigation :** (AIIMS Nov. 05)

  • A. An ECG
  • B. An upper GI endoscopy
  • C. Urgent ultrasound of the abdomen
  • D. An x-ray chest
Q277

Which of the following ECG findings is associated with acute myocardial Infarction** (NBE)

  • A. Elevation of S wave
  • C. Tall T waves with increased amplitude
Q278

What is diagnostic of fresh myocardial infarction in ECG:** (AIIMS May 95)

Q279

ST elevation and hyperacute T waves in precordial leads V1 to V6 and in lead aVL indicates:** (PGI Dec

  • A. Anterolateral wall MI
  • B. Posterior wall MI
  • C. Inferior MI
  • D. Lateral wall MI
Q280

A patient presents 12 hours following a Myocardial infarction. Test of choice :** (AIIMS June 98)

  • A. Lactate dehydrogenase
  • B. Cardiac troponins
  • C. Creatinine phosphokinase
  • D. Myoglobin
Q281

Troponin-T is preferable to CPK-MB in the diagnosis of acute myocardial infarction (MI) in all of the following situations except:** (AI 2003)

  • A. Bedside diagnosis of MI
  • B. Postoperatively (after CABG)
  • C. Reinfarction after 4 days
  • D. Small infarcts
Q282

Cardiac Biomarker of Choice for diagnosis of Myocardial Infarction 10 days after the initial event:

  • A. Troponins
  • B. CK-MB
  • C. Myoglobin
  • D. LDH
Q283

Cardiac Biomarker of Choice for diagnosis of Re-infarction 8 days after the initial ischemic event is:

Q284

In MI, which enzyme is raised in 4 to 6 hrs. & decreases in 3 to 4 days :** (AIIMS Nov. 2000)

  • A. SGOT
  • B. LDH
  • C. CPK
  • D. SGPT
Q285

Which of the following is the preferred marker for detecting Acute STEMI in Athletes:** (AI 2012)

  • A. CK-MB
  • B. Troponin T/I
  • C. C-Reactive Protein
Q286

Flipped LDH indicating Myocardial Infarction is represented by:** (NBE)

  • A. LDH-1 > LDH-2
  • B. LDH-2 > LDH-1
  • C. LDH-4 > LDH-5
  • D. LDH-5 > LDH-4
Q287

Reperfusion is believed to restore contractile function of:** (AIIMS May 2011)

  • A. Stunned Myocardium
  • B. Hibernating Myocardium
  • C. Ischemic non-viable myocardium
  • D. Non ischemic viable myocardium
Q288

Agent of first choice in an acute attack of Prinzmetal's angina is:** (AI 1995)

  • A. Diltiazem
  • C. Propranolol
  • D. Verapamil
Q289

The best possible intervention for acute myocardial infarction is:** (AIIMS May 2005)

  • A. Streptokinase
  • B. Streptokinase and aspirin
  • C. Early primary coronary intervention
  • D. Streptokinase and heparin
Q290

Dose of streptokinase to be used in MI is?** (NBE)

  • A. 0.15 Million units
  • B. 1.5 Million units
  • C. 15 Million units
  • D. 150 Million units
Q291

Dose of Reteplase for management of Acute MI is?** (NBE)

  • A. 5 IU
  • B. 10 IU
  • C. 15 IU
  • D. 50 IU
Q292

Half-life of alteplase?** (NBE)

  • A. 3 min
  • B. 6 min
  • C. 9 min
  • D. 12 min
Q293

40 year old male patient presents to the Emergency department with central chest pain for 2 hours. The ECG shows ST segment depression and cardiac troponins are elevated. Patient has a positive history of previous PCI 3 months back. He is administered Aspirin, Clopidogrel, Nitrates and LMWH, in the Emergency Department, Nitrates and LMWH, in the coronary care unit. The best recommended course of further action should include** (AIIMS)

  • A. Immediate Revascularization with Thrombolytics
  • B. Early Revascularization with PCI
  • C. Continue conservative management and monitoring of cardiac enzymes and ECG
  • D. Continue conservation management and plan for delayed Revascularization procedure after patient is
Q294

A 50 year old man with a history of smoking, hypertension, and chronic exertional angina develops several daily episodes of chest pain at rest compatible with cardiac ischemia. The patient is hospitalized. All the following would be part of an appropriate management plan except :** (AIIMS Nov 99)

  • A. Intravenous heparin
  • B. Aspirin
  • C. Intravenous nitroglycerin
  • D. Lidocaine by bolus infusion
Q295

The treatment of choice for a patient of Acute ST Elevation Myocardial infarction with cardiogenic shock is:** (NBE)

  • A. Intra-aortic balloon pump (IABP) and PCI
  • B. PCI with Angioplasty
  • C. Thrombolysis
  • D. Intraaortic balloon pump (IABP)
Q296

A patient present with acute anterior wall infarction and hypotension. Which will be the immediate treatment modality for this atient :** (AI 2007)

  • A. Intra aortic balloon counter pulsation
  • B. Anticoagulation
  • C. Thrombolytic therapy
  • D. Primary angioplasty
Q297

A patient presents with intense chestpain of 2 hrs duration. ECG shows ST depression in leads I and V1 to V4. There is associated T inversion and CPK-MB is elevated. All of the following should be included in the management of this patient, Except:** (PGI June 2000)

  • A. Nitroglycerine drip
  • C. Coronary angiography
  • D. Streptokinase
  • E. I.V. metoprolol
Q298

A 40 years old male, chronic smoker comes with acute epigastric discomfort, for past one hour. ECG showing ST segment elevation in inferior leads. What is the immediate intervention?** (AIIMS Nov 07)

  • A. Aspirin
  • B. Thrombolytic therapy
  • C. IV pantoprazole
  • D. Beta blockers
Q299

All of the following drugs are used in the management of acute myocardial infarction, except:** (AIIMS May 04)

  • A. Tissue Plasminogen activator
  • B. Intravenous beta blockers
  • C. Acetylsalicylic acid
  • D. Calcium channel blockers
Q300

In MI following are used Except:** (PGI Dec 97)

  • A. Fibrinolytics
  • B. Plasminogen activator inhibitor
  • C. Anti thrombin
  • D. Platelet inhibitor
Q301

Allof the following are used in the management of acute myocardial infarction Except:** (PGI Dec 01)

  • B. Heparin
  • C. Alteplase
  • D. Warfarin
Q302

Streptokinase and Urokinase are contraindicated in:** (AI 2010)

  • A. Intracranial malignancy
  • B. Pulmonary Embolism
  • C. AV fistula
  • D. Thrombophlebitis
Q303

Which of the following is an oral direct thrombin inhibitor** (NBE)

  • A. Dabigatran
  • B. Rivoraxaban
  • C. Lepirudin
Q304

All of the following are Oral Factor Xa Inhibitors, Except** (NBE)

  • A. Rivoraxaban
  • B. Apixaban
  • C. Edoxaban
  • D. Argatroban
Q305

Which of the following is an approved antidote for dabigatran?** (NBE)

  • A. Abciximab
  • B. Idarucizumab
  • C. Infliximab
  • D. None of the above
Q306

All of the following are complications of streptokinase, Except:** (PGI June 01)

  • A. Joint pain
  • B. Intracranial bleed
  • C. Fever
  • D. Anaphylaxis
  • E. Hypotension
Q307

A patient with acute inferior wall myocardial infarction has developed shock. Which of the following is the most likely cause of shock** (AIIMS May 04)

  • A. Cardiac rupture
  • B. Interventricular septal perforation
  • C. Papillary muscle rupture
  • D. Right ventricular infarction
Q308

A previously healthy 58 years old man is admitted to the hospital because of an acute inferior myocardial infarction. Within several hours, he becomes oliguric and hypotensive (blood pressure is 90/60 mmHg). Insertion of a pulmonary artery (Swan-Ganz) catheter reveals the following pressures: pulmonary capillary wedge-4 mm Hg; pulmonary artery- 22/4 mmHg; and mean right atrial- 11 mmHg. This man would best be treated with :** (NBE)

  • A. Fluids
  • C. Dopamine
  • D. Intraaortic balloon counter pulsation
Q309

In a pt of acute inferior wall MI; best modality of rx is:** (AI 2001)

  • A. IV fluids
  • C. Diuretics
  • D. Vasodilators
Q310

A patient had an inferior wall myocardial infarction and was in shock. The reason for the patient being in shock is:-** (AIIMS May 02)

  • A. Mitral regurgitation
  • B. Infarction causing septal defect
  • C. Right ventricular infarction
  • D. Decreased ejection fraction from left ventricle
Q311

In a patient with myocardial infarction the valvular lesion commonly seen in:-** (AIIMS May 02)

  • C. Aortic regurgitation
  • D. Septal defect
Q312

Ramkumar a 70 year old hypertensive male was admitted in the intensive care unit with transmural anterolateral myocardial infarction. His condition was stable till fifth day of admission, when he developed a pericardial friction rub and pleuritic chest pain which persisted despite narcotic and steroid therapy. On the seventh morning, he suddenly developed marked distension of the jugular veins, accompanied with electromechanical dissociation. Most likely, the patient had developed :** (AIIMS Nov 02)

  • A. Severe acute mitral regurgitation.
  • B. Ventricular septal rupture.
  • C. Right ventricular infarction.
  • D. External cardiac rupture.
Q313

Which of the following is a most common mechanism of ventricular aneurysm following myocardial infarction?** (NBE)

  • A. Anterior wall transmural
  • B. Inferior wall transmural
  • C. Posterior wall transmural
  • D. Sub-endocardial
Q314

A new systolic murmur after Acute myocardial infarction may be due to all of the following Except:

Q315

True about Dressler's syndrome is all, except :** (AIIMS June 98)

  • A. Occurs with in hours after myocardial infarction
  • B. Recurrence may be seen
  • C. Chest pain is common
  • D. Responds well to salicylates
Q316

Which is incorrect about Dressler syndrome?** (NBE)

  • A. Post MI pericarditis
  • B. Post MI pleuritis
  • C. Autoimmune
  • D. Treatment with steroids is necessary
Q317

Which of the following is the preferred pharmacological agent for Stress Echocardiography to detect coronary artery disease** (NBE)

  • A. Dipyridamole
  • B. Adenosine
  • C. Dobutamine
Q318

Which of the following tests is used to detect reversible myocardial ischemia** (AIIMS May 09)

  • A. Angiography
  • B. Thallium scan
  • C. MUGA
  • D. Resting Echocardiography
Q319

Which test is performed to detect reversible myocardial ischemia?** (AIIMS May 03)

  • A. Coronary angiography.
  • B. MUGA scan.
  • C. Thallium scan.
  • D. Resting echocardiography.

Which of the following drugs has been linked with increased cardiac mortality:** (AI 2012)

  • A. Rofecoxib
  • B. Metoprolol
  • C. Losartan
  • D. Nicorandil
Q321

Following an attack of myocardial infarction the mortality and morbidity of the patient is indicated by** (AIIMS June 2000)

  • A. Ventricular extra systole
  • B. Left ventricular ejection fraction
  • C. Duration of syncope
  • D. Percentage of narrowness of coronary artery
Q322

Cardiomyopathy may be seen in all of the following except :** (AIIMS May 2006)

  • A. Duchene muscular dystrophy
  • B. Friedrich's ataxia
  • C. Type II glycogen storage disease
  • D. Alkaptonuria

  • B. Restrictive cardiomyopathy
  • D. Infiltrative cardiomyopathy

  • A. Dilated Cardiomyopathy
  • B. Hypertrophic Cardiomyopathy
  • C. Toxic Cardiomyopathy
  • D. Restrictive cardiomyopathy
Q325

Which of the following is a most common type of alcohol induced cardiomyopathy?** (NBE)

  • A. Hypertrophic cardiomyopathy
  • B. Dilated cardiomyopathy
Q326

The most common toxin causing Dilated Cardiomyopathy is:** (NBE)

  • B. Chemotherapeutic agents
  • C. Heavy metal
  • D. Occupational exposure

Reversible Myocardial stunning with ECG changes of Acute Myocardial Infarction is seen in** (NBE)

  • A. Tako-Tsubo cardiomyopathy
  • C. Brigade's cardiomyopathy

Most common cause of adult dilatedcardiomyopathy is** (NBE)

  • A. Alcoholism
  • B. Viral infection
  • C. Idiopathic
Q329

Tako-Tsubo Cardiomyopathy is a type of:** (NBE)

  • B. Restrictive Cardiomyopathy
  • C. Hypertrophic Cardiomyopathy
  • D. Toxic Cardiomyopathy

Which one of the following is the most common identifiable cause for Restrictive cardiomyopathy': (AIIMS May 04)

  • B. Hemochromatosis
  • C. Amyloidosis
  • D. Sarcoidosis

Cardiomyopathy-Neutropenia syndrome is also called as** (NBE)

  • A. Pompe Disease
  • B. Barth Syndrome
  • C. Holt-Oram Syndrome
  • D. Fraser Syndrome
Q332

All are true about Hypertrophic Obstructive Cardiomyopathy, except :** (AI 2000)

  • A. β agonist are useful
  • B. Asymmetrical hypertrophy of septum
  • C. Sudden death may occur
  • D. Condition improves on passive leg raising
Q333

Typical age of presentation of Hypertrophic Cardiomyopathy is** (NBE)

  • A. 10 - 30 years
  • B. 20 - 40 years
  • C. 30 - 50 years
  • D. 40 - 60 years
Q334

Which of the following accompanies HOCM** (NBE)

  • C. MS
Q335

  • B. Right ventricular conduction Abnormality
  • C. Epilepsy
  • D. Snake bite
