Which of the following heart diseases is most commonly cause of sudden death in young Athletes:

Q337

Features of Hypertrophic Cardiomyopathy include all of the following except:** (NBE)

  • A. Asymmetric Septal Hypertrophy
  • B. Systolic Anterior Motion of Mitral Valve
  • C. Dynamic Left Ventricular Outflow Obstruction
  • D. Pathophysiological Systolic Dysfunction
Q338

All are true about hypertrophic cardiomyopathy, except:** (AIIMS 1996)

  • A. Systolic dysfunction
  • B. Concentric hypertrophy
  • C. Diastolic dysfunction
  • D. Double apical impulse
Q339

The murmur of hypertrophic obstructive cardiomyopathy is decreased in which of the following: (AIIMS Nov. 2000)

  • A. Supine position
  • B. Standing position
  • C. Valsalva maneuver
  • D. Amyl nitrate inhalation
Q340

A 68-year old man who has had a recent syncopal episode is hospitalized with congestive heart failure. His blood pressure is 160/80 mmHg. His pulse rate is 80 beats per minute, and there is a grade III/IV harsh systolic murmur. An echocardiogram shows a disproportionately thickened ventricular septum and systolic anterior motion of the mitral valve. Which of the following findings would most likely be present in this man :** (AIIMS Nov 99)

  • A. Radiation of the murmur to the neck.
  • B. Decrease of the murmur with hand grip
  • C. Delayed carotid upstroke
  • D. Reduced left ventricular ejection fraction
Q341

A 16 year old male is referred by high school coach for a physical examination before joining the football team. His elder brother had died suddenly during football practise, no autopsy was done. The patient has a loud systolic murmur on chest auscultation. All of the following would be constant with hypertrophic cardiomyopathy, except :** (AIIMS Nov 99)

  • A. A crescendo-decrescendo systolic murmur
  • B. Murmur radiating to neck
  • C. Brisk carotid upstroke
  • D. Increase in murmur during valsalva or standing
Q342

Murmur of obstructive type of HCM (HOCM), is best heard at** (NBE)

  • A. A1 area
  • B. A2 area
  • C. Pulmonary area
  • D. Lower left sternal border
Q343

Which of the following drugs are contraindicated in HOCM** (NBE)

  • B. Propranolol
  • C. Digoxin
Q344

The 9 month old child of a diabetic mother presents with tachypnea and hepatomegaly. Echocardiography of the heart showed normal cardiac morphology with asymmetric septal hypertrophy. Which of the following you will give to treat this child :** (AIIMS Nov. 2000)

  • B. Frusemide
  • D. Isoprin
Q345

Aggravation of symptoms of angina in a patient when given nitrates is seen in :** (AIIMS June 2000)

  • C. Single left coronary artery stenosis
  • D. Idiopathic hypertrophic subaortic stenosis
Q346

A 35-year-old farmer consulted a local medical practitioner for recurrent attacks of chest pain. His elder brother had similar complaints and had died suddenly at the age of 40 years. The farmer was advised to take nitroglycerine sublingually at the time of pain. However, the patient finds that the intensity of pain is increased by nitroglycerine. Most probably, he is suffering from :** (AIIMS Nov 02)

  • A. Subacute bacterial endocarditis involving the aortic valve.
  • B. Hypertrophic obstructive cardiomyopathy.
  • C. Degenerative mitral regurgitation.
  • D. Chronic Type A dissection of aorta.
Q347

All are the true regarding hypertrophic obstructive cardiomyopathy, except:** (AIIMS Dec 97)

  • A. Digitalis is useful
  • B. Left ventricular outflow obstruction
  • C. Asymmetrical septal thickness
Q348

Pericardial cavity normally contains how much fluid** (NBE)

  • A. 5-10 ml of fluid
  • B. 10-20 ml of fluid
  • C. 15-50ml of fluid
  • D. 50-100ml of fluid
Q349

All of the following are seen in cardiac tamponade except:** (AI 2004)

  • A. Pulsus paradoxus
  • B. Diastolic collapse of right ventricle on echocardiogram
  • C. Electrical alternans
  • D. Kussmaul's sign
Q350

All of the following may be seen in patients of cardiac tamponade except:** (AI 06)

  • A. Kussmaul's sign
  • B. Pulsus paradoxus
  • D. Right ventricular diastolic collapse on echocardiogram
Q351

All are features, seen in cardiac tamponade, except:** (AIIMS Dec 95)

  • B. Kussmaul sign
  • C. Rapid y descent
  • D. Pulsus paradoxus
Q352

Pulsus paradoxus is a characteristic feature of:** (AIIMS Dec 92)

  • B. Cardiac Tamponade
  • C. Hypertrophic obstructive cardiomyopathy
Q353

A 25 year old patient presented with cardiac tamponade. As an intern you were asked to measure the blood pressure of the patient. What precautions you will advise the patient?** (AIIMS Nov 18)

  • A. Take deep breaths
  • B. Rapid shallow breaths
  • C. Hold breaths
  • D. Breath normally
Q354

Electric alternans is seen in:** (AI 1995)

  • D. Right Ventricular MI (RVMI)
Q355

Beck's Triad is seen in:** (NBE, AI 2010)

  • A. Constrictive Pericarditis
  • B. Cardiac tamponade
  • C. Right ventricular Myocardial Infarction (RVMI)
  • D. Restrictive Cardiomyopathy
Q356

Beck's triad of cardiac tamponade includes all, Except:** (AI 1991)

  • A. Hypotension
  • B. Increased JVP
  • C. Muffled Heart sounds (Silent Heart)
Q357

Beck's triad is?** (NBE)

  • A. ↑ heart sound, distended neck veins, hypotension
  • B. ↑ heart sound, distended neck veins, hypertension
  • C. Muffled heart sound, distended neck veins, hypotension
  • D. Muffled heart wound, distended neck veins, hypertension
Q358

Beck's triad of cardiac tamponade includes (select three correct options):** (PGI Dec 03)

  • B. Neck vein distension
  • C. Paradoxical pulse
  • D. Silent heart
  • E. Tachycardia
Q359

A pt presents with engorged neck veins, BP 80/50 and pulse rate of 100 following blunt trauma to the chest : Diagnosis is:** (AI 2001)

  • A. Pneumothorax
  • B. Right ventricular failure
  • C. Cardiac tamponade
  • D. Hemothorax
Q360

A young motorist suffered injuries in a major road traffic accident. He was diagnosed to have fracture of left femur and left humerus. He was also having fractures of multiple ribs anteriorly on both the sides. On examination the blood pressure was 80/60 mm Hg, and heart rate was 140/minute. The patient was agitated, restless, and tachypneic. Jugular veins were distended. Air entry was adequate in both the lung fields. Heart sounds were barely audible. Femoral pulses were weakly palpable but distally no pulsation could be felt. On priority basis, the immediate intervention would be:** (AIIMS Nov 02)

  • A. Rapid blood transfusion.
  • B. Urgent pericardial tap.
  • C. Intercostal tube drainage on both the sides.
  • D. Fixation of left femur & repair of femoral artery
Q361

A post-operative cardiac surgical patient developed sudden hypotension, raised central venous pressure, pulsus paradoxus at the 4th post operative hour. The most probable diagnosis is:** (AI 2003)

  • A. Excessive mediastinal bleeding
  • B. Ventricular dysfunction
  • C. Congestive cardiac failure
  • D. Cardiac tamponade
Q362

Kussmaul's sign is NOT seen in** (AI 2001)

  • A. Restrictive cardiomyopathy
  • D. RV infarct
Q363

Kussmaul's sign is classically described in:** (NBE)

  • A. Restrictive Cardiomyopathy
  • B. Pericardial Tamponade
  • D. Right Ventricular Infarct
Q364

Kussmaul's sign is seen in all of the following, Except:** (NBE)

Q365

A 30 year male patient presents to the emergency department with ahistory of acute breathlessness. On examination, JVP is increased and an inspiratory decline in systolic blood pressure of 14mm Hg is observed. Which of the following statements about the condition is true:** (PGI June 2008)

  • B. Low ECG voltage
  • C. Prominent 'y' descent
  • D. Thickened Pericardium
Q366

Auenbrugger's sign is seen in:** (NBE)

  • A. Pericardial Effusion
  • D. Mitral Stenosis
Q367

All of the following are features of Moschowitz triad in Pericardial effusion, except:** (NBE)

  • A. Widening of the cardiac silhouette
  • B. Bunting of cardiophrenic angle on the right side
  • C. An increase in cardiac dullness in the second intercostal space
  • D. An abrupt transition of pulmonary resonance to cardiac dullness.
Q368

Preferred Approach for Pericardiocentesis is:** (NBE)

  • A. Sub-xiphoid
  • B. Left Parasternal
  • C. Right Parasternal
  • D. Apical
Q369

Most commonly utilized Intercostal Space for Pericardiocentesis is:** (NBE)

  • A. Left 5th Intercostal Space
  • B. Left 6th Intercostal Space
  • C. Right 5th Intercostal Space
  • D. Right 6th Intercostal Space
Q370

Which of the following structures of the pericardium is insensitive to pain:** (NBE)

  • A. Fibrous Pericardium
  • B. Parietal layer of Serous pericardium
  • C. Visceral layer of Serous pericardium
Q371

Chest pain from pericardial inflammation in pericarditis is referred by:** (NBE)

  • A. Vagus nerve
  • B. Phrenic nerve
  • C. Trigeminal nerve
  • D. Sympathetic nerves
Q372

Which is the clinical type of acute pericarditis?** (< 6 weeks) (NBE)

  • A. Adhesive
  • B. Fibrinous
  • C. Caseous
  • D. Purulent
Q373

An unexplained hemorrhagic pericardial effusion should prompt consideration of** (NBE)

  • A. Tuberculosis
  • B. Viral Pericarditis
  • C. Metastatic disease
  • D. Fungal Pericarditis
Q374

Haemorrhagic pericarditis occurs in all of the following conditions except:** (AIIMS May 03)

  • A. Transmural myocardial infarction.
  • B. Dissecting aneurysm of aorta.
  • C. Metastatic disease of pericardium.
  • D. Constrictive pericarditis.
Q375

Which of the following is least likely to cause constrictive pericarditis?** (AIIMS Nov. 03)

  • A. Tuberculous pericardial effusion
  • B. Staphylococcal effusion
  • C. Post cardiac surgery
  • D. Acute rheumatic fever
Q376

All of the following statements about chronic constrictive pericarditis are true except** (PGI Dec 99)

  • A. Commonest cause in India is 'idiopathic'
  • B. Kussmaul's sign is present
  • C. Ascitis is not in proportion to edema
  • D. Right ventricular End Diastolic pressure is raised
Q377

All of the following are features of constrictive pericarditis Except:** (PGI Dec 99)

  • A. Ascitis
  • B. Retractile Apex
  • C. Pericardial knock
  • D. Acute pulmonary Edema
Q378

During ventricular pressure pulses square root wave is seen in –** (PGI Dec-98)

  • B. MVPS
Q379

Restrictive cardiomyopathy may be differentiated from constrictive pericarditis by the following features (select two options):** (PGI June 02)

  • A. Diastolic pressures are equalized
  • C. Thick pericardium is present
  • D. RV size
Q380

Rapid X descent unlikely in** (PGI Dec 99)

  • C. RVMI
Q381

Broadbent's sign is a feature of** (NBE)

  • C. Acute constrictive pericarditis
  • D. Adhesive pericarditis
Q382

Most common cause of Hypertension in adults is:** (NBE)

  • A. Essential Hypertension
  • B. Pheochomocytoma
  • C. Renal Artery Stenosis
  • D. Chronic Glomerulonephritis
Q383

Non-modifiable risk factor for hypertension:** (NBE)

  • A. Gender
  • C. Salt intake
  • D. Cultural characteristic acquired over time
Q384

Which of the following is a non-modifiable risk factor for hypertension?** (NBE)

  • A. Gender/sex
  • B. High salt intake
  • C. Alcohol addiction
  • D. Dietary pattern due to strong socio-cultural influences
Q385

Hypertension with Hypokalemia is seen in:** (NBE)

  • A. Bartter Syndrome
  • B. Liddle's Syndrome
  • C. Gitelman's Syndrome
Q386

Causes of hypokalemic metabolic alkalosis with hypertension** (NBE)

  • A. Liddle syndrome
  • B. Bartter syndrome
  • C. Gitelman's syndrome
Q387

A patient with Hypertension presents with Hypokalemia, Aldosterone levels and Renin levels are both elevated. The most likely diagnosis is:** (NBE)

  • A. Renal Artery Stenosis
  • B. Primary Hyperaldosteronism (Conn's Syndrome)
  • C. Addison's Disease
Q388

Management of uncomplicated essential hypertension is:** (NBE)

  • A. No need to treat
  • B. Diet modification and exercise
  • C. Diet modification, exercise & drugs
  • D. Drugs alone
Q389

First line drug choice for management of hypertension in patients with angina:** (NBE)

  • A. Beta Blockers
  • B. ACE Inhibitors
  • C. Calcium Channel Blockers
  • D. Hydralazine
Q390

Initial Antihypertensive agent of choice in a patient with stable ischemic heart disease is** (NBE)

  • A. Beta-Blockers
  • B. Alpha-Blockers
  • D. Ace Inhibitors
Q391

Which of the following drugs is MOST suitable for a 45-year-old diabetic with blood pressure 150/95 mmHg?** (NBE)

  • A. Lisinopril
  • B. Amlodipine
  • D. Hydrochlorothiazide
Q392

A hypertensive diabetic is having proteinuria, antihypertensive of choice is:** (AIIMS May 95)

  • A. Propranolol
  • B. Clonidine
  • D. Alpha methyldopa
Q393

An elderly patient presents with hypertension and diabetes, proteinuria without renal failure. Antihypertensive of choice is:** (AIIMS Nov 93)

  • A. Furosemide
  • B. Methyldopa
  • D. Propranolol
Q394

A young patient presented with blood pressure of 190/120 mm of Hg without any clinical symptom and fundus examination is normal, treatment of choice:** (PGI June 03)

  • A. Oral Nitroglycerine
  • B. I.V. Nitroglycerine
  • C. Oral Enalapril
  • D. IV Enalapril
Q395

Monogenic AD cause of HTN?** (PGI 2009)

  • A. 17-α Hydroxylase deficiency
  • B. Gordon's Syndrome
  • C. Pregnant Exacerbated HTN
  • D. Glucocorticoid responsive Aldosteronism
  • E. Glucocorticoid Remediable Aldosteronism
Q396

Which of the following statements regarding hypertensive crisis is True.** (AIIMS Nov 1992)

  • A. Hypertensive urgency is characterized by marked elevation of blood pressure and rapidly evolving end
  • B. Hypertensive emergencies require immediate reduction of blood pressure within minutes
  • C. Hypertensive urgencies may be managed without extensive monitoring on an outpatient basis.
Q397

All the following agents may be used for the management of hypertensive crisis Except:** (AIIMS May 1992)

  • B. Nitroglyarine
  • C. Indapamide
  • D. Esmolol
Q398

A male patient presents with headache, profuse sweating and palpitations with a blood pressure of 180/120 mm Hg. The drug of choice would be** (NBE)

  • A. Nifedipine
  • B. Labetalol
  • C. Prazocin
  • D. Phenoxy benzamine
Q399

All of the following drugs can be used in the management of hypertensive crises Except:** (AIIMS May 1994)

  • A. Hydralazene
  • B. Trimethophan
  • C. Nifedipine
  • D. Indapamide
Q400

All of the following are used for hypertensive emergencies Except:** (NBE)

  • A. Fenoldapam
  • B. Nitroglycerine
  • C. Nitroprusside
  • D. Clonidine
Q401

All of the following are useful intravenous therapy for hypertensive emergencies except-** (AIIMS May 03)

  • A. Fenodopan
  • B. Uradipil
  • C. Enalaprilat
  • D. Nifedipine
Q402

A patient presents with headache and profuse sweating. On examination his blood pressure is recorded as 200/120 mm Hg. Which of the following agents is preferred

  • B. Sodium nitroprusside
  • C. Methyldopa
  • D. Phenoxybenzamine
Q403

In Accelerated HTN what is metabolic defect:** (PGI June 2000)

  • A. Normal non-ionic metabolic acidosis
  • B. Ionic gap met acidosis
  • D. Metabolic alkalosis & Hypokalemia
Q404

Which of the following is the most specific and sensitive screening test for Renovascular Hypertension.** (AIIMS May 01)

  • A. HRCT
  • B. CT Angiography
  • C. Captopril enhanced radionucleotide scan
  • D. MRI
Q405

Which of the following is the most specific screening test for renovascular hypertension:** (PGI 2008)

  • A. Magnetic Resonance Angiography (MRA)
  • B. Spiral Computed Tomographic Angiography (CT Angiography)
  • C. Captopril induced Radionucleotide Scan (Captopril Renogram)
  • D. Duplex Doppler Ultrasonography
Q406

A 20 year old female presents with a blood pressure of 160/110 mm Hg. Clinical examination reveals a bruit in both flanks. Which of the following statements about this patient is not true (select one option)** (PGI 09)

  • A. Enalapril may deteriorate renal function
  • B. Most definitive diagnostic procedure is contrast enhanced angiography
  • C. Condition is nearly always bilateral
  • D. Surgical intervention may be used
  • E. Fibromuscular dysplasia is the likely cause in this patient
Q407

Most common cause of renal artery stenosis in young adults in India is:** (AIIMS Dec 97)

  • A. Atherosclerosis
  • B. Non specific aorto-arteritis
  • C. Fibro muscular dysplasia
Q408

Renal artery stenosis may occur in all of the following except:** (AI 2006)

  • B. Fibromuscular dysplasia
  • C. Takayasu's arteritis
  • D. Polyarteritis nodosa
Q409

In primary pulmonary hypertension basic abnormality in gene lies in:** (AIIMS May 07)

  • A. Bone morphogenetic protein receptor II
  • B. Endothelin
  • C. Homebox gene
  • D. PAX – 11
Q410

Not seen in pre capillary pulmonary hypertension:** (PGI June 98)

  • A. ↑ pressure in pulm. circulation
  • B. ↑ capillary pressure
  • C. Rt-vent.hypertrophy
  • D. ↑ Pulmonary capillary wedge pressure
Q411

Precapillary Pulmonary hypertension is caused by all Except:** (PGI June 03)

  • B. Pulmonary vasculitis
  • C. Primary pulmonary hypertension
  • D. Thromboembolism
Q412

All are causes of pulmonary hypertension except-** (AIIMS Nov 07)

  • A. Hyperoventilation
  • B. Morbid obesity
  • C. High altitude
  • D. Fenfluramine
Q413

Pulmonary hypertension may occur in all of the following conditions except-** (AIIMS Nov 06)

  • A. Toxic oil syndrome
  • B. Progressive systemic sclerosis
  • C. Sickle cell anemia
  • D. Argemone mexicana poisoning
Q414

A 50 year old man, an alcoholic and a smoker presents with a 3 hour history of increasing shortness of breath. He started having this pain while eating, which was constant and radiated to the back and interscapular region. He was a known hypertensive. On examination, he was cold and clammy with a heart rate of 130/min, and a BP of 80/40 mmHg. JVP was normal. All peripheral pulses were present and equal. Breath sounds were decreased at the left lung base and chest x-ray showed left pleural effusion. Which one of the following is the most likely diagnosis?** (AI 2005)

  • A. Acute aortic dissection.
  • B. Acute myocardial infarction.
  • C. Rupture of the esophagus.
  • D. Acute pulmonary embolism.
Q415

Manifestation of Acute Dissection include all of the following, Except:** (PGI June 01)

  • C. MR
  • D. AMI
  • E. Limb ischemia
Q416

All of the following factors predispose to Aortic dissection, Except:** (PGI June 02)

  • A. Systemic hypertension
Q417

All of the following agents may be used in the treatment of Aortic dissection. (select two options): (PGI Dec 05)

  • B. Diazoxide
  • C. Na nitroprusside
  • E. Labetolol
Q418

The 2010 AHA Guidelines for CPR during Basic Life Support Recommends:** (AIIMS 2013)

  • A. Airway – Breathing – Compression (A-B-C)
  • B. Compression – Breathing- Airway (C-B-A)
  • C. Compression- Airway – Breathing (C-A-B)
  • D. Breathing – Airway – Compression (B-A-C)
Q419

Key issues and major changes in the 2010 AHA Guidelines for CPR for lay rescuer in adult CPR include all of the following Except:** (AIIMS 2013)

  • A. Change in CPR Sequence: C-A-B Rather than A-B-C
  • B. Emphasis on "Look, Listen, and Feel' for Breathing
  • C. Chest Compression Rate: At Least 100 per Minute
  • D. Chest Compression Depth to at least 2 inches (5 cm).
Q420

The 2010 AHA Guidelines for CPR during Basic Life Support for neonates Recommends:** (AIIMS 2013)

Q421

Cardiopulmonary resuscitation in adults the depth of the chest compression should be** (NBE)

  • A. 2 cm
  • B. 4 cm
  • C. 5cm
  • D. 6 cm
Q422

In cardiopulmonary resuscitation chest compression should be administered in the center of the chest on the** (NBE)

  • A. Upper half of the sternum
  • B. Middle of the sternum
  • C. Lower half of the sternum
Q423

In cardiopulmonary resuscitation performed by two persons the ratio of compressions to breaths is

  • A. 30:1
  • B. 30:2
  • C. 15:1
  • D. 15:2
Q424

Which of the following is the preferred antiarrhythmic agent in a patient with cardiac arrest:** (AIIMS 2013)

  • B. Lidocaine
  • C. Procainamide
  • D. Atropine
Q425

According to the 2010 ACLS Guidelines by AHA, which of the following pharmacological agents is not used in the management of cardiac arrest :** (AIIMS 2013)

  • A. Epinephrine
  • B. Vasopressin
  • C. Atropine
Q426

Alternative drug for cardiac arrest in place of epinephrine that has been removed from the AHA/ACLS Cardiac Arrest Algorithm:** (NBE)

  • B. Atropine
  • C. Vasopressin
Q427

Alternative drug for cardiac arrest in place of epinephrine is?** (NBE)

  • E. Amiodarone infusion
Q428

A young patient sustains cardiac arrest, in the medical ward. Immediate defibrillation is advised when the ECG shows:** (AI 1992)

  • A. Ventricular Tachycardia
  • B. Asystole
  • C. Electromechanical dissociation
  • D. Persistant Bradyarrhythmia
Q429

True regarding cardio pulmonary resuscitation is:** (AIIMS Dec 98)

  • A. Most common presentation in ECG is asystole
  • B. Compression to ventilation ratio is 5:1
  • C. Adrenaline is given if cardioversion fails
  • D. Calcium gluconate is given immediately
Q430

All of the following may be used in the treatment of cardiac arrest following ventricular fibrillation, Except (Select two best options)** (PGI 09)

  • B. External cardiac pacing
  • C. Epinephrine
  • D. Antiarrhythmic agents
  • E. Vasopressin
Q431

True regarding cardio pulmonary resuscitation is-** (AIIMS Dec 97)

Q432

Asynchronous cardioversion is given in:** (AIIMS Nov 98)

  • A. AF
  • B. VF
  • C. Atrial Flutter
  • D. VT
Q433

What would be the first line of treatment is a patient who develops ventricular fibrillation after intravenous injection of potassium chloride-** (AIIMS May 2004)

  • A. Cardiac massage
  • B. I.V Adrenaline
  • C. Defibrillation
  • D. IPPV
Q434

The best chances of recovery after successful cardiopulmonary resuscitation are seen in:** (AI 1993)

  • B. Ventricular Fibrillation
  • C. Asystole
  • D. Electromechanical dissociation
Q435

In cardio pulmonary resuscitation, calcium can be given in all, EXCEPT-** (AIIMS Dec 97)

  • C. Hyperkalemia
  • D. Calcium channel blocker
Q436

Most common tumour of heart is:** (AI 99)

  • A. Myxoma
  • B. Rhabdomyosarcoma
  • C. Fibroma
  • D. Leiomyosarcoma
Q437

The commonest tumor of the myocardium is :** (AI 94)

  • B. Rhabdomyoma
  • C. Sarcoma
  • D. Fibroma
Q438

Most common tumor of cardiac valves is** (NBE)

  • A. Lipoma
  • B. Papillary Fibroelastoma
  • C. Myxoma
  • D. Cardiac Metastasis
Q439

Which of the following is the most common site of involvement in cardiac metastases** (NBE)

  • A. Pericardium
  • B. Myocardium
  • C. Endocardium
  • D. Cardiac Valves
Q440

Which of the following statements about atrial myxomas is true** (NBE)

  • A. Most common in Left Atrium
  • B. More common in Males
  • C. Distant metastasis are seen
  • D. Most myxomas are familial
Q441

All of the following statement about atrial myxomas are true, except:** (AI 2002)

  • A. Most common site is Left Atrium
  • B. Most common in young individuals
  • C. Distant metastasis are rare
Q442

True statement about cardiac myxoma is :** (AI 1999)

  • A. Commonest site is left atrium
  • B. Rarely reoccurs after excision
  • D. More common in females
Q443

All of the following are clinical features of myxoma, except :** (AIIMS June 2000)

  • A. Fever
  • B. Clubbing
  • C. Hypertension
  • D. Embolic phenomenon
Q444

Gradient in pulmonary artery wedge pressure and left ventricular end diastolic pressure is seen in

  • C. Left atrial myxoma & mitral stenosis
  • D. Pulmonary thromboembolism
Q445

Carney syndrome is characterized by all except(NBE)

  • A. Spotty Skin Pigmentation
  • B. Myxomas
  • C. Cataract
  • D. Pituitary Adenomas
Q446

NAME syndrome includes all except** (NBE)

  • A. Nevi
  • C. Myxoedema
  • D. Ephelides
Q447

LAMB syndrome includes all except** (NBE)

  • A. Lentigines
  • C. Melanoma
  • D. Blue Nevi
Q448

Which is a minor criteria for diagnosis of RF according to modified Jones criteria?** (AI 2007)

  • A. ASO titre
  • B. Past History of Rheumatic Fever
  • D. Subcutaneous nodules
Q449

Which of the following is a minor criteria for diagnosis of Rheumatic Fever (RF) according to modified Jones criteria?** (AI 2007)

Q450

Which of the following is not included in Jone's Major Criteria** (AIIMS Nov 2010)

  • A. Pancarditis
  • B. Chorea
  • C. Subcutaneous nodule
  • D. High ESR
Q451

All of the following are major criteria in the diagnosis of Acute Rheumatic Fever, Except:** (AIIMS

  • A. Migratory Polyarthralgia
  • B. Subcutaneous Nodules
  • C. Chorea
  • D. Carditis
Q452

Major criteria of Rheumatic fever include (select three options) -** (NBE)

  • A. Chorea
  • B. Erythema nodosum
  • C. Arthritis
  • D. Fever
  • E. Carditis
Q453

True about acute rheumatic fever (select three options)** (PGI Dec-02)

  • D. Caused by antecedent α - hemolytic streptococcus infection
Q454

Characteristic feature of Rheumatic carditis is:** (AI 99)

  • A. Pericarditis
  • B. Endocarditis
  • C. Myocarditis
  • D. Pancarditis
Q455

A 10 year old boy, Pappu, died of acute rheumatic fever. All the following can be expected at autopsy except :** (AI 02)

  • A. Ashoff nodules
  • B. Rupture of chordae tendinae
  • C. Mc Callum patch
  • D. Fibinous pericarditis
Q456

Diagnostic feature in rheumatic heart disease is :** (AI 97)

  • A. Aschoff's nodule
  • B. Adeno cell Carcinoma
  • C. Alveolar cell Carcinoma
  • D. Libman-sacks endocarditis
Q457

Aschoff's nodules are seen in :** (AI 05)

  • A. Subacute bacterial endocarditis
  • B. Rheumatic carditis
  • C. Nonbacterial thrombotic endocarditis
  • D. Rheumatic infarction
Q458

McCallum's patch is diagnostic of :** (AI 95)

  • A. Infective endocarditis
  • B. Rheumatic infarction
  • C. Tetralogy of fallout (TOF)
  • D. Myocardial infarction
Q459

All are true about rheumatic fever, except :** (AIIMS June 99)

  • A. Common in poor socioeconomic group
  • B. Develops after streptococcal pharyngitis
  • C. Communicable disease
  • D. Seen in 5-15 year of children
Q460

True about Rheumatic fever -** (PGI Dec 03)

  • A. Chorea is aggravated during pregnancy
  • B. Chorea & arthritis co-existing
  • C. Subcutaneous nodules are tender
  • D. Erythema multiforme seen
Q461

True statement about Rheumatic fever in children (select two options)-** (PGI Dec- 03)

  • A. Polyarthritis
  • B. Caused by α hemolytic streptoccci
  • C. Erythem marginatum is most common manifestation
  • D. MC valve involvement is Mitral
  • E. Erythema marginatum is common in face
Q462

True about Erythema Marginatum in Acute Rheumatic fever is:** (PGI Dec 01)

  • A. Pruritic
  • B. Commonly involves face
  • C. Common manifestation of Acute Rheumatic fever
  • D. Usually associated with carditis
Q463

True about subcutaneous nodule in Rheumatic fever (select two options)-** (PGI Dec 03)

  • A. Non tender
  • B. Most common manifestation
  • C. Present in extensor surfaces & ear (knee)
  • D. Associated with arthritis
Q464

Earliest valvular lesion in a case of acute rheumatic fever is-** (AIIMS May 94)

  • A. Mitral regurgitation (MR)
  • B. Aortic Regurgitation (AR)
  • C. Mitral stenosis (MS)
  • D. Aortic Stenosis (AS)
Q465

The commonest mode of inheritance of congenital heart disease is** (AI 2002)

  • A. Autosomal dominant
  • B. Autosomal recessive
  • C. Sex linked dominant
  • D. Multifactorial
Q466

Cardiac abnormality seen with Noonan's syndrome is:** (AIIMS May 95)

  • A. VSD
  • C. Pulmonary stenosis
Q467

Differential cyanosis is seen in:** (AI 1996)

Q468

Pressure difference of 5mm Hg between the two upper limbs occurs in which congenital heart disease:** (NBE)

  • B. TGA
  • D. Supra-valvular aortic stenosis
Q469

Congenital heart disease associated with decreased pulmonary blood flow -** (PGI Dec 04)

  • A. Truncus arteriosus
  • B. TAPVC
Q470

All of the following are cyanotic heart diseases, Except :** (PGI June 05)

  • C. Tricuspid Atresia
  • D. Eisenmenger's complex
Q471

All can cause recurrent pulmonary infection except:** (AIIMS Sep 96)

  • B. Recurrent LVF
  • C. TOF
  • D. ASD
Q472

The following features are true for tetralogy of Fallot, except:** (AI 2006)

  • A. Ventricular septal defect
  • C. Atrial septal defect
Q473

Essential criteria for TOF includes all except -** (AIIMS Nov 07)

  • A. Valvular stenosis
  • B. Infundibular stenosis
  • C. Over riding of aorta
  • D. RVH
Q474

Which of the following is a component of Pentalogy of Fallot:** (AI 2007)

  • A. Atrial Septal Defect (ASD)
  • B. Patent Ductus Arteriosus (PDA)
  • C. Coarctation of Aorta (COA)
  • D. Left Ventricular Hypertrophy (LVH)
Q475

All of the following statements about Tetralogy of fallot are true, Except** (NBE)

  • A. JVP is normal
  • B. Second Heart sound is single
  • C. Ejection systolic murmur is third left intercostal space
  • D. First Heart sound is soft
Q476

All of the following are true regarding Tetralogy of fallot except –** (AIIMS May 05)

  • A. Ejection systolic murmur in second intercostal space
  • B. Single second heart sound
  • C. Predominantly left to right shunt
  • D. Normal jugular venous pressure
Q477

In which of the following a 'Coeur en Sabot' shape of the heart is seen:** (AI 2004)

  • A. Tricuspid atresia
  • C. Transposition of great arteries
Q478

Blalock and Taussig shunt is done between:** (AI 2006)

  • A. Aorta to pulmonary artery
  • B. Aorta to pulmonary vein
  • C. Subclavian artery to pulmonary vein
  • D. Subclavian vien to artery
Q479

Potts shunt is:** (AI 2001)

  • A. Rt subclavian artery to rt pulmonary artery
  • B. Descending aorta to left pulmonary artery
  • C. Left subclavian to left pulmonary artery
  • D. Ascending aorta to right pulmonary artery
Q480

Fallots tetralogy manifestation (select two options) -** (PGI June 06)

  • D. Blalock taussig shunt is between pulmonary artery & subclavian artery
Q481

A young female presents with history of dyspnoea on exertion. On examination, she has wide, fixed split S2 with ejection systolic murmur (III/VI) in left second intercostal space. Her EKG shows left axis deviation. The most probable diagnosis is :** (AIIMS May 03)

  • A. Total anomalous pulmonary venous drainage.
  • B. Tricuspid atresia.
  • C. Ostium primum atrial septal defect.
  • D. Ventricular septal defect with pulmonary arterial hypertension.
Q482

All of the following are true about ASD except:** (AIIMS May 04)

  • A. Right atrial hypertrophy
  • B. Left atrial hypertrophy
  • C. Right ventricular hypertrophy
  • D. Pulmonary hypertension
Q483

True about ASD :** (AI 1998)

  • A. Foramen ovale is patent
  • B. Left parasternal heave is due to increased pulmonary artery flow
  • C. S2 is wide and variable
  • D. Systolic murmur is due to rapid flow of blood across the shunt.
Q484

Presence of a pansystolic murmur of Mitral Regurgitation with left axis deviation in a patient with ASD suggest:** (NBE)

  • A. TGA
  • B. Ostium secundum with floppy mitral valve
  • C. Ostium primum with floppy mitral valve
  • D. Pulmonary Hypertension
Q485

In which of the following conditions left atrium is not enlarged:** (AI 2006)

  • C. Aortopulmonary window
  • D. Patent ductus arteriosus
Q486

True about V.S.D. is all except :** (AIIMS June 97)

  • A. Small hole closes spontaneously
  • B. Defect is usually in membranous part
  • C. Endocarditis is commonest complication
  • D. Pulmonary oligemia in chest X-ray
Q487

Differential Cyanosis is seen in: ** (AI 1996)

Q488

True statement about Ductus Arteriosus is :** (AI 2000)

  • A. It undergoes anatomic closure within 24 hrs of birth
  • B. Forms the ligamentum venosum in later life
  • C. It is induced to close by high levels of prostaglandins
  • D. May cause a machinery murmur by its patency.
Q489

MC cause of death in adult with PDA is –** (PGI Dec 99)

  • A. CCF
  • B. Infective endocarditis
  • C. Rupture
  • D. Embolism
Q490

All of the following are characteristics features of Tricuspid Atresia Except:** (AIIMS Dec 98)

  • A. Left Axis deviation
  • B. Right ventricular hypoplasia
  • C. Pulmonary vascularity is diminished
  • D. Splitting of S2
Q491

A five year old child presents with Left ventricular hypertrophy and central cyanosis. What is the most probable diagnosis?** (AIIMS Nov 2000)

  • B. Eisenmenger syndrome
  • C. Tetralogy of Fallot
  • D. Total anomalous pulmonary venous drainage
Q492

A child presents with LVH and pulmonary complications. ECG shows left axis deviation. Most likely diagnosis is:** (AI 2001)

  • B. Tricuspid atresia
  • C. TAPVC
Q493

Right axis deviation is seen in all except –** (AIIMS June- 98)

Q494

Ebstein's anomaly is characterized by:** (NBE)

  • A. Upward displacement of an abnormal tricuspid valve into right atrium
  • B. Downward displacement of an abnormal tricuspid valve into right ventricle
  • C. Ventricularized right atrium
  • D. Atrialized left ventricle
Q495

A patient complains of intermittent claudication, dizziness and headache, likely cardiac lesion is:** (AI 2001) (AIIMS Nov 2000)

Q496

A 1-month-old boy is referred for failure to thrive. On examination, he shows feature of congestive failure. The femoral pulses are feeble as compared to branchial pulses. The most likely clinical diagnosis is:** (AI 2006)

  • A. Congenital aortic stenosis
Q497

All of the following are seen in Coarctation of Aorta, Except:** (NBE)

  • A. Diminution of femoral pulsations
  • B. High incidence of associated Bicuspid aortic valve
  • C. Left ventricular Hypertrophy
  • D. Boot Shaped Heart
Q498

A 27 year old man in noted to have blood pressure of 170/100 mmHg. He has prominent aortic ejection click and murmurs heard over the ribs on the both sides anteriorly and over the back posteriorly. In addition the pulses in the lower extremities are feeble and he complains of mild claudication with exertion. The most likely diagnosis is** (AIIMS Nov 04)

  • A. Atrial septal defect
  • C. Cardiomyopathy
  • D. Coarctation of the aorta
Q499

A 4 1/2- year-old girl always had to wear warm socks even is summer season. On physical examination, it was noticed that she had high blood pressure and her femoral pulse was weak as compared to radial and carotid pulse. A chest radiograph showed remarkable notching of ribs along with their lower borders. This was due to:** (AIIMS Nov 02)

  • A. Femoral artery thrombosis.
  • B. Coarctation of aorta.
  • C. Raynaud's disease.
  • D. Takayasu's arteritis.
Q500

A Ten year old boy presents to the pediatric emergency unit with seizures. Blood pressure in the upper extremity measured as 200/140 mm Hg. Femoral pulses were not palpable. The most likely diagnosis amongst the following is:** (AI 2010)

  • A. Takayasu Aortoarteritis
  • B. Renal parenchymal disease
  • C. Grandmal seiures
  • D. Coarctation of Aorta
Q501

A child presented with headache, dizziness, intermittent claudication with occasional dyspnoea. The most probable diagnosis in :** (AI 1999)

Q502

A 20 year young man presents with exert ional dyspnoea, headache, and giddiness. On examination, there is hypertension and LVH. X-ray picture shows notching of the anterior ends of the ribs. The most like diagnosis is :** (AI 2002)

  • A. Phaeochromocytoma
  • B. Carcinoid syndrome
  • C. Coarctation of the aorta
  • D. Superior Mediastinal syndrome
Q503

All the following findings would be expected in a person with coarctation of the aorta except :** (NBE)

  • A. A systolic murmur across the anterior chest and back and a high-pitched diastolic murmur along the left
  • B. A higher blood pressure in the right arm than in the left arm
  • C. Inability to augment cardiac output with exercise
  • D. Persistent hypertension despite complete surgical repair
Q504

True about co-arctation of aorta :** (AI 1999)

  • A. Most common site is distal to the origin of the left subclavian artery
  • B. Most common age of presentation is at 15-20years
  • C. Superior rib notching is due to erosion by dilated collateral vessels
  • D. Right ventricular hypertrophy is prominent
Q505

Which condition is most commonly associated with coarctation of aorta?** (AI 2008)

  • B. Bicuspid aortic valve
Q506

Coarctation of Aorta is most commonly associated with:** (AI 2011)

  • A. Bicuspid Aortic valve
  • C. Ventricular Septal Defect (VSD)
  • D. Atrial Septal Defect (ASD)
Q507

Coarctation of aorta is associated with all, except:** (AIIMS June 98)

  • A. Turner's syndrome
  • D. Atresia of aortic arch
Q508

Coarctation of the aorta is common in which syndrome:** (AI 1997) (AIIMS 1995)

  • A. Bicuspid aortic valve
  • B. Turner's syndrome
  • C. Renal artery stenosis
Q509

Coarctation of the aorta is common in which syndrome:** (AI 1995)

  • A. Down's
  • B. Turner's
  • C. Klinefelter's
  • D. Noonan's
Q510

Ribnotching of 4-9th ribs with double bulging is seen in :** (AI 1998)

  • A. Aortic aneurysm
  • B. Aortic dessection
  • C. Co-arctation of aorta
  • D. Diaphragmatic hernia

All of the following causes death in coarctation of Aorta except:** (PGI June 2000)

  • B. CCF
  • C. Intra cranial hemorrhage
  • D. Anterior MI

  • A. Often undetected in early life
  • B. It is more common in females than in males
  • C. Post-stenotic dilatation of ascending aorta can be seen
  • D. Diagnosis is made by echocardiography

  • B. Infracardiac.
  • C. Mixed.
  • D. Cardiac.

  • C. The oxygen saturation of the blood in the pulmonary artery is higher than that in the aorta
  • D. Infracardiac type is always obstructive

  • A. Tetralogy of Fallot
  • B. Total Anomalous Pulmonary Venous Connection
  • C. Transposition of Great Vessels

A five day old, full term male infant was severely cyanotic at birth. Prostaglandin E was administered initially and later ballooned atrial septosomy was done which showed improvement in oxygenation. The most likely diagnosis of this infant is :** (AI 2004)

  • B. Transposition of great vessels
  • C. Truncus Arteriosus
  • D. Tricuspid Atresia
Q517

A neonate has recurrent attacks of abdominal pain, restless irritability and diaphoresis on feeding. Cardiac auscultation reveals a nonspecific murmur. He is believed to be at risk for M.I. Likely diagnosis here is:** (AI 2001)

  • A. ASd
  • B. VSd
  • D. Anomalous coronary artery

  • C. Pruning of peripheral pulmonary arteries.
  • D. Dilatation of central pulmonary arteries.

The heart lesion not found in Congenital Rubella infection is:** (AI 1995)

  • D. Drug induced
Q521

NADA's criteria are used for:** (AI 1996)

  • B. Assessment of child for degree of malnutrition
  • C. Assessment of child for presence of heart disease
  • D. Assessment of child for degree of mental retardation

Which of the following is not correct about Duke's major criteria?** (NBE)

  • C. Two positive culture for Staphylococcus Aureus
  • D. One positive culture for Coxiella Burnetii

Bacterial endocarditis is rarely seen in-** (AI 96)

  • A. Small VSD

Infective endocarditis is least common in-** (AIIMS Nov 93)

  • B. Libman sacks Endocarditis

  • B. Both sides of valves
  • C. Valve cusps
  • D. Free margin of valves
Q561

Firm warty vegetations along the line of closure of valves is due to :** (AI 2000)

  • B. Libman Sacks Endocarditis

  • C. Collagen rich, elastic deposits in endocardium of right ventricle and Pulmonary valve

  • B. Tricuspid valves
  • C. Mitral valves
  • D. Aortic valves

Carcinoid syndrome produces valvular disease primarily involving:** (NBE)

  • A. Venous valves
  • B. Tricuspid valve
  • C. Mitral valve
  • D. Aortic valve

Most common valvular lesion in Carcinoid Syndrome of the Heart is:** (NBE)

Most common valvular lesion in carcinoid syndrome of heart is:** (NBE)

In carcinoid syndrome, the appt of heart mostly affected is:** (PGI Dec 97)

  • B. Inflow tract of RV

  • D. T Wave inversion
Q572

Pulmonology

337 Questions

▼
Q1

All of the following types of Respiratory Failures are correctly matched, except:** (NBE)

  • A. Type I – Hypoxemic
  • B. Type II – Hypercapnic
  • C. Type III – Atelectasis
  • D. Type IV – Perioperative
Q2

False statement about type I respiratory failure is:** (AI 2001)

  • A. Decreased PaO₂
  • B. Decreased PaCO₂
  • C. Normal PaCO₂
  • D. Normal A-a gradient
Q3

In ICU setting patients suffering from which of the following respiratory pathology is most predisposed for CO₂ narcosis?** (NBE)

  • A. Motor Neuron Disease
  • B. Asthma
  • C. Emphysema
  • D. Bronchiectasis
Q4

In type - II respiratory failure, there is :** (AIIMS Nov 02)

  • A. Low pO₂ and low pCO₂
  • B. Low pO₂ and high pCO₂
  • C. Normal pO₂ and high pCO₂
  • D. Low pO₂ and normal pCO₂
Q5

Type III respiratory failure occurs due to:** (NBE)

  • A. Hypoxemia
  • B. Inability to eliminate CO₂
  • C. Lung atelectasis
  • D. Hypoperfusion
Q6

Type IV respiratory failure occurs due to:** (NBE)

  • A. Alveolar flooding
  • D. Hypoperfusion of respiratory muscles
Q7

Respiratory acidosis is primarily recognized by:** (NBE)

  • A. Decrease in PO₂
  • B. Increase in PCO₂
  • C. Decrease in HCO₃
Q8

A patient with salicylic acid poisoning has the following arterial blood gas analysis report : pH = 7.12 : pCO₂ = 18mmHg; HCO₃ = 12mmol/L. The resulting acid base abnormality can be best labeled as:** (AIIMS Nov 03, 18)

  • A. Metabolic acidosis with compensatory respiratory alkalosis
  • B. Metabolic acidosis with compensatory respiratory alkalosis
  • C. Respiratory acidosis with metabolic alkalosis
  • D. Metabolic acidosis
Q9

In a patient PO₂ is 85 mmHg, PCO₂ – 50mmHg, pH is 7.2 and HCO₃ is 32 meq/l is suffering from :** (AIIMS

  • A. Respiratory acidosis with compensatory metabolic alkalosis
  • B. Respiratory acidosis with compensatory metabolic acidosis
  • C. Metabolic acidosis
Q10

A patient presents with following parameters pH 7.5, p CO₂ 30 mmHg, pO₂ 102 mmHg and HCO₃ 16 meq/l. Which of the following correctly describes the compensatory mechanism:** (AI 2010)

  • A. Respiratory Alkalosis
  • B. Metabolic Alkalosis
  • C. Respiratory Acidosis
  • D. Metabolic Acidosis
Q11

The acid base status of a patient reveals a pH = 7.46 and pCO₂=30 mm Hg. The patient has a partially compensated primary:** (AI 2011)

  • A. Metabolic acidosis
  • B. Metabolic alkalosis
  • C. Respiratory alkalosis
  • D. Respiratory acidosis
Q12

A patient was admited to the casualty with hysteria and anxiety. Estimation of arterial plasma showed PH of 7.53; HCO₃ of 22 meq and Pco₂ of 27 mm H g. which of the following is the patient likely to have?

  • C. Respiratory acidosis
  • D. Respiratory alkalosis
Q13

Arterial blood gas analysis of a patient reveals pH 7.2, HCO₃ 36mmol/L, pCO₂ 60mm of Hg. The abnormality is :** (AIIMS June 99)

  • B. Respiratory acidosis
  • C. Respiratory alkalosis with compensatory metabolic acidosis
  • D. Respiratory acidosis with compensatory metabolic acidosis
Q14

A 40 year old male develops excessive hyperventilation. ABG reveals pH- 7.5, pCO₂ 24 mmHg, PO₂ 88 mm of Hg. Treatment is :** (AIIMS June 1999)

  • A. Respiratory alkalosis
Q15

An ABG analysis shows : pH 7.2, raised pCO₂, decreased HCO₃; diagnosis is :** (AIIMS June 97)

  • A. Respiratory acidosis
Q16

A 10 year old boy with short stature presents with polyuria & polydipsia. Laboratory values reveal a pH of 7.4; HCO₃ of 17; Na⁺ 140; pCO₂ 32; K⁺ 4.9; Cl⁻ 112. Likely acid base abnormality is:** (AIIMS Nov 2010)

  • A. Metabolic Alkalosis
  • B. Non-Anion gap Metabolic Acidosis
  • C. Anion gap Metabolic Acidosis
  • D. Respiratory Acidosis

  • B. Acute exacerbation of COPD
  • C. ARDS
  • D. Severe pneumonia

  • B. Pulmonary artery hypertension

Asprin sensitive asthma is associated with which of the following?** (NBE)

  • B. Urticaria
