  • D. Extrinsic asthma
Q39

Samter’s triad refers to the association of Asprin sensitive asthma and** (NBE)

  • D. Rhinosinusitis
Q40

Bronchial Asthma is characterized by all of the following, Except:** (PGI June 05)

  • A. Inflammatory disease of airway
  • B. Allergic disease of airway
  • C. Hyporesponsive ness of airway
  • D. Hyperresponsiveness of airway
  • E. Treatment is mostly inhaled steroid
Q41

All of the following are true about Asthma, Except:** (NBE)

  • A. Charcol Leydin crystals may be seen in sputum
  • B. Reversible Airflow obstruction is a characteristic feature
  • C. Large airways are involved
  • D. Small airways are not involved
  • E. Intermittent Asthma responds better to bronchodilator therapy than persistant Asthma
Q42

True about Asthma except:** (PGI 05)

  • A. Inflammatory disease
  • B. Hyperresponsive airways
  • C. Necrosis of airways
  • D. Mucous plug formation
  • E. Airway edema
Q43

Feature of Acute severe Asthma include all of the following, Except:** (NBE)

  • A. Tachycardia > 120/min
  • D. PEFR < 50%
  • E. None (All are Features of Acute severe Asthma)
Q44

Life threatening features of asthma are the following except:** (NBE)

  • A. Drowsy or confused patient
  • B. Presence of pulsus paradoxus
  • C. Silent chest
Q45

In a patient with bronchial asthma silent chest signifies** (NBE)

  • A. Good Prognosis
  • B. Bad Prognosis
  • C. Grave Prognosis
  • D. Not a Prognostic sign
Q46

An Adult with asthma presents with asthma symptoms every day and wakes up in the night approximately 2 to 3 days in a week. He can be classified as having :** (NBE)

  • A. Intermittent Asthma
  • B. Mild Persistent Asthma
  • C. Moderate Persistent Asthma
  • D. Severe Persistent Asthma
Q47

Universal finding in Asthma is** (PGI June 02)

  • A. Hypoxia
  • B. Hypercarbia
Q48

In a Patient with clinical signs of Asthma which of the following tests will confirm the diagnosis:** (NBE)

  • A. Increase in FEV1/FVC
  • B. > 200 ml increase in FEV1 after Methacholine
  • C. Diurnal variation in PEF > 20 Percent
  • D. Reduction of FEV1 > 20 % after bronchodilators
Q49

A Patient with history of shortness of breath has Decreased FEV1/FVC Ratio, Normal DLCO. A 200 ml increase in baseline FEV1 is observed 15 minutes after administration of bronchodilators. The likely diagnosis

  • B. Chronic Bronchitis
  • D. Interstitial Lung Disease
Q50

Bronchial Asthma is best diagnosed by:** (PGI Dec 01)

  • A. Wheeze
  • B. Dyspnea and cough
  • C. FEV₁ values
  • D. Demonstration of Reversible obstruction
  • E. Normal FEV₁
Q51

Anti-inflammatory action on airways is seen with (Mark two correct option):** (PGI June 05)

  • A. Fluticasone
  • B. Iprotropium bromide
  • C. Budesonide
  • D. Theophylline
  • E. Tebutaline
Q52

All of the following are used in Bronchial Asthma, Except:** (NBE)

  • A. Salbutamol
  • B. Aminophylline
  • C. Steroids
  • D. Morphine
Q53

All of the following drugs useful in the treatment of a patient with acute bronchial asthma except : (AIIMS Nov 03)

  • A. Ipratropium
  • B. Salbutamol
  • C. Montelukast
  • D. Hydrocortisone
Q54

Leukotriene inhibitors are used in asthma for -** (NBE)

  • A. Monotherapy for acute attack
  • B. Add-on therapy in patients not controlled by lowdose ICS
  • C. Stratus asthmaticus
Q55

All of the following statements about Leukotriene modifiers in the management of bronchial asthma are true except :** (AIIMS May 1996)

  • A. May be used for acute asthma
  • B. May be used for exercise induced asthma
  • C. Zileuton is Leukotriene modifier
  • D. May uncover Churg strauss syndrome
Q56

Antibody used in the treatment of Bronchial Asthma is:** (NBE)

  • A. Omalizumab
  • B. Rituximab
  • C. Daclizumab
  • D. Transtusuzumab
Q57

The most predictive and dangerous side effect of propranolol that makes it to be avoided in known patient of COPD is induction of :** (AIIMS Nov 01)

  • A. Respiratory failure
  • B. Acute asthmatic attack
  • C. Glaucoma
Q58

A drug is to be delivered by a nebuliser. The size of a droplet for its humidification is :** (AI 2000)

  • A. < 5 μ
  • B. 5-10
  • C. 10-15
  • D. 15-20
Q59

All of the following are used to characterize Asthma, Except :** (NBE)

  • A. Curschman Spirals
  • B. Charcot Layden Crystals
  • C. Reid Index
  • D. Hyperresponsive Airways
Q60

Increased Reid Index is used to characterized:** (AI 2012)

  • B. Bronchiectasis
  • C. Bronchial Asthma
  • D. Emphysema
Q61

Chronic bronchitis is said to be present when patient has chronic cough:** (NBE)

  • A. 3 consecutive months in 2 consecutive years
  • B. 2 consecutive months in 3 consecutive years
  • C. 3 consecutive months in 1 year
  • D. 2 consecutive months in 1 year
Q62

All of the following are true about Chronic Bronchitis, Except:** (NBE)

  • A. Predominant symptom is Dyspnea
  • B. Also known as Blue Bloaters
  • C. DLCO is normal
  • D. Patients are usually obese
Q63

All of the following are clinical signs of COPD except:** (NBE)

  • A. Pursed Lip Breathing
  • B. Hoover sign
  • C. Barrel Chest
  • D. Digital Clubbing
Q64

Which of the following is the least common bacteria responsible for Acute Exacerbation of Chronic Bronchitis** (NBE)

  • A. Streptococcus pneumoniae
  • B. Moraxella catarrhalis
  • C. Haemophilus influenza
  • D. Staphylococcus aureus
Q65

α₁ Antitrypsin deficiency is associated with :** (AI 1999)

  • A. Centriaciner emphysema
  • B. Panaciner-emphysema
  • C. Irregular emphysema
  • D. Paraseptal-emphysema
Q66

All of the following statements about Emphysema are true, Except:** (PGI Dec 04)

  • A. Breathlessness is the charachteristic presenting symptom
  • B. Diffusion rate of carbon monoxide is reduced
  • C. Restrictive pattern on pulmonary function test is seen
  • D. Long term bronchadilater therapy does not improve lung function
Q67

Lung functions in Emphysema reveal all of the following , Except** (PGI – Dec –01)

  • A. Decreased vital capacity
  • B. Increased diffusion capacity for carbon monoxide (DLCO)
  • C. Increased Total Lung capacity
  • D. Decreased FEV₁
  • E. Decreased FEV₁ / FVC ratio
Q68

All of the following are true about Emphysema, except:** (NBE)

  • A. Cyanosis
  • B. Hyperventilation
  • C. Hyperinflated chest
  • D. Slender physque
Q69

Hoover’s Sign is most characteristic of** (NBE)

Q70

Gold criteria for Very Severe COPD is best represented by** (NBE)

  • A. FEV1 / FVC < 0.7 and FEV1 < 30% predicted
  • B. FEV1 / FVC < 0.7 and FEV1 ≥ 30% but <50% predicted
  • C. FEV1 / FVC < 0.7 and FEV1 ≥ 50% but <80% predicted
  • D. FEV1 / FVC < 0.7 and FEV1 ≥ 80% predicted
Q71

GOLD staging to assess severity is used for** (NBE)

  • A. COPD
  • B. Bronchial Asthma
Q72

In an emphysematous patient with bullous lesions, which is the best investigation to measure lung volume:** (AIIMS Nov 08)

  • A. Body Plethysmography
  • B. Gas dilution
  • C. Transdiaphragmatic pressure
  • D. DLco
Q73

In a patient with COPD, Which of the following is most closely associated with decreased mortality

  • A. Supplemental Oxygen
  • B. Inhaled Glucocorticoids
  • C. Beta Agonists
  • D. Bronchodilators
Q74

First line therapy for management of COPD is** (NBE)

  • A. Smoking Cessation
  • B. Oxygen
  • C. Bronchodilators
  • D. Corticosteroids
Q75

Lung volume reduction surgery is recommended for patients with all of the following, except** (NBE)

  • A. Diffuse emphysema
  • B. Upper lobe Emphysema
  • C. Low baseline exercise capacity
Q76

Bilateral upper lobe bronchiectasis suggests a diagnosis of:** (NBE)

  • B. Cystic Fibrosis
  • C. Rheumatoid Arthritis
  • D. Recurrent Aspiration
Q77

Bronchiectasis is most common in which lobe:** (AIIMS May 95)

  • A. Right upper lobe
  • B. Right middle lobe
  • C. Left upper lobe
  • D. Left lower lobe
Q78

Bronchiectasis Sicca is seen with:** (NBE)

  • B. Pertussis
  • C. Cystic fibrosis
  • D. Kartagener syndrome
Q79

Brock’s Syndrome is:** (NBE)

  • A. Bronchiectasis Sicca
  • B. Middle Lobe Bronchiectasis
  • C. Kartagener’s Syndrome
Q80

Which of the following is NOT a complication of bronchiectasis :** (AIIMS Sept 96) (AI 1998)

  • A. Lung abscess
  • B. Lung cancer
  • D. Empyema
Q81

Which of the following is presently the most common cause of death in a patient with chronic extensive bronchiectasis** (NBE)

  • A. Right sided heart failure
  • B. Infection
  • C. Hemoptysis
  • D. Carcinoma
Q82

Imaging modality for confirming the diagnosis of bronchiectasis is:** (NBE)

  • A. High Resolution CT
  • B. Volumetric Multidetector CT
  • C. MRI Scan
  • D. Bronchography
Q83

Rampal, 45 yr old man presents with history of recurrent hemoptysis and purulent sputum. His chest X- Ray is normal, which of the following will be the next best investigation for him?** (AIIMS Nov 01)

  • B. CT guided angiography
  • C. Angiography
  • D. Spiral CT
Q84

All of the following are CT findings in Bronchiectasis, except** (NBE)

  • A. Tram-track sign
  • B. Bronchial tapering
  • C. Tree bud appearance
  • D. Bronchial Wall thickening
Q85

A 1½ months old child with cough, mild stridor is started on oral antibiotics. The child showed initial improvement but later developed wheeze, productive cough and mild fever. X-ray shows hyperlucency and PFT shows an obstructive curve. The most probable diagnosis is:** (AI 2008)

  • A. Bronchiolitis obliterans
  • B. Post viral syndrome
  • C. Pulmonary alveolar microlithiasis
  • D. Follicular bronchitis
Q86

All are true about Interstitial Fibrosis, Except** (PGI – June 03)

  • A. FVC < 80% Predicted
  • B. FEV1/FVC < 0.7
  • C. DLCO is decreased
  • D. TLC is decreased
Q87

All of the following are true about interstitial lung disease, Except:** (PGI June 07)

  • A. Decreased FVC
  • B. Decreased FEV1
  • C. Decreased FEV1/FVC
  • D. Decreased Diffusion capacity
Q88

A 40 year old female presents with progressive dyspnea for one year. Physical examination reveals bibasilar end-inspiratory crepitations. All of the following statements about her condition are true, except:

  • A. May be associated with connective tissue disease
  • B. Residual volume is increased
  • C. Total Lung capacity is decreased
  • D. HRCT is a useful diagnostic test
Q89

A female presents with history of progressive breathlessness. Histology shows heterogenous patchy fibrosis with several fibroblastic foci. The most likely diagnosis is:** (AI 2010)

  • A. Cryptogenic Organizing Pneumonia
  • B. Non Specific Interstitial pneumonia
  • C. Usual Interstitial Pneumonia
  • D. Desquamative Interstitial Pneumonia
Q90

Investigation of choice for interstitial disease is:** (AIPGMEE 08)

  • A. Chest X-ray
  • B. HRCT
  • C. Gallium-67 DTPA scan
Q91

Investigation of choice for detection and characterization of interstitial lung disease is:** (NBE)

  • A. MRI.
  • B. Chest x-ray.
  • C. High resolution CT.
  • D. Ventilation perfusion scan.
Q92

Farmer's Lung is caused due to exposure to:** (NBE)

  • A. Bacillus Subtilis
  • B. Thermoactinomyces Sacchari
Q94

Occupational Lung Disease commonly seen in Textile Industry Workers is:** (NBE)

  • A. Byssinosis
  • B. Bagassosis
  • C. Farmer's Lung
  • D. Asbestosis
Q95

Which of the following is the most common initial symptom of byssinosis?** (NBE)

  • A. Wheezing
  • B. Dyspnea on exertion
  • C. Cough
  • D. Chest tightness
Q96

Caplan Syndrome is Pneumoconiosis with** (NBE)

  • A. Lymphadenopathy
  • B. Congestive Cardiac Failure
  • D. HIV
Q97

All of the following features are seen in asbestosis except-** (AIIMS Nov 02)

  • A. Diffuse pulmonary interstitial fibrosis
  • B. Fibrous pleural thickening
  • D. Calcific pleural plaques
Q98

True statements about asbestosis –** (PGI Dec 04)

  • A. Causes Lung Ca
  • B. Causes mesothelioma
  • C. Peritoneal mesothelioma
  • D. Pulmonary fibrosis
Q99

All of the following statements about silicosis are true, Except** (NBE)

  • A. Pleural plaques
  • B. Predilection for upper lobes
  • C. Calcific Hilar Lymphadenopathy
  • D. Associated with tuberculosis
Q100

Which of the following is true about pneumoconiosis** (NBE)

  • A. Pleural plaques of asbestosis is always symptomatic
  • B. Silicosis is not associated with increased risk of Tuberculosis
  • C. Asbestosis and smoking act synergistically to cause lung cancer
  • D. Coal Workers Pneumoconiosis is associated with Lung cancer
Q101

Not seen idiopathic pulmonary hemosiderosis** (AI 2009)

  • A. Eosinopenia
  • B. Iron deficiency anemia
  • C. Diffuse alveolar hemorrhage
  • D. Hemoptysis
Q102

All of the following are true about Idiopathic Pulmonary Hemosiderosis Except:

  • B. Alveolar capillary constriction
  • C. Hyperplasia of type II Pneumocytes
  • D. Hemosiderin laden macrophages
Q103

All of the following are associated with pulmonary eosinophilic pneumonia, Except:** (PGI 2009)

  • A. ABPA
  • B. Loeffler’s pneumonia
  • C. Churg Strauss Syndrome
  • D. Tropical pulmonary eosinophilia
  • E. Wegener’s granulomatosis
Q104

Lower lobe fibrosis is seen in :** (NBE)

  • A. Silicosis
Q105

All of the following constitute primary diagnostic criteria for ABPA, EXCEPT:** (NBE)

  • A. Elevated serum IgE levels (>417 IU/ml)
  • B. Peripheral eosinophilia (> 500 cells/ml)
  • C. Central /Proximal bronchiectasis
  • D. Identification of A. fumigatus from sputum
Q106

Diagnostic features of allergic bronchopulmonary aspergillosis (ABPA) include all of the following except:** (AI 2003)

  • A. Changing pulmonary infiltrates
  • B. Peripheral eosinophilia
  • C. Serum precipitins against Aspergillosis fumigants
  • D. Occurrence in patients with old cavitatory lesions.
Q107

Diagnostic criteria for Allergic Bronchopulmonary Aspergillosis include all, except** (PGI-Dec05)

  • A. Peripheral eosinophilia (>0.1 x 10⁹/mm³)
  • B. Central bronchiectasis
  • C. Episodic Asthma
  • D. Detection of Aspergillus in sputum
  • E. ↑ IgG antibodies specific to A. Fumigatus
Q108

A 40 year old man presented with repeated episodes of bronchospasm and hemoptysis. Chest X-ray revealed perihilar bronchiectasis. The most likely diagnosis is :** (AI 2002)

  • A. Sarcoidosis
  • B. Idiopathic pulmonary fibrosis
  • C. Extrinsic allergic alveolitis
  • D. Bronchopulmonary aspergillosis
Q109

All the following are true about bronchopulmonary aspergillosis except:** (AI 1995)

  • A. Central bronchiectasis
  • B. Pleural effusion
  • D. Eosinophilia
Q110

True statements about ABPA include all of the following, Except:** (PGI Dec 04)

  • A. Serum IgE > 1000 ng/ml
  • B. Eosinophils > 1000/mm³
  • C. Elevated IgG antibodies
  • D. Proximal Bronchiectasis
  • E. Lower lobe predominance
Q111

Which is not true about aspergillosis-** (NBE)

  • A. Aspergillus Niger is the cause of fungal otitis externa
  • B. It is highly contagious
  • C. Aspergilloma is common in preexisting TB, or cystic disease
  • D. Aspergillus fumigatus is a cause of bronchial aspergillosis
Q112

40 year old patient with history of prolonged exposure to Aspergillus presents with repeated episodes of breathlessness. Chest X-ray shows diffuse pulmonary infiltrates. Skin hypersensitivity test is positive for Aspergillus antigen Peripheral blood picture shows normal eosinophil count and serum IgE levels are normal The most likely diagnosis is** (NBE)

  • A. Allergic bronchial Asthma
  • B. Allergic Bronchopulmonary Aspergillosis (ABPA)
  • C. Extrinsic Allergic Alveolitis
  • D. Invasive Pulmonary Aspergillosis
Q113

All the following are features of Tropical pulmonary Eosinophilia except-** (NBE)

  • A. Eosinophilia > 3000/mm3
  • B. Microfilaria in blood
  • C. Paroxysmal cough and wheeze
  • D. Bilateral chest mottling and increased bronchovascular markings
Q114

Tropical pulmonary eosinophilia is caused because of:** (NBE)

  • A. Occult filariasis.
  • B. Cerebral malaria.
  • C. Pneumonic plague.
  • D. Asthmatic bronchitis.
Q115

All the following diseases are associated with peripheral blood eosinophilia except :** (AIIMS Nov 02)

  • A. Allergic bronchopulmonary aspergillosis (ABPA).
  • B. Loffler’s syndrome.
  • C. Pulmonary eosinophilic granuloma.
  • D. Chrug-Strauss syndrome.
Q116

A 54 year old smoker man comes with severe hemoptysis weight loss and oligoarthritis. Serial skiagram shows fleeting opacities. What is the diagnosis?** (AIIMS Nov 08)

  • A. Allergic bronchopulmonary aspergillosis
  • B. Ca lung
  • C. Tb
  • D. Wegener’s Granulomatosis
Q117

Hypersensitivity Pneumonitis is classically described as a:** (AI 2009)

  • A. Type I hypersensitivity reaction
  • B. Tye II hypersensitivity reaction
  • C. Type III (Immune complex) Hypersensitivity
  • D. Type IV (Cell mediated) Hypersensitivity
Q118

Most common pattern of Pneumonia seen in Klebsiella infection is:** (NBE)

  • A. Lobar Pneumonia
  • B. Bronchopneumonia
  • C. Interstitial Pneumonia
  • D. Miliary Pneumonia
Q119

All of the following statements about Pneumococcal Pneumonia are true, except :** (NBE)

  • A. Non-segmental Consolidation
  • B. Air Bronchogram Absent
  • C. Usually confined to one lobe
  • D. Cavitation is rare
Q120

All of the following are more commonly seen in Klebsiella Pneumonia than in Pneumococcal Pneumonia, Except:** (NBE)

  • A. Lower lobe involvement
  • B. Abscess Formation
  • C. Pleural Effusion
  • D. Cavitation
Q121

Friedlander Pneumonia refers to Pneumonia caused by:** (NBE)

  • A. Klebsiella
  • B. Pneumococcus
  • C. H. Influenzae
  • D. Staphylococcus
Q122

The most common causative organism for lobar pneumonia is:** (NBE)

  • B. Streptococcus pyogenes
  • C. Streptococcus pneumoniae
  • D. Haemophilus influenza
Q123

Round pneumonia is seen with:** (NBE)

  • A. Streptococcal pneumonia
  • B. Kerosene oil aspiration
  • C. Lung cancer
  • D. Mendelson syndrome
Q124

Most common agent for Early Onset Nosocomial pneumonia is** (AI 1998)

  • A. Hemophilus
  • C. Pseudomonas
  • D. Acinobacter
Q125

Cavitatory lesions in lung are seen in:** (AI 2010)

  • A. Primary pulmonary Tuberculosis
  • B. Staphylococcal pneumonia
  • C. Pneumoconiosis
  • D. Interstitial Lung disease
Q126

Devi, a 28 year female, has diarrhoea, confusion, high grade fever with bilateral pneumonitis. The diagnosis is:** (AI 2000)

  • A. Legionella
  • B. Neisseria meningitis
  • D. H. influenza
Q127

A 30 year old male presents with pneumonia and diarrhea five days after discharge from a hospital. The drug of choice for treating him is:** (NBE)

  • A. Vancomycin
  • B. Ciprofloxacin
  • C. Azithromycin
  • D. Gentamycin
  • E. Tetracycline
Q128

Which is not true about legionnaire pneumonia:** (AIIMS May 95)

  • A. Seen mainly in immunocompromised person
  • B. Erythromycin is drug of choice
  • C. Common in patients with hairy cell leukemia
  • D. Smoking and alcohol are risk factor.
Q129

Fever persisting even after treatment of pneumonia likely diagnosis is :** (AIIMS June 97)

  • A. Empyema
  • B. Fungal lesion
  • C. Carcinoma bronchus
Q130

Most common cause of empyema is:** (AIIMS Dec 97)

  • A. Bronchopleural fistula
  • B. Tubercular pneumonia
  • C. Bacterial pneumonia
  • D. Pleurisy
Q131

Atypical pneumonia can be caused by the following microbial agents except?** (AI 2005)

  • A. Mycoplasma pneumoniae.
  • B. Legionella penmophila.
  • C. Human Corona virus.
  • D. Klebsiella pneumoniae.
Q132

All of the following features are seen in the viral pneumonia except :** (AI 2005)

  • A. Presence of interstitial inflammation.
  • B. Predominance of alveolar exudates.
  • C. Bronchiolitis.
  • D. Multinucleate giant cells in the bronchiolar wall.
Q133

Features of pneumocystic carini pneumonia are:** (AIIMS)

  • A. Interstitial pneumonitis
  • B. Eosinophilic exudate
  • C. Vacuolated intralveolar exudate
  • D. Damage to type II pneumocytes
Q134

A 45 year old, HIV positive patient presents with features of pneumonia. Characteristic histopathological features suggesting pneumocystis carinii pneumonia is:** (PGI)

  • A. Prominent Interstitial Pneumonitis
  • B. Eosinophilic Alveolar Exudates
  • C. Prominent mononuclear cells in Alveolar exudates
  • D. Neutrophelic infiltration of alveolar interstitium
  • E. Hypertrophy of type I pneumocytes
Q135

A truck driver presented with history of fever since four weeks, and dry cough. He also gives a history of weight loss of about 10 kg. X-ray shows bilateral reticulonodular infiltrates. The most likely diagnosis is:** (AI 2010)

  • B. Pneumocystis carinii Pneumonia
  • C. Pneumococcal pneumonia
Q136

All of the following statements about Pneumocystis Jiroveci are true Except:** (AI 2008)

  • A. Usually associated with CMV infection
  • B. May be associated with Pneumatocele
  • C. Usually diagnosed by sputum examination
  • D. Causes disease only in the immunocompromised host
Q137

True Statement about Pneumocystic Jiroveci is:** (AIIMS)

  • A. Often associated with CMV infection
  • B. Usually diagnosed by sputum examination
  • C. Infection occurs only in immunocompromised patients
  • D. Always associated with Pneumatocele
Q138

Indication for prophylaxis in pneumocystis carini pneumonia include (choose two best option)** (NBE)

  • A. CD4 count < 200 /µl
  • B. Tuberculosis
  • C. Viral load > 25,000 copies/ml
  • D. Oral candidiasis
Q139

All the following are used in the treatment of Pneumocystis carinii except:** (AI 1994)

  • A. Pentamidine
  • B. Dapsone
  • C. Cotrimoxazole
  • D. Fluoroquinolones
Q140

Drug of choice for pneumocystis carinii is:** (NBE)

  • A. Doxycycline
  • B. Cotrimoxazole
  • C. Tetracycline
  • D. Dapsone
Q141

Drug of choice for Mycoplasma pneumoniae is :** (AIIMS Dec 94, May 94)

  • A. Penicillin
  • B. Tetracycline
  • C. Cefuroxime
Q142

In mycoplasma pneumonia, following are seen except** (PGI-Dec-97)

  • A. Diffuse infiltration of lungs
  • B. Cannot be cultured routinely
  • C. Cefotaxime is the drug of choice
  • D. Serology is useful in diagnosis
Q143

Treatment of chlamydia pneumonia is :** (AIIMS Sept 96)

  • A. Erythromycin
  • B. Ceftriaxone
  • C. Penicillin
  • D. Sulphonamide
Q144

Preferred Outpatient Empirical Antibiotic Treatment of Community-Acquired Pneumonia in a healthy individual with no comorbidities and no antibiotics in the last three months is:** (NBE)

  • A. Clarithromycin
  • B. Levofloxacin
  • C. Ceftriaxone
  • D. Linezolid
Q145

An elderly male admitted for Pneumonia presents with diarrhea and gripping abdominal pain five days after discharge from the hospital. Drug which is likely to benefit is.** (NBE)

  • A. Imodium
  • B. Metranidozole
  • C. Diphenoxylate
  • D. Levofloxacin
Q146

Commonest sign of aspiration pneumonitis is :** (AIIMS Dec 94)

  • B. Tachypnea
  • C. Crepitations
  • D. Rhonchi
Q147

True regarding presentation of primary T.B. is :** (AIIMS Dec 98)

  • A. B/L pleural effusion with negative Tuberculin test.
  • B. U/L hilar lymphadenopathy
  • C. Sustained chronic pyrexia
  • D. B/L pleural effusion with positive tuberculin test.
Q148

All of the following statements about primary Tuberculosis are true, Except:** (NBE)

  • A. Cavitatory lesion
  • C. Fibrocaseous lesion
  • D. Phlyctenular conjunctivitis
Q149

Primary Tuberculosis most commonly involves** (NBE)

  • A. Lungs
  • C. Brain
  • D. Intestine
Q150

Reactivation tuberculosis is usually seen at-** (NBE)

  • A. Posterior segment of Upper lobes
  • B. Posterior segment of Middle lobe
  • C. Posterior segment of Lower lobe
  • D. Inferior segment of Middle lobe
Q151

Primary complex in which of the following sites suggest congenital tuberculosis** (NBE)

  • C. Lymph nodes
  • D. Skin
Q152

Most common site of primary infection in congenital tuberculosis is** (NBE)

Q154

Most commonly involved organ in congenital tuberculosis is** (NBE)

  • A. Lymph nodes
  • B. Skin
  • C. Liver
  • D. Lungs
Q155

Most common site for extra-pulmonary tuberculosis is-** (NBE)

  • B. Pleura
  • C. Genitourinary tracts
  • D. Bones
Q156

All of the following statements about Miliary Tuberculosis are true Except:** (NBE)

  • A. May occur following primary infection
  • B. May occur following secondary reactivation
  • C. Sputum microscopy is usually negative
  • D. Montoux is always positive
  • E. Liver, kidney and spleen are common sites of involvement
Q157

The following statement stands true for Mantoux test** (AI 1996)

  • A. Test is read before 48 hrs
  • B. 6-9 mm induration shows maximum chances of developing TB
  • C. A positive test does not indicate that the person is suffering from disease.
  • D. New cases are more likely to occur in tuberculin negative person than those who already are tuberculin
Q158

False-negative tuberculin test is seen in all except:** (AI 1996)

  • A. After 4-6 weeks of measles attack
  • B. Immunodeficiency state
  • C. Miliary tuberculosis
  • D. Atypical mycobacterial infection
Q159

Positive tuberculin test signifies:** (NBE)

  • A. Active tuberculous infection
  • B. Latent tuberculous infection
  • C. BCG Vaccination
Q160

All of the following are true about Boosting in Tuberculin Skin Testing, except** (NBE)

  • A. Spurious TST Conversion in absence of Infection
  • B. Increase in TST induration upon retesting in absence of infection
  • C. Maximum boosting is seen 3 months after initial TST
  • D. All of the above are correct
Q161

Investigations in a clinically suspected case of tuberculosis –** (PGI June 07)

  • A. Mantoux (in children)
  • B. Sputum AFB
  • C. PCR
  • D. Bactec
Q162

A man presents with fever, wt loss and cough; Mantoux reads an induration of 17 x 19 mm; Sputum cytology is negative for AFB. Most likely diagnosis is:** (AI 2001)

  • A. Palm tuberculosis
  • B. Fungal infection
  • C. Viral infection
  • D. Pneumonia
Q163

A 25 year old man presented with fever, cough, expectoration and breathlessness of 2 months duration. Contrast enhanced computed tomography of the chest showed bilateral upper lobe fibrotic lesions and mediastinum had enlarged necrotic nodes with peripheral rim enhancement. Which one of the following is the most probable diagnosis:** (AI 03)

  • C. Lymphoma
  • D. Silicosis
Q164

A 25 year old man presented with fever and cough, expectoration and breathlessness of 2 months is the most probable diagnosis:** (AIIMS May 04)

Q165

Rasmussen's aneurysm arises from** (NBE)

  • A. Bronchial artery
  • B. Pulmonary artery
  • C. Vertebral artery
  • D. Posterior intercostals artery
Q166

Multi drug resistant tuberculosis is defined as resistance to?** (NBE)

  • A. INH and Pyrizinamide
  • B. INH and Rifampicin
  • C. Rifampicin and Pyrizinamide
  • D. Resistance to all first line drugs
Q167

Multidrug-resistant TB (MDR-TB) is defined as disease resistant to:** (NBE)

  • A. INH alone
  • B. Rifampicin alone
  • C. INH +Rifampicin
  • D. INH + Rifampicin + Fluoroquinolones
Q168

Extensively drug-resistant TB (XDR-TB) is defined as disease resistant to:** (NBE)

  • B. INH +Rifampicin
  • C. INH + Rifampicin + Fluoroquinolones
  • D. INH + Rifampicin + Fluoroquinolones + Capreomycin
Q169

Mycobacterium tuberculosis having resistance to Rifampicin, Isoniazid, and Pyrazinamide is BEST described as-** (NBE)

  • A. Total Drug resistant tuberculosis
  • B. Multi drug resistant tuberculosis
  • C. Extremely drug resistant tuberculosis
  • D. Poly-Resistant Tuberculosis
Q170

A young man with tuberculosis presents with massive recurrent hemoptysis. For angiographic treatment which vascular structure should be evaluated first-** (AIIMS May 03)

  • A. Pulmonary artery
  • B. Bronchial artery
  • C. Pulmonary vein
  • D. Superior vena cava
Q171

SARS is caused by the infection with which type of virus:** (NBE)

  • A. Rhinovirus
  • B. Coronavirus
  • C. RSV
  • D. Parainfluenza Virus
Q172

Which of these is not a complication of advanced SARS?** (NBE)

  • A. Muscular atrophy
  • B. Pneumonia
  • C. Heart failure
  • D. Liver failure
Q173

SARS infection case fatality rate of >50% observed in patients of which age group?** (NBE)

  • A. < 20 yrs
  • B. 20 - 49 years
  • C. 40- 60 years
  • D. >65 years
Q174

SARS has resulted in deaths for a majority of people in which of these age groups:** (NBE)

  • A. Newborns
  • B. Kids between the ages of 5-10 years
  • C. Young Adults
  • D. Elderly people
Q175

B/L hilar lymphadenopathy, along with non caseating granulomas is a characteristic feature of:** (AI 1997)

  • B. Scleroderma
  • C. SLE
  • D. Stein-leventhal syndrome
Q176

All are correct regarding sarcoidosis except:** (AIIMS Dec 98)

  • A. Often cavitate
  • B. Spontaneous remission is usual
  • C. Tuberculine test is negative
  • D. B/L hilar lymphadenopathy.
Q177

Which is the most common organ involved in Sarcoidosis:** (NBE)

  • C. CNS
  • D. Eye
Q178

A woman is admitted with complains of low-grade fever of 6 weeks duration. Chest radiograph reveals bihilar adenopathy with clear lung fields. All of the following investigations will be useful in differential diagnosis except :** (AI 2004)

  • A. CD4/CD8 counts in the blood
  • B. Serum ACE levels
  • C. CECT of chest
  • D. Gallium scan
Q179

Kviem Siltzbach test is used in the diagnosis of:** (NBE)

  • C. Histoplasmosis
  • D. Leishmaniasis
Q180

Lambda -Panda sign is typically seen in:** (NBE)

Q181

Garland sign on CXR in sarcoidosis involves all except:** (NBE)

  • A. Right paratracheal nodes
  • B. Right hilar nodes
  • C. Left hilar nodes
  • D. Left pretracheal lymph nodes
Q182

Treatment Of choice in acute Sarcoidosis is:** (NBE)

  • A. Prednisolone
  • B. Cyclosporin
  • D. IV Immunoglobulins
Q183

Hydrostatic Pulmonary edema with fluid in alveoli is usually seen when PCWP is:** (NBE)

  • A. >6 mm Hg
  • B. >10 mm Hg
  • C. >15 mm Hg
  • D. >25 mm Hg
Q184

Normal Pulmonary Capillary Wedge Pressure (PCWP) is:** (NBE)

  • A. 6-12 mm Hg
  • B. 10-14 mm Hg
  • C. 12-18 mm Hg
  • D. 18-25 mm Hg
Q185

Severe pulmonary edema is seen when PCWP rises above?** (NBE)

  • A. 5 mm Hg
  • B. 10 mm Hg
  • C. 15 mm Hg
  • D. 25 mm Hg
Q186

Which of the following is recommended in a patient with Acute Cardiogenic Pulmonary Edema with Systolic BP 80 mm Hg but No signs/symptoms of shock:** (NBE)

  • A. Nitroglycerine
  • B. Norepinephrine
  • D. Dobutamine
Q187

Drug of choice for management of acute pulmonary edema is-** (NBE)

  • B. Hydrochlorothiazide
  • C. Spironolactone
  • D. Triamterene
Q188

Shock Lung is better known as:** (NBE)

  • A. Alveolar Proteinosis
  • B. Alveolar Haemorrhage
  • C. Pulmonary edema
Q189

Shock lung is characterized by which of the following?** (NBE)

  • A. Alveolar proteinosis
  • B. Bronchiolitis obliterans
  • C. Diffuse alveolar damage
  • D. Diffuse pulmonary hemorrhage
Q190

Which of the following is a cause of DIRECT lung injury leading to development of Adult Respiratory Distress Syndrome?** (NBE)

  • A. Sepsis
  • B. Cardiopulmonary bypass
  • C. Multiple transfusions
  • D. Lung transplantation
Q191

Acute lung injury in caused by all of the following except :** (AIIMS May 02)

  • A. Aspiration
  • B. Toxic gas inhalation
  • C. Cardiopulmonary bypass with heart lung machine.
  • D. Lung contusion.
Q192

Normal PCWP with pulmonary oedema is seen in :** (AIIMS June 98)

Q193

Normal PCWP with pulmonary edema is seen in?** (NBE)

  • A. Left atrial Myxoma
  • D. LV systolic dysfunction
Q194

ARDS is associated with** (PGI Dec-04)

  • A. Acute pancreatitis
  • B. Trauma
  • C. Severe Falciparum malaria
Q195

A patient comes with sudden respiratory distress, on examination, bilateral basal crepts are present over chest suggestive of pulmonary edema with normal alveolar wedge pressure. The likely cause is** (AIIMS

Q196

Which of the following is the most characteristic feature of Adult Respiratory Distress Syndrome (ARDS):** (AI 2012)

  • A. Diffuse Alveolar Damage
  • B. Hypoxemia and Hypoxia
  • C. Surfactant Deficiency
  • D. Hypocapnia
Q197

All are seen in ARDS, except:** (AIIMS May 95)

  • A. Pulmonary edema
  • B. Decreased tidal volume.
  • C. Hypercapnia
  • D. Decreased compliance
Q198

Which of the following is not seen in ARDS:** (NBE)

  • B. Hypoxemia
  • C. Stiff lung
  • D. Hypercapnia
Q199

All of the following features can seen in ARDS except:** (NBE)

  • A. Pulmonary Shunting
  • B. Reduced compliance
  • C. Hypoxemia
Q200

Which of the following is not seen in ARDS:** (AI 1996)

Q201

Which of the following is not seen in ARDS** (NBE)

  • B. Hypercapnia
  • D. Stiff lung
Q202

In ARDS, not true is –** (PGI June 99)

  • A. Decreased pulmonary compliance
  • B. Increased pulmonary artery pressure
  • C. Increased left atrial pressure
  • D. Severe hypoxemia
Q203

Feature of shock lung is-** (AIIMS Nov 07)

  • A. Diffuse alveolar damage
  • B. Usual interstitial penumonitis
Q204

All of the following define Severe ARDS according to the Berlin Definition of ARDS** (NBE)

  • A. PaO₂/FiO₂ < 200
  • B. Acute onset< 1 Week
  • C. Bilateral Alveolar opacities
  • D. Non-Cardiogenic Pulmonary edema
Q205

Which of the following defines ARDS based on Berlin criteria** (JIPMER 2018)

  • A. PaO₂ /FiO₂ Less than 200 mm Hg
  • B. PaO₂ /FiO₂ less than 300 mm Hg
  • C. PaO₂ /FiO₂ More than 200 mm Hg
  • D. PaO₂ /FiO₂ More than 300 mm Hg
Q206

Acute Lung Injury (ALI) according to AECC criteria is characterized by all except** (NBE)

  • A. PaO₂/FiO₂ < 200mm Hg.
  • B. Bilateral interstitial infiltrates
  • C. PCWP <18 mm Hg
  • D. Normal Left atrial pressure
Q207

Acute Lung injury is characterized by all, Except:** (PGI Dec 04)

  • A. Alveolar infiltrates
  • C. Pulmonary shunting
  • D. PaO₂/FiO₂ <200mm of Hg
  • E. None of the above
Q208

A patient with fracture pelvis is admitted in ICU after surgery. Post operatively he develops sudden dyspnea and chest pain. The likely cause is :** (AIIMS Nov 99)

  • A. Pulmonary thromboembolism
  • B. Shock
  • C. Respiratory infection
Q209

All are true about pulmonary embolism, except :** (NBE)

  • A. Chest pain is the most common symptom
  • B. Most commonly presents within 2 weeks
  • C. More is the survival time, more is the chance of recovery
  • D. Arises from leg veins
Q210

A 60 year old woman with fracture neck of femur presents to the emergency department a few days after her injury with sudden onset breathlessness and chest pain. The most likely diagnosis is:** (NBE)

  • A. Myocardial Infarction
  • B. Deep Vein Thrombosis
  • C. Pulmonary Embolism
  • D. Pleuritis
Q211

The most common cause of preventable Hospital Death is** (NBE)

  • A. Acute Pulmonary Embolism
  • B. Heart Failure
  • C. Myocardial Infarction
  • D. Cancer
Q212

The most common cause of Pulmonary thromboembolism** (AIIMS May 95)

  • A. DIC
  • B. Coagulation Disorder
  • C. DVT
  • D. Venous Hypertension
Q213

The most common source of pulmonary embolism is:** (AI 2003)

  • A. Amniotic fluid embolism
  • B. Calf vein thrombi
  • C. Large veins of leg
  • D. Cardio thoracic surgery
Q214

All of the following conditions may predispose to pulmonary embolism except:** (AI 2003)

  • A. Protein S deficiency
  • B. Malignancy
  • C. Obesity
  • D. Progesterone therapy
Q215

Pulmonary infarction typically indicates** (NBE)

  • A. Small PE
  • B. Submassive PE
  • C. Massive PE
Q216

In acute pulmonary embolism, the most frequent ECG finding is :** (AIIMS May 06)

  • A. S1Q3T3 pattern
  • B. P. pulmonale
  • C. Sinus tachycardia
  • D. Right axis deviation
Q217

Which of the following is the most common abnormality in ECG manifestation of pulmonary embolism?** (NBE)

  • A. T wave inversion V1 to V4
  • B. Sinus bradycardia
  • C. U wave
  • D. S1Q3T3 pattern
Q218

D-Dimer values may be increased in all of the following Except:** (NBE)

  • C. Anticoagulant therapy
  • D. Pregnancy
Q219

D-dimer is the most sensitive diagnostic test for-** (AIIMS May 05)

  • B. Acute pulmonary oedema
  • D. Acute myocardial infarction
Q220

D-Dimer is the most sensitive test for** (NBE)

  • A. DVT
  • C. Acute Pulmonary adema
Q221

Which of the following should be the initial investigation in a patient with low clinical likelihood of Pulmonary Embolism** (NBE)

  • A. D-Dimer
  • B. Chest CT
Q222

Which of the following should be the initial investigation in a patient with high clinical likelihood of

  • A. Pulmonary Angiography
  • B. V/Q Lung Scan
  • C. Chest CT
  • D. Pulmonary Angiography
Q223

A 55 year old man who has been on bed rest for the past 10 days, complains of breathlessness and chest pain. The chest X-ray is normal. The next investigation should be:** (AI 2003) (AI 2004)

  • A. Lung ventilation-perfusion scan
  • B. Pulmonary arteriography
  • C. Pulmonary venous angiography
  • D. Echocardiography
Q224

The most definitive method of diagnosing pulmonary embolism is :** (AIIMS Nov. 05)

  • A. Pulmonary aterigraphy
  • B. Radioisotope perfusion pulmonary scintigraphy
  • C. EKG
  • D. Venography
Q225

The treatment of choice in a patient with Massive Pulmonary Embolism in Shock is:** (NBE)

  • A. Thrombolytic Therapy
  • B. Low Molecular Weight Heparin
  • C. Surgical Embolectomy
  • D. Aggressive Fluid Resuscitation
Q226

A young healthy patient presents to the Emergency with Acute pulmonary embolism. Patient's blood pressure is normal but echocardiography reveals Right ventricular hypokinesia and compromised cardiac output. The treatment of choice in this patient is:** (AIIMS Nov 2001)

  • A. Thrombolytic therapy
  • B. Anticoagulation with low molecular weight heparin
  • C. Anticoagulation with warfarin
  • D. Inferior vena cava filters
Q227

Hypokinesia of Right Ventricular Free Wall on Transthoracic Echocardiography is known as** (NBE)

  • A. McConnell's sign
  • B. Westermark's sign
  • C. Hampton's hump
  • D. Palla's sign
Q228

Pulmonary Hypertension is defined as Pulmonary Artery Pressure:** (NBE)

  • A. ≥15mm Hg
  • B. ≥18mmHg
  • C. ≥20mmHg
  • D. ≥25mmHg
Q229

Pre-capillary Pulmonary Hypertension is defined as:** (NBE)

  • A. PAP ≥25 mm Hg; PCWP >15mmHg
  • B. PAP ≥25mmHg; PCWP ≤15mm Hg
  • C. PCWP ≥25mmHg; PAP >15mmHg
  • D. PCWP ≥25mmHg; PAP ≤15mmHg
Q230

All of the following are signs of Pulmonary Hypertension, except:** (NBE)

  • A. Right Parasternal Heave
  • B. Raised JVP
  • C. Palpable P2
  • D. Low Volume Pulse
Q231

In primary pulmonary hypertension basic abnormality in gene lies in:** (AIIMS May 07)

  • A. Bone morphogenic protein receptor II
Q232

Not seen in pre capillary pulmonary hypertension:** (PGI June 98)

  • D. ↑ Pulmonary wedge pressure
Q233

Precapillary Pulmonary hypertension is caused by all Except:** (PGI June 03)

Q234

All are causes of pulmonary hypertension except-** (AIIMS Nov 07)

Q235

Pulmonary hypertension may occur in all of the following conditions except-** (AIIMS Nov 06)

Q236

Pulmonary hypertension in COPD is due to all, Except:** (PGI Dec 97)

  • B. Pulmonary vasoconstriction
  • C. High lung volume
  • D. Bronchoconstriction
Q237

Iloprost in Pulmonary Arterial Hypertension is administered:** (NBE)

  • A. Intravenous
  • B. Intramuscular
  • C. Oral
  • D. Inhalation
Q238

A 29 year old unmarried female presents with progressive dyspnea. Her X ray chest shows clear lung fields. Pulmonary function testing reveals an FVC of 92%; FEV1/FVC of 89%; and DLCO of 59%. On exercise testing her oxygen saturation drops from 92% to 86%. What is the likely diagnosis:** (AIIMS Nov 2008)

  • A. Alveolar hypoventilation
  • D. Anxiety
Q239

A 29 year old anxious lady presents with a history of progressive breathlessness and exercise intolerance since four months. Her FVC is 90% and FEV1 / FVC is 86%. Oxygen saturation after exercise was observed to drop from 92% to 86%. What is the likely diagnosis :** (AI 2010)

  • A. Primary alveolar hypoventilation
  • C. Anxiety disorder
  • D. Interstitial lung disease
Q240

Chronic Cor pulmonale is seen in all except:** (PGI-Dec-04)

  • A. Pulmonary embolization
  • D. Primary pulmonary hypertension
Q241

The most common cause for Chronic cor pulmonale is** (PGI Dec 04)

  • A. Recurrent pulmonary embolization
  • D. Bronchial Asthma
Q242

The most common cause of acute cor pulmonale is:** (AIIMS Nov 02)

  • A. Pneumonia.
  • B. Pulmonary thromboembolism.
  • C. Chronic obstructive pulmonary disease.
  • D. Primary spontaneous pneumothorax.
Q243

All the following are radiological features of Chronic Cor pulmonale except-** (NBE)

Q244

Which one of the following conditions may lead to exudative pleural effusion** (AI 2003)

  • A. Cirrhosis
  • B. Nephrotic syndrome
  • D. Bronchogenic carcinoma
Q245

All of the following are Light's criteria for exudative pleural effusion:** (NBE)

  • A. Pleural fluid protein to serum protein > 0.5
  • B. Pleural fluid LDH to serum LDH > 0.6
  • C. Pleural fluid LDH is > 2/3 times the normal upper limit for serum
  • D. Pleural fluid ADA to Serum ADA <16
Q246

Which of the following is NOT a feature of an exudate?** (NBE)

  • A. Proteins< 3gm
  • B. Cells> 1000/ml
  • C. Cloudy appearance
  • D. Cells predominantly are neutrophils
Q247

All of the following are features of Exudative effusions, Except:** (PGI-June-02)

  • A. Serum : Pleural Albumin ratio < 1.2mg/dl
  • B. Pleural fluid cholesterol > 55
  • C. Fluid:Serum Protein ratio > 0.5
  • D. Fluid : Serum Protein ratio >0.5
Q248

All of the following are causes of Transudative pleural effusion Except:** (PGI- June-02)

  • A. Nephrotic syndrome
  • B. Rheumatoid arthritis
  • D. Myxedema
  • E. Pulmonary embolism
Q249

Left-sided pleural effusion seen in** (NBE)

  • A. Pancreatitis
  • C. Hypoproteinemia
  • D. CCF
  • E. Esophageal rupture
Q250

All of the following are true about pleural effusion, Except:** (select two options) (NBE)

  • A. Horizontal fluid level
  • B. Decreased lung volume
  • C. Decreased chest movement
  • D. Succussion splash
Q251

Tuberculous pleural effusion is characterised by all of the following features except diffusion:** (AI

  • A. Harmorrhagic effusion
  • B. Pleural fluid LDH more than 60% that of serum LDH.
  • C. Increased deaminase
  • D. Increased mesothelial cells
Q252

Pleural fluid having low glucose is seen in all, except-** (AIIMS May 93)

  • B. Empyema
  • C. Mesothelioma
  • D. Rheumatoid arthritis
Q253

All of the following show low glucose in pleural fluid, EXCEPT-** (AIIMS Dec 94)

  • B. Malignant pleural effusion
  • C. Rheumatoid arthritis
  • D. Dressler's syndrome
Q254

Low glucose in pleural effusion is seen in all, except** (AIIMS May 94)

  • A. Dressler syndrome
  • C. Malignant pleural effusion
Q255

Decreased Glucose in pleural effusion is seen in: (select best two correct options)** (PGI June 99)

  • A. Rheumatoid arthritis
  • B. SLE
  • C. Pneumococcal infection
  • D. P. carinii infection
Q256

A high amylase level in pleural fluid suggests a diagnosis of** (AIIMS May 03)

  • D. Esophageal perforation
Q257

Amylase increased in pleural fluid is seen in following except:** (AIIMS June 97)

  • C. Gall stone pancreatitis
Q258

Presence of cholesterol crystal in pleural effusion is a feature of:** (PGI June 2000)

  • A. Myxoedema
  • B. Hypertriglyceridemia
  • C. Hyperthyroidism
Q259

In an adult patient with plural effusion, the most appropriate site for pluriesis done by inserting a needle is in:** (AI 2002)

  • A. 5th intercostals space in midclavicular line
  • B. 7th intercostals space in mid axillary's bone
  • C. 2nd intercostals space adjacent to the sternum
  • D. 10th intercostal space adjacent to the vertebral column
Q260

Pneumothorax is seen in all, except-** (AIIMS May 94)

  • A. Marfan syndrome
  • B. Assisted ventilation
  • C. Eosinophilic granuloma
  • D. Bronchopulmonary Aspergillosis
Q261

While inserting a central venous catheter, a patient develops respiratory distress. The most likely cause is:** (AI 2002)

  • A. Hemothorax
  • B. Pneumothorax
  • D. Hypovolumia
Q262

All of the following are characteristic features of primary spontaneous penumothorax Except** (AIIMS 92)

  • A. Male gender
  • B. Old age
  • C. Tall stature
  • D. History of smoking
Q263

Spontaneous pneumothorax is commonly seen in:** (PGI June 02)

  • A. Smokers
  • B. Young females
  • C. Old age
  • D. Short statureed men
Q264

Which of the following is true about Pneumothorax?** (NBE)

  • A. Decreased Chest Expansion
  • B. Increased Vocal Resonance
  • C. Increased Breath Sounds
  • D. Dull percussion note
Q265

Which of the following statements about pneumothorax is true:** (AIIMS Dec 94)

  • A. Breath sounds are increased
  • B. Percussion note is decreased
  • C. Always needs chest tube insertion
  • D. Often needs chest tube insertion
Q266

A 34 year old male with chest trauma presents with sudden onset respiratory distress which is increasing. Hyper-resonant note is observed on the left side. Which of the following is the most appropriate immediate emergent management?** (NBE)

  • A. Needle puncture and emergent decompression
  • B. Tube insertion
  • C. Antibiotic and oxygen
  • D. No treatment needed.
Q267

A 30 year old female comes acute breathlessness, neck vein distention, and absent breath sounds and mediastinal shift. Which of the following should be done immediately?** (PGI June 2008)

  • A. HRCT is the investigation of choice
  • B. ABG analysis should be done immediately?
  • C. CXR
  • D. Large bore needle puncture of pleura
Q268

A patient with history of chest trauma presents to the emergency department in severe respiratory distress with a BP of 90/70 mm Hg and a Pulse rate of 120/min. The most likely diagnosis is:** (NBE)

  • A. Simple Pneumothorax
  • B. Hemothorax
  • D. Tension Pneumothorax
Q269

A patient with history of chest trauma presents to the emergency department with a BP of 90/70 mm Hg and a Pulse rate of 120/min. The patient is observed to be short of breath using his accessory muscle of respiration. The JVP is not elevated. The most likely diagnosis is:** (NBE)

  • A. Tension Pneumothorax
  • B. Massive Hemothorax
Q270

In left sided massive pneumothorax, ECG shows all, except:** (AIIMS May 94)

  • C. Peaked P wave
Q271

IPPV can cause all of the following except:** (AI 2001)

  • A. Barotrauma
  • C. Missing
Q272

"Sleep apnea", is defined as a temporary pause in breathing during sleep lasting at least-** (AIIMS May

  • A. 40 seconds
  • B. 30 seconds
  • D. 10 seconds
Q273

Obstructive Sleep Apnea (OSA) syndrome is defined by daytime sleepiness and an Apnoea-Hypopnea index (AHI):** (NBE)

  • A. >5
  • B. >15
  • C. >30
  • D. >40
Q274

Duration of apnea in obstructive sleep apnea syndrome is:** (NBE)

  • A. >10 sec
  • B. >20 sec
  • C. >30 sec
  • D. >60 sec
Q275

The Epworth scale is used for assessing:** (NBE)

  • A. Body mass index
  • B. Vital capacity in post-operative patients
  • C. Sleep apnea
  • D. Risk of embolism in perioperative patient
Q276

True about primary pulmonary hypoventilation is:** (NBE)

  • A. Does not respond to chemical stimuli
  • B. Hypocapnoea and normal PaO₂
  • C. Common in children
  • D. Respiratory alkalosis is diagnostic
Q277

All are true about bilateral diaphragmatic paralysis, except:** (AIIMS Feb 97)

  • A. Sniff test is positive
  • B. Causes normocapnic failure
  • C. Diaphragmatic pacing is useful if any nerve is intact
Q278

Most common cause of Mediastinitis is:** (AIIMS 97)

  • A. Tracheal rupture
  • B. Esophageal rupture
  • C. Drugs
  • D. Idiopathic
Q279

Most common bronchogenic carcinoma is** (AIIMS June 2000)

  • A. Small cell carcinoma
  • B. Squamous cell carcinoma
  • C. Mixed cell carcinoma
  • D. Adenocarcinoma
Q280

Most common type of carcinoma lung is-** (AIIMS May 93)

  • B. Adenocarcinoma
Q281

All of the following are true regarding oat cell carcinoma of lung, except:** (AIIMS June 99)

  • A. Variant of large cell anaplastic CA
  • B. Chemotherapy is effective
  • C. Paraneoplastic syndrome may be present
  • D. Causes SIADH
Q282

All of the following statements about small cell carcinomas are true, Except** (PGI-June-06)

  • A. Commonest Malignancy of lung
  • B. Associated with parancoplastic syndrome
  • C. Cause SVC obstruction
  • D. Chemosensitive
Q283

Which of the following statements about small cell carcinoma is true?** (AI 2009)

  • A. Bone metastasis is uncommon
  • B. Peripheral in location
  • C. Chemosensitive tumor
  • D. Paraneoplastic syndrome with ↑PTH is common
Q284

All of the following statements about Non Small Cell Carcinoma of Lung (NSCLC) are true, Except:** (AI 2012)

  • A. Contralateral mediastinal nodes are a contraindication to surgical resection
  • B. Single Agent Chemotherapy is preferred for patient > 70 years with advanced disease
  • C. Squamous Cell Carcinoma is the most common NSCLCL amongst Asian population
  • D. Gefitinib is most effective for female smokers with adenocarcinoma on histology
Q285

In a chronic smoker, a highly malignant aggressive and metastatic lung carcinoma is :** (AIIMS May 01)

  • A. Squamous cell Carcinoma
  • B. Small cell Carcinoma
  • C. Adenocarcinoma
  • D. Large cell carcinoma
Q286

Following hormonal levels are increased in small cell carcinoma of lung except :** (AI 1997)

  • A. ACTH
  • B. Growth hormone
  • C. ANF
  • D. AVP
Q287

Type of lung carcinoma producing superior vena cava syndrome:** (AIIMS June 97)

  • A. Squamous cell carcinoma
  • C. Small cell carcinoma
  • D. Anaplastic carcinoma
Q288

Carcinoma lung responding best to chemotherapy:** (NBE)

  • A. Squamous cell type
  • B. Oat cell type
  • D. All respond equally

Clubbing is least common in:** (AIIMS Dec 97)

Q290

A 60 year old man presents with non productive coughing and haemoptysis for 4 weeks; He has grade III clubbing, and a lesion in the apical lobe on x ray. Most likely diagnosis here is :** (AI 2001) (AIIMS June

  • A. Small cell ca
  • B. Non small cell ca
  • C. Fungal infection
  • D. Tuberculosis
Q291

What is TRUE regarding adenocarcinoma lung** (AIIMS Dec 94)

  • A. Causes 50% of lung cancers
  • B. Unlikely histological variant in young patients
  • C. Associated with subcutaneous angiomyolipoma
  • D. Peripheral location
Q292

True statement about adenocarcinoma lung are (Choose two best options):** (PGI June 05)

  • A. Common in females
  • B. Not associated with smoking
  • C. Central cavitation is a characteristic feature
  • D. Peripheral involvement is common
  • E. Upper lobe involvement is common
Q293

Commonest type of lung carcinoma in nonsmokers is** (AIIMS Dec 94)

  • C. Alveolar cell carcinoma
  • D. Small cell carcinoma
Q294

Lung to lung metastasis is seen in-** (NBE)

  • A. Adenocarcinoma of lung
  • D. Neuroendocrine tumor of lung
Q295

Which of the following statements about lung carcinoma is true** (AI 2010)

  • A. Squamous cell variant accounts for 70% of all lung cancers
  • B. Oat cell variant typically present with cavitation
  • C. Oat cell variant is typically associated with hilar adenopathy
  • D. Adenocarcinoma variant is typically central in location

Most common symptom of lung carcinoma** (AIIMS 90)

  • A. Cough
  • B. Dyspnoea
  • C. Weight loss
  • D. Chest pain
Q297

The commonest intrabronchial cause of haemoptysis is** (AIIMS May 95)

  • A. Carcinoma lung
  • B. Adenoma lung
Q298

A 60 years old chronic smoker presents with complaints of hemoptysis. Her chest X-ray appears to be normal. What is the next best investigation:** (AIIMS Nov. 2000)

  • A. Bronchoscopy
  • B. High resolution CT
Q299

A 60 yr old man is suspected of having endobronchial carcinoma; What should be the best investigation :** (AI 2001)

  • A. CT guided FNAC
  • B. Bronchoscopy and biopsy
  • C. Sputum cytology
  • D. X-Ray chest.
Q300

Serum ACE may be raised in all of the following except:** (AI 2005)

  • B. Silicosis
  • C. Berylliosis
Q301

Ramesh 40 yrs male patient presenting with polyuria, pain abdomen, nausea, vomiting, altered sensorium was found to have bronchogenic carcinoma. The electrolyte abnormality seen in him would be:** (AIIMS May 02)

  • B. Hyperkalemia
Q302

A 60 year old male presented to the emergency with breathlessness, facial swelling and dilated veins on the chest wall. The most common cause is:** (AI 2003)

  • A. Thymoma
  • C. Hodgkin's lymphoma
  • D. Superior vena caval obstruction
Q303

Which of the following tumor is most commonly associated with superior vena cava syndrome:** (AI 2011)

  • A. Lymphoma
  • B. Small cell carcinoma
  • C. Non small cell carcinoma
  • D. Metastasis
Q304

Which of the following statements about Cystic Fibrosis is true** (NBE)

  • A. X-Linked Recessive Inheritance
  • B. Associated with gene defect in Chromosome 9
  • C. Most common mutation is deletion of F508
  • D. Characterized by defect in Sodium Channel
Q305

Which of the following systems is least likely to be affected in cystic fibrosis** (NBE)

  • A. Respiratory
  • B. Genitourinary
  • C. Hepatobiliary
  • D. Endocrine
Q306

Which of the following is associated with cystic fibrosis ?** (NBE)

  • A. Impaired fertility due to immobile sperm
  • B. Steatorrhea
  • C. Dextrocardia
  • D. Situs Inversus
Q307

The most common cause of pulmonary infection in cystic fibrosis is** (NBE)

  • A. Pseudomonas Aeroginosa
  • B. Staphylococcus Aureus
  • C. Bukholderia cenocapacia
  • D. Nontuberculous mycobacteria
Q308

Most common pathogen causing pulmonary infection in cystic fibrosis in infants & young children is

  • B. Pseudomonas Aeroginosa
  • C. Bukholderia Cenocapacia
  • D. Nontubercular mycobacteria
Q309

The best way to make a diagnosis of cystic fibrosis in a patient suspected of having this disorder is

  • A. Sweat chloride test
  • B. Pulmonary function testing
  • C. Stool for fetal fat content
  • D. DNA analysis
Q310

An infant has a positive newborn screening test for cystic fibrosis. What cut off of sweat chloride strongly suggests cystic fibrosis:** (NBE)

  • A. Sweat Chloride >30
  • B. Sweat Chloride >40
  • C. Sweat Chloride >50
  • D. Sweat Chloride >60
Q311

Finger in glove sign results from:** (NBE)

  • C. Tuberculosis
  • D. Bronchocele
Q312

Bronchial adenoma commonly present as :** (NBE)

  • A. Recurrent hemoptysis
  • B. Cough
  • C. Dyspnoea

Which of the following statements about Bronchial cysts is true:** (AI 1993)

  • A. 50-70% are seen in lungs
  • B. Most common site is mediastinum
  • C. They are premalignant lesions
  • D. Infection is uncommon in Pulmonary bronchogenic cysts

All of the following are true about Kartagener's syndrome, Except** (PGI-Dec-04)

  • A. Dextrocardia
  • B. Infertility
  • C. Mental retardation
Q315

Which of the following manifestations is typical of Kartagener's syndrome?** (NBE)

  • A. Intestinal obstruction
  • B. Dextrocardia
  • C. Steatorrhea
  • D. Infertility

Kartagener's syndrome includes all of the following, Except:** (PGI June 01)

  • A. Situs inversus
  • C. Sinusitis
  • D. Cystic fibrosis
Q317

A patient presents with hemoptysis and hematuria few weeks after a respiratory tract infection. ANCA antibodies are present. Likely diagnosis is:** (AI 1995)

  • A. Goodpasture's syndrome
  • B. IgA Nephropathy
  • D. PSGN
Q318

Rama, a 20 year old smoker, presents with hemoptysis and hematuria Likely diagnosis is:** (AI 1997)

  • A. Good pasture's syndrome
  • C. Gullian Barre syndrome
  • D. IgA Nephropathy
Q319

A male born at term after an uncomplicated pregnancy, labor and delivery develops severe respiratory distress within a few hours of birth. Results of routine culture were negative. The chest roentogram reveals a normal heart shadow and fine reticulonodular infiltrates radiating from the hilum. ECHO findings reveal no abnormality. Family history reveals similar clinical course and death of a male and female sibling at 1 month and 2 months of age respectively. The most likely diagnosis is:** (AI 2008)

  • A. Neonatal Alveolar Proteinosis
  • B. Total Anomalous Pulmonary Venous Circulation (TAPVC)
  • C. Meconium Aspiration Syndrome
  • D. Diffuse Herpes simplex infection
Q320

3.5 kg term male baby, born of uncomplicated pregnancy, developed respiratory distress at birth, not responded to surfactant, ECHO finding revealed nothing abnormal, X-ray showed ground glass appearance and culture negative. Apgars 4 and 5 at 1 and 5 minutes. History of one month female sibling died before. What is the diagnosis?** (AIIMS Nov 07)

  • A. TAPVC
  • B. Meconium aspiration
  • C. Neonatal pulmonary alveolar proteinosis
  • D. Diffuse herpes simplex infection
Q321

Which of the following is not a feature of consolidation** (NBE)

  • A. Increased vocal resonance
  • B. Dull Percussion note
  • C. Bronchial Breath sounds
  • D. Tracheal shift to side of consolidation
Q322

A patient presents with sudden onset of breathlessness after a subclavian vein cannulation. On examination, breath sounds are absent while the chest is hyper-resonant on percussion on one side. Most likely cause is:** (PGI June 2000)

  • B. Subclavian Vein air embolus
  • C. Malposition of cannula
  • D. Cardiac Arrhythmia
Q323

Homogenous opacification of right hemithorax with right sided shift of mediastinum may be caused by:** (PGI June 97)

  • A. Collapse of Right lung
  • B. Consolidation of Right lung
  • C. Right sided hemothorax
  • D. Left sided hydrothorax
Q324

Cavernous Respiration is seen in:** (NBE)

  • A. Cavity
  • B. Consolidation
  • C. Fibrosis
  • D. Interstitial Inflammation
Q325

Bilateral Rhonchi may be seen in all of the following Except:** (PGI June 08)

  • A. Pulmonary Edema
  • B. Bronchiectasis
  • C. Pulmonary Embolism
  • D. Emphysema
Q326

Presence of Velcro crackles at the lung base on auscultation is a sign of:** (NBE)

  • A. Pulmonary Fibrosis
  • B. Bronchial Asthma
  • C. Bronchiectasis
  • D. Pneumonia
Q327

All of the following are true about Velcro Crackles, except:** (NBE)

  • A. Also called Cellophane crackles
  • B. High Pitched, low amplitude sounds
  • C. Best heard in lung bases
  • D. Not changed by body position

Inspiratory Squeaks are typical of:** (NBE)

  • A. Bronchial Asthma
  • B. Pulmonary Fibrosis
  • C. Bronchiolitis
  • D. Bronchiectasis

The most common site of bleeding in Hemoptysis is:** (NBE)

  • A. Tracheobronchial tree
  • B. Pulmonary Parenchyma
  • C. Pleural Disease
  • D. All of the above

The artery most frequently responsible for bleeding in massive hemoptysis is:** (NBE)

  • A. Bronchial Artery
  • B. Pulmonary Artery
  • C. Intersegmental Artery
  • D. Intercostal Artery
Q331

Pulmonary Apoplexy is seen in:** (NBE)

  • A. Mitral Stenosis
  • B. Mitral Regurgitation
  • C. Aortic Stenosis
  • D. Aortic regurgitation

All of the following statements about central cyanosis are true Except:** (NBE)

  • A. Central cyanosis becomes evident when reduced haemoglobin < 5g/dl
  • B. Chronic Asthma may cause central cyanosis
  • C. Alveolar Hypoventilation causes central cyanosis
  • D. Methemoglobinemia causes central cyanosis
Q333

Cause of central cyanosis include all of the following, Except:** (NBE)

  • A. Chronic Asthma
  • B. Congenital Pulmonary stenosis
  • C. Congestive heart failure
  • D. Alveolar hypoventilation
Q334

Cyanosis NOT improving with 100% oxygen in case of:** (AIIMS May 93)

  • A. Cardiac Asthma
  • B. Interstitial lung disease
  • C. Bronchial asthma
  • D. Tetralogy of Fallot (TOF)

One of the following condition is not associated with clubbing:** (AIIMS Dec 98)

  • A. Primary biliary cirrhosis
  • B. Chronic bronchitis
  • C. Cryptogenic fibrosing alveolitis
  • D. Central bronchiectasis
Q336

Causes of unilateral clubbing include all of the following, Except:** (PGI June 05)

  • A. Congenital cyanotic heart disease
  • B. Pancoast tumor
  • C. Aortic Aneurysm
  • D. Brachial AV fistulas
Q337

Multiple episodes of acute chest syndrome are associated with:** (NBE)

  • A. Asthma
  • C. Sickle cell Disease
  • D. Sjogren's syndrome
Q338

Platypnea may be seen in all of the following conditions, except:** (PGI June 05)

  • A. Bilateral Diaphragmatic palsy
  • B. Pleural effusion
  • C. Pulmonary embolism
  • D. Cirrhosis
  • E. COPD
Q339

A child develops acute respiratory distress, stridor, hyperinflation on one side of chest with decreased breath sound on that side. Most likely cause is:** (AIIMS June 2000)

  • B. Aspiration pneumonia
  • C. Foreign body aspiration
  • D. Pleural effusion
🧠

Neurology

471 Questions

▼
Q1

A 45-year-old man presents with a daily headache. He describes two attacks per day over the past 3 weeks. Each attack lasts about an hour and awakens the patient from sleep. The patient has noted associated tearing and reddening of his right eye as well as nasal stuffiness. The pain is deep, excruciating, and limited to the right side of the head. The neurologic examination is nonfocal. The most likely diagnosis of this patient's headache is:** *(AIIMS Nov 99)

  • A. Migraine headache
  • B. Cluster headache
  • C. Tension headache
  • D. Brain tumour
Q2

Cluster headache is characterized by all, except:** *(AI 2005)

  • A. Affects predominantly females.
  • B. Unilateral headache.
  • C. Onset typically in 20-50 years of life.
  • D. Associated with conjunctival congestion.
Q3

A female has episodic, recurrent headache in left hemicranium with nausea and paresthesia on right upper and lower limbs is most probably suffering from:** *(AIIMS June 2000)

  • A. Migraine
  • B. Glossopharyngeal neuralgia
  • C. Herpes zoster infection of trigeminal Nerve
Q4

A female aged 30, presents with episodic throbbing headache for past 4 yrs. It usually involves one half of the face and is associated with nausea and vomiting. There is no aura. Most likely diagnosis is:** *(AI 2001)

  • C. Angle closure glaucoma
  • D. Temporal arteritis
Q5

A woman complains of headache associated with paresthesias of the right upper and lower limb: likely diagnosis is:** *(AI 2001)

  • A. Trigeminal neuralgia
  • C. Migraine
  • D. Cluster headache
Q6

Ophthalmoplegic migraine means:** *(AIIMS May 03)

  • A. When headache is followed by complete paralysis of the IIIrd and VI nerve on the same side as the
  • B. When the headache is followed by partial paralysis of the IIIrd nerve on the same side as the hemicrania
  • C. Headache associated with IIIrd, IVth and VIth nerve paralysis.
  • D. Headache associated with optic neuritis
Q7

A woman has bilateral headache that worsens with emotional stress; she has two children, both doing badly in school; diagnosis is:** *(AI 2001)

  • D. Trigeminal neuralgia
Q8

A 64 year old lady Kamla complains of severe unilateral headache on the right side and blindness for 2 days. On examination there is a thick cord like structure on the lateral side of the head. The ESR is 80 mm/hr in the first hour. The most likely diagnosis is:** *(AIIMS May 01)

  • A. Temporal arteritis
  • B. Migraine
  • C. Cluster headache
  • D. Sinusitis
Q9

A 35 year old Lady Malti has unilateral headache, nausea, vomiting and visual blurring. The diagnosis is: *(AIIMS June 99)

  • A. Cluster headache
  • B. Glaucoma
  • C. Subarachnoid haemorrhage
  • D. Posterior fossa cyst.
Q10

A young girl presents with repeated episodes of throbbing occipital headache associated with ataxia and vertigo. The family history is positive for similar headaches in her mother. Most likely diagnosis is:** *(AI 2011)

  • A. Vestibular Neuronitis
  • B. Basilar migraine
  • D. Tension headache
Q11

All of the following statements about treatment of migraine are true, Except:** *(NBE)

  • B. Rizatriptan is more efficacious than sumatriptan
  • C. Sumatriptan is a selective 5-HT 1B/ID agonist
  • D. Sumatriptan is used for chronic migraine

  • A. Methysergide
  • B. Caffeine
  • C. Amitriptyline
  • D. Sumatriptan

All of the following agents are used for prophylaxis of migraine, Except:** *(AI 2010)

  • B. Valproate
  • C. Topiramate
  • D. Ethosuximide

A 52 years old male patient presented with multiple episodes of unilateral short accompanying conjunctival congestion headache lacrimation. Which of the following is the diagnosis of this patient?** *(NBE)

  • B. Primary stabbing headache

UMN lesion is characterised by:** *(AI 1999)

  • B. Fasciculations

  • D. Localised muscle atrophy

  • B. Spastic paralysis
  • C. Denervation potential in EMG
  • D. Exaggeration of tendon reflexes

Fasciculations are seen in:** *(AI 1995, 96)

  • B. C4-C5
  • C. T1-T2
  • D. T3-T4
