A child with Acute myeloid Leukemia presents with Hyperleukocytosis. Treatment includes all of the following, Except: *(NBE)

  • A. IV fluids
  • B. Allopurinol
  • C. Alkalinization
  • D. Immediately start induction chemotherapy
Q327

Poor prognosis in AML is indicated by: *(AI 2008)

  • A. Inversion 16
  • B. Translocation 15/17 (t15; 17)
  • C. Normal cytogenecity
  • D. Monosomy 7
Q328

AML with worst prognosis is : *(AIIMS Nov 2006)

  • A. 8;21 translocation
  • B. Inversion 16
  • C. Normal Cytogenetics
Q329

In PML, all of the following are seen except : *(AI 2007)

  • A. Retinoic acid is used in treatment
  • B. 15/17 translocation
  • C. CD 15/34 both seen in same cell
  • D. Associated with DIVC
Q330

A 15-years-old boy presented with one day history of bleeding gum, subconjunctival bleed and purpuric rash. Investigations revealed followed results:Hb- 6.4 gm/dl; TLC-26, 500/mm3 platelet 35,000 mm3; prothrombin time-20 sec with a control of 13 sec; partial thromboplastin time-50sec; and Fibrinogen 10mg/dL.Peripheral smear was suggestive of acute myeloblastic leukemia. Which of the following is the most likely? *(AIIMS May 2006)

  • A. Myeloblastic leukemia without maturation
  • B. Myeloblastic leukemia with maturation
  • C. Promyelocytic leukemia
  • D. Myelomonocytic leukemia
Q331

Arsenic is used in treatment of: *(AIIMS May 07)

  • A. Acute promyelocytic leukemia
  • B. A.L.L
  • C. CML
  • D. Transient myeloproliferative disorder
Q332

All the following are seen in Multiple myeloma except *(NBE)

  • A. Visual Disturbance
  • B. Bleeding tendency
  • C. Proteinuria
  • D. Dystrophic calcification
Q333

All of the following are seen in multiple myeloma except: *(NBE)

  • C. Bleeding tendency
Q334

Which of the following is the least common feature of Multiple Myeloma: *(AI 2012)

  • A. Bone pan
  • B. Normocytic Normochromic Anemia
  • C. Susceptibility to bacterial infection
  • D. Hyperviscosity syndrome
Q335

What percentage of Multiple myeloma patients have bonepain? *(NBE)

  • D. 70%
Q336

All of the following statements about Bone involvement in Multiple Myeloma is true, except: Bone pain may be seen in 70 percent of patients Most common site of involvement is Vertebra Most common vertebra involved is the Lumbar Vertebra Long bone involvement below the elbow and the knee is rare

Q337

Which of the following conditions is least likely to be associated with lymphadenopathy *(NBE) Follicular lymphoma Castleman disease Waldenström macroglobulinemia

Q338

Which of the following is not a major criteria for diagnosis of multiple myeloma? *(AI 2006) Lytic bone lesions Plasmacytoma on tissue biopsy Bone marrow plasmacytosis > 30% 'M' spike > 3g% for Ig G, > 2g% for IgA

Q339

Which is not a minor criteria of Multiple Myeloma? *(AIIMS Nov 2008) Multiple lytic lesions Plasmacytosis > 20% Plasmacytoma in tissue S. IgG > 3gm, IgA > 1.5

Q340

All of the following are minor criteria for multiple Myeloma, Except *(AIIMS Nov 2010) Plasmacytosis 20% Monoclonal Ig spike < 2g/dl for IgA and < 3.5 for IgG

Q341

All seen in multiple myeloma, except : *(AIIMS May 95) Lytic bone lesion Hypercalcemia Plasmacytosis of bone marrow > 2% Decreased serum alkaline phosphatase activity

Q342

The immunoglobulin least commonly involved in Multiple Myeloma is : *(NBE) IgD

Q343

True about electrophoresis in patients of multiplemyeloma- *(NBE) M component refers to the concentration of IgM M component is IgD in 53 % of cases M component is IgM in 53% of the patients M component is IgA in 25% of the patients

Q344

The immunoglobulin least commonly involved in Multiple Myeloma is: *(NBE)

Q345

The most common translocation seen in patients with Multiple Myeloma is *(NBE) t(11;14) t(4;14) t(14;16) t(14;20)

Q346

The most common translocation associated with poor prognosis in patients with Multiple Myeloma is *(NBE)

Q347

All the following are true about multiple myeloma except: *(AI 1994) Osteolytic bone disease t(8-14) translocation Light chain proliferation Bence-Jones proteins in urine

Q348

All of the following are true about multiple myeloma except: *(NBE) t (8-14) translocation Bence-jones proteins in urine

Q349

True about Myeloma is all, except *(AIIMS Nov 01) Plasma cell clonal proliferation Common after 50 yrs of age Amyloidosis can occur Protein casts in urine are made up of complete Ig chains

Q350

Commonest site of lytic lesion, in multiple myeloma is *(AIIMS Dec 97) Vertebral column Femur Clavicle Pelvis

Q351

Which of the following may be seen in Multiple Myeloma: *(PGI June 05) Decreased Calcium Sclerotic bone lesion Bone deposition Renal failure

Q352

The following is the least useful investigation in multiple myeloma : *(AI 2007) ESR X-Ray Bone scan Bone marrow biopsy

Q353

An elderly male presents with headache, recurrent infections and multiple punched out lytic lesions of x-ray skull. The investigation that will best help in establishing a diagnosis is: *(AI 2009)* Protein Electrophoresis Serum calcium Alkaline phosphatase levels Acid phosphatase levels

Q354

Which is not seen in multiple myeloma : *(AIIMS Feb 97) ↑ed ESR

Q355

True regarding multiple myeloma is all, except : *(AI 1998) Lytic lesions Tartare resistance acid phosphatase positive

Q356

Raised serum alkaline phosphatase is seen in all, except *(AIIMS Dec 94) Paget's disease Osteomalacia

Q357

Which one of the following is not a feature of multiple myeloma? *(NBE) Hyperviscosity Elevated alkaline phosphatase

Q358

Maximum ESR is seen in: *(AI 1998) CHF

Q359

In multiple myeloma following are seen except: *(PGI Dec 97) Anion gap raised Polyarticular pain M spike present with polyarticularpathy

Q360

Multiple myeloma is characterized by all except: *(PGI 2000) Presence of light chains Monoclonal gammopathy Polyclonal gammopathy Hypergammaglobulinemia

Q361

True about Multiple myeloma are all Except: *(PGI 2000) Bence Jones protein in urine Hypogammaglobulinemia Amyloidosis Plasmacytosis < 10%

Q362

Russell bodies are found in- *(AI 96) Multiple Myeloma Gonadal tumor Parkinsonism Intracranial neoplasms

Q363

Russell body is found in - *(AIIMS June 97, AI 95) WBC RBC Mast cells Plasma cells

Q364

'Rain drop' skull is seen in: *(NBE) Haemophilia

Q365

All of the following are poor prognostic factors in Multiple Myeloma, Except: *(PGI June 99) Azotemia Hypocalcemia ↑ LDH Chromosoma 13 deletion High M component production

Q366

A patient of multiple myeloma presents with bony lesions. What is the best marker for prognosis of the disease: *(AIIMS June 99; AIIMS 2000)* Bone marrow plasma cell Serum calcium level Beta 2 microglobulin Beta 1 microglobulin

Q367

A 58 years old woman, who had backache and recurrent chest infections for 6 months, develops sudden weakness of the legs and urinary retention. Her investigations show hemoglobin of 7.3 gm/dl, serum calcium 12.6 mg/dl, phosphate 2.5 mg/dl, alkaline phosphatase 100 u/l, serum albumin 3 gm/dl, globulin 7.1 gm/dl, and urea 178 gm/dl. What is the most likely diagnosis ? *(AI 06)* Lung cancer Disseminated tuberculosis Osteoporosis

Q368

Ramla, 65 yrs old male, presents with low back pain especially at L3, anaemia and fatigability. His investigation profile reveals - Hb = 7 gm%, TLC-9000/cmm, DLC -N- 55%, L-30%, M-10%, E-1%, B-2%, Serum proteins-8 gm %, ratio-2.9/5.9, ESR-90 and serum creatinine-3.2 mg%. Likely diagnosis is : *(AIIMS Nov 01)* Waldenströms macroglobulinemia TB spine Secondaries in spine

Q369

Which of the following drugs is not used for the management of multiple myeloma *(NBE) Bortezomib Melphelan Cyclophosphamide

Q370

All of the following drugs are used in the treatment of Multiple myeloma, Except: *(PGI 09) Melpelan

Q371

Drug/s used in management relapsed multiplemyeloma is - *(NBE) Lenalidomide Doxorubicin All the above

Q372

True about smoldering myeloma is: *(PGI June 2008) Bone Marrow Plasma cell < 10%

Q373

An 80 year old asymptomatic woman was detected to have a monoclonal spike on serum electrophoresis (IgG levels 1.5 g/dl). Bone marrow revealed plasma cells of 8%. The most likely diagnosis is: *(AI 2004)* Indolent myeloma Monoclonal gammopathy of unknown significance Waldenstrom's macroglobulinemia

Q374

Ramesh 60 years, presents with generalized bone pain. On examination there is elevated ESR of 100 mm, serum globulin 7, lytic lesions in the skull, serum creatinine of 3.5 mg/dL and serum calcium of 11 mg/dL. What is the most likely diagnosis : *(AIIMS Nov. 2000)* Hyperparathyroidism

Q375

Which of the following statement is not true? *(NBE) Patients with IgD myeloma may present with no evident M-spike on serum electrophoresis. A diagnosis of plasma cell leukemia can be made if circulating peripheral blood plasmablasts comprise 14% of peripheral blood white cells in a patient with white blood cell count of 1 x 10⁹/L and platelet count of 88 x 10⁹/L. In smoldering myeloma plasma cells constitute 10-30% of total bone marrow cellularity. In a patient with multiple myeloma, a monoclonal light chain may be detected in both serum and urine

Q376

A Patient presents with renal failure and bone pain. X-rays show evidence of skeletal destruction. Laboratory evaluation reveals hypercalcemia. Serum Electrophoresis shows a prominent M spike. Tissue biopsy shows Plasmacytoma. Bone marrow shows plasmacytosis with 35 percent Plasma cells. The most likely diagnosis is: *(AIIMS May 2013)* Plasmacytoma MGUS Smoldering Myeloma

Q377

A patient presents with evidence of lytic lesion in the bone. Histology from the bone lesion reveals monclonal plasma cells. Bone marrow biopsy from the from the sternum shows <10 percent Plasma cells. Skeletal Survey is otherwise unremarkable. A small positive M spike is reported on electrophoresis. The diagnosis is: *(AIIMS Nov 99)

Q378

Most common heavy chain disease is: *(AIIMS MAY 2013) Franklin disease Seligmann disease Mu heavy chain dise4ase Waldenstrom cryoglobulinemia

Q379

Franklin's disease is associated with: *(AIIMS May 95) Gamma heavy chain disease Alpha heavy chain disease Waldenstorm's macroglobulinemia

Q380

Heavy chain disease with Kappa light chains in urine: *(NBE) Mu chain disease Seligman disease Waldenstrom macroglobulinemia **General

Q381

Vitamin K dependent coagulation factors include: *(AI 2010) II and III IX and X III and V VIII and XII

Q382

Vitamin K-dependent factor is- *(NBE) II III IV VI

Q383

Which is most likely to be increased in Vit K deficiency: *(AI 2000) P.T.T. P.T. Platelet count Fibrinogen time

Q384

Feature of hemorrhagic disease of new born is- *(AIIMS June 97) Defective platelet count Prolonged bleeding time Prolonged thrombin time

Q385

A 78 year old man who lives alone and prepares his own food is found to have ecchymotic patches over posterior aspect of his lower extremities. He has haemorrhagic areas around hair follicles, hair are fragmented and splinter haemorrhages are present in the nail beds and several hematomas are present in the muscles of arms and legs. PT and CT are normal. This clinical syndrome is most likely due to : *(AIIMS Nov 99)* Vitamin K deficiency Vitamin C deficiency Pyridoxine deficiency Vitamin A deficiency

Q386

A patient is on aspirin, what will be the finding? *(AI 2007) Prolonged BT Prolonged APTT Prolonged CT Prolonged PT

Q387

Platelet function may be assessed by (select two options): *(PGI Dec 02) Platelet adhesion Assays BT CT PTT APTT

Q388

Which of the following is not involved in intrinsic pathway? *(AI 2009) Factor XII Factor XI Factor IX Factor VII

Q389

Which of the following helps in bridging the fibrin in a clot and stabilizes the clot? *(AI 2009) Factor XIII Factor VIII Factor V Factor III

Q390

Converging point of both pathway in coagulation is at *(PGI June 99) Smart factor X

Q391

Which of the following may present with isolated prolongation of prothrombin time (PT): *(NBE) Factor VIII deficiency Factor XII deficiency Factor IX deficiency Factor VII deficiency

Q392

A patient has normal PT and Platelet Count. The aPTT is increased and factor VIII levels are observed to be 60 Iu/dL (60%). There is no associated history of bleeding even after a surgical procedure. The most likely diagnosis is: *(AIIMS Nov 2011)* Lupus anticoagulant Factor VIII inhibitors

Q393

Both PT and aPTT are prolonged in *(NBE) Factor II Deficiency Factor VII Deficiency Heparin Therapy

Q394

A child underwent a tonsillectomy at 6 years of age with no complications. He underwent a preoperative screening for bleeding at the age of 12 years before an elective laparotomy, and was found to have a prolonged partial thromboplastin time but normal Prothrombin time. There was no family history of bleeding. The patient is likely to have- *(AI 2001)* Acquired vitamin K deficiency Acquired liver disease Mild hemophilia A

Q395

A 35 year old lady presents with an isolated prolongation of aPTT. Prothrombin time (PT) and platelet count are normal and there is no obvious bleeding tendency. Two years back, she was operated for cholecystectomy and had no adverse bleeding episode. Which of the following should be the next step in evaluating this patient *(AIIMS Nov 2010)* Factor VIII Assay Platelet aggregation test Russell viper venom assay Ristocetin cofactor assay

Q396

Which of the following statements about coagulation factor VII is not true *(AI 2009) Deficiency is inherited as an Autosomal Recessive trait Deficiency is associated with prolonged APTT Deficiency can be managed by Fresh Frozen plasma Has a shorter half life in comparison to Hageman factor (XII)

Q397

Which of the following drugs requires serial monitoring of Prothrombin time *(NBE) Aspirin Lepirudin Digoxin Coumadin

Q398

Most common inherited bleeding disorder: *(NBE) Von wilebrand disease Bernard soulier Glanzmann thrombasthenia ITP acute

Q399

Spontaneous muscle bleeding is typically seen in: *(NBE) Hemophilia Afibrinogenemia Von Willebrand's disease Scott's syndrome

Q400

All of the following statements about Hemophilia are true, except: *(NBE) X-Linked Recessive Inheritance Hemophilia-A accounts for 80 percent of cases Present with isolated prolongation of the a-PTT assay Desmopressin (DDAVP®) may be used in Mild Hemophilia B

Q401

True about Haemophilia A are all except : *(AI 2001) PTT increased PT increased Clotting time is increased. Serum levels of factor VIII are decreased.

Q402

Hemophilic A has following diagnostic features except- *(AI 97) ↓ VIII factor ↑ PTT ↑ PT Normal BT

Q403

All are true about hemophilia, except *(AIIMS May 94) Increased Bleeding time (BT) Decreased factor VIII Decreased partial thromboplastin time (PTT) Increased factor thromboplastin time

Q404

Which of the following feature may be used to differentiate hemophilia A from von Willebrand disease. *(NBE)* Bleeding time Prothrombin time Partial thromboplastin time Factor VIII levels

Q405

Recurrent Spontaneous Hemarthrosis is commonly seen in patients with Hemophilia when factor VIII levels are: *(NBE)* < 36 % <10% <5% <1 %

Q406

The most common manifestation of moderate /severe Hemophilia A is: *(NBE) Recurrent Hemarthrosis Recurrent muscle bleeding Recurrent bleeding from gums Recurrent Hematuria

Q407

The most common cause of fracture in a patient with Hemophilia is *(NBE) Osteoporosis and Restricted Joint Movement Osteonecrosis Iron deposition Pseudo-tumors

Q408

In a patient of Hemophilia to be taken for dental extraction true is all, except *(AIIMS Nov 01) All patients should be screened for HIV Extraction should be done under general anaesthesia and skilled anaesthetic care Factor VIII or cryoprecipitate can be needed Dose of Lignocaine required for anaesthesia is same as that for normal individuals

Q409

The coagulation profile in a 13-year old girl with Menorrhagia having Von Willebrands disease is: Isolated prolonged PTT with a normal PT Isolated prolonged PT with a normal PTT Prolongation of both PT and PTT Prolongation of thrombin time

Q410

Von Willebrand Disease is associated with : *(AI 97) ↑PTT ; ↓PT; ↑BT; ↑CT ↑PTT ; ↑PT; ↑BT; ↑CT ↑PTT ; Normal PT; ↑BT; Normal CT ↑PTT ; Normal PT; ↑BT; ↑CT

Q411

True about Von Willebrand's disease is all except : *(AIIMS 2000) Increased bleeding time Factor VIII c levels are decreased in circulation. Increased platelet aggregation in response to Ristocetin APTT is increased

Q412

True about Von Willebrand's disease is all except : *(AIIMS 2000) Defect in VWF is detected by ristocetin PT is increased

Q413

A seven year old girl presents with repeated episodes of bleeding into joints. APTT is prolonged and PT is normal. The most likely diagnosis is. *(AI 2009)* Factor VIII deficiency (Hemophilia A) Von Willebrand Disease

Q414

Best assay for deficiency of von Willebrand factor is: *(PGI Dec 01) BT + APTT BT + APTT + vWF-ristocetin factor assay PT

Q415

The commonest mode of inheritance of Von Willebrand's disease : *(AIIMS Dec 97) Codominant Autosomal recessive Autosomal dominant X-linked recessive

Q416

All of the following are inherited platelet function disorders Except: *(NBE) Bernard Soulier syndrome Glanzman Thrombasthenia Von-Willebrand disease Weber-Christian disease

Q417

Wiskott Aldrich syndrome is characterized by all Except: *(AIIMS Nov 2009) Failure of aggregation of platelets in response to agonists Eczema

Q418

A patient presents with Thrombocytopenia, eczema & recurrent infection. The most probable diagnosis is *(NBE)* Wiskott Aldrich syndrome Chediak-Higashi syndrome Job's syndrome Bruton's Agammaglobulinemia

Q419

All of the following statements about Wiskott-Aldrich syndrome are true, Except *(AIIMS Nov 2008) Autosomal Recessive disorder Eczematous Rash Impaired platelet aggregation in response to agonist

Q420

The presence of small sized platelets on the peripheral smear is characteristic of: *(AIIMS Nov. 03) Bernard soulier syndrome Disseminated intravascular coagulation

Q421

All of the following statements about Bernard-Soulier syndrome (BSS) are true, except: *(NBE) Inherited defect in Platelet Adhesion Ristocetin induced Platelet aggregation is normal Large Platelets Deficiency / dysfunction of GpIb / Ix Receptor

Q422

Which is not true regarding Bernard soulier syndrome? *(AI 2007) Ristocetin aggregation is normal Aggregation with collagen and ADP is normal Large platelets

Q423

Thrombocytopenia is not seen in: *(AI 1996) Metastasis

Q424

All of the following can cause megakaryocytic thrombocytopenia, except- *(AIIMS Nov 04) Systemic lupus erythematosus Disseminated intravascular coagulation (DIC)

Q425

Thrombocytopenia occurs in all except: *(AI 2001) Henoch schonlein purpura

Q426

Platelet count is decreased in all of the following condition except: *(AIIMS Dec 98) H.S Purpura

Q427

Auto immune destruction of platelets is seen in *(NBE) Chronic Lymphocytic leukemia

Q428

Autoimmune destruction of platelet is seen in : *(AIIMS May 95) Rheumatoid arthritis Reiter disease

Q429

Palpable purpura could occur in the following conditions, except: *(AI 2005) Thrombocytopenia. Small-vessel vasculitis. Disseminated gonococcal infection. Acute meningoccemia.

Q430

Palpable purpura is seen in *(AIIMS May 94) Idiopathic thrombocytopenic purpura (ITP) Scurvy Henoch Sconlein Purpura Monoclonal cryoglobulinemia

Q431

Cause of ITP is : *(AIIMS Feb 97) Vasculitis Antibody to vascular epithelium Antibody to platelets Antibody to clotting factors

Q432

Secondary Immune thrombocytopenic purpura is most commonly associated with- *(NBE) Hepatitis A infection Hepatitis B infection Hepatitis C infection Hepatis D infection

Q433

Which of the following is the least likely cause of secondary/idiopathic thrombocytopenic purpura? HIV infection

Q434

Which is the most common cause of NEW ONSET immune mediated thrombocytopenia in an ICU patient? *(NBE)* Bone marrow failure

Q435

Idiopathic thrombocytopenic purpura is associated with all of the following Except: *(NBE) Mucosal bleeding Increased megakaryocytes

Q436

Which of the following statements about Acute Immune Thrombocytopenic Purpura is not true: Autoimmune Mediated Massive Splenomegaly Increased Megakaryocytes in marrow IV immunoglobulins may be required Usually self limiting condition

Q437

All of the following features about Idiopathic Thrombocytopenia are true, Except: *(AIIMS Nov 99) Bleeding into the joints is a common manifestation Bone marrow will show increased megakaryocytes Platelet count is decreased Condition resolves spontaneously in 80% of patients in 2 to 6 weeks

Q438

A six year old boy presents with acute onset ecchymoses and petichiae all over the body 2 weeks after an upper respiratory tract infection Abdominal examination is unremarkable with no hepatosplenomegaly All of the following statements about the affecting condition are true Except: *(AIIMS Nov 99)* Acute ITP is more common in children Acute ITP is more common in males Platelet transfusion is not indicated in Dilutional Thrombocytopenia Platelet transfusion is not indicated in Immunogenic Thrombocytopenia

Q439

The following laboratory determinants is abnormally prolonged in ITP : *(AI 2002) Prothombin time Clotting time

Q440

Platelet transfusion is not indicated in: *(AI 1996) Dilutional Thrombocytopenia Immunogenic Thrombocytopenia

Q441

All of the following feature may be seen in thrombotic thrombocytopenic purpura, except : *(AI Haemolysis Hypertension Low platelets count

Q442

All of the following statements about Thrombotic thrombocytopenic purpura (TTP) are true, Except : *(AI 2002)* Microangiopathic Hemolytic Anemia Normal complement levels Grossly abnormal coagulation tests

Q443

Thrombotic thrombocytopenic purpura is a syndrome characterized by: *(NBE) Thrombocytosis, anemia, neurologic abnormalities, progressive renal failure and fever Thrombocytopenia, anemia, neurologic abnormalities, progressive hepatic failure and fever Thrombocytosis, anemia neurologic abnormalities, progressive renal failure and fever Thrombocytopenia, anemia, neurological abnormalities, progressive renal failure and fever

Q444

All of the following are features of Thrombotic Thrombocytopenic purpura, Except *(AIIMS June 90) Low platelet count

Q445

A person presents with fever and altered consciousness. Investigations reveal anemia with fragmented red blood cells, platelet count of 20,000/mm³, serum creatinine of 3.0 mg % and normal PT and aPTT. Which of the following is the most appropriate treatment for the patient: *(PGI 09)* Plasma Exchange Therapy Corticosteroids and Intravenous Immunoglobulins Anticoagulation with Heparin Platelet transfusion

Q446

All of the following are used in the treatment of Thrombotic Thrombocytopenic Purpura, Except *(PGI-09)* Plasmapharesis Corticosteroids Immunotherapy Heparin

Q447

False statement regarding DIC is: *(AI 2001) Decreased fibrinogen Decreased PTT Increased PT

Q448

The following is the finding seen in DIVC : *(AI 2007) Increased fibrinogen, increased antithrombin III, increased thrombin-antithrombin III complexes Increased FDP, decreased PT, increased antithrombin III Increased FDP, prolonged PT, increased thrombin-antithrombin complexes Increased FDP, prolonged PT, reduced Plalets

Q449

The most sensitive test for DIC is: *(AI 2001) Serum fibrinogen levels Serum levels of fibrin degradation products (FDP) Prolonged PT and PTT

Q450

Which of the following features is NOT seen in DIC? *(NBE) Hyperfibrinogenemia Consumption coagulopathy

Q451

Bleeding in DIC is most closely related to: *(NBE) Raised fibrin degradation products level in blood Prolong prothrombin time Low serum fibrinogen level Raised thrombin time

Q452

DIC is commonly seen in- *(NBE) M2 AML M3 AML M4 AML

Q453

Disseminated intravascular coagulation (DIC) differs from thrombotic thrombocytopenic purpura. In this reference from the DIC is most likely characterized by: *(AI 2004)* Significant numbers of schistocytes A brisk reticulocytosis Decreased coagulation factor levels Significant thrombocytopenia

Q454

All are hypercoagulable states, except : *(AIIMS June 98) Protein C resistance Factor V Leiden deficiency Antiphospholipid antibody Polycythemia

Q455

All of the following conditions are associated with venous and arterial thrombotic events, except: *(AIIMS May 2011)* Paroxysmal Nocturnal Haemoglobinuria (PNH) Disseminated Intravascular Coagulation Idiopathic Thrombocytopenic Purpura (ITP) Heparin Induced Thrombocytopenia (HIT)

Q456

All of the following conditions predispose to thrombosis except: *(AIIMS Dec 98) Homocysteinemia Behcets syndrome Dysfibrinogenemia

Q457

Coagulation defects associated with ↑ed coagulation are seen in: *(PGI Dec 06) ↑ Protein C ↑ Protein B ↑ Anti thrombin III

Q458

All of the following are true about Factor V Leiden Mutation, except: *(NBE) Lifelong increased Risk of DVT Glutamine to Arginine substitution at position 506 Results in Activated Protein C Resistance (APCR) Most common inherited cause of thrombophilia

Q459

Most common inherited cause of thrombophilia *(AIIMS Nov. 03) Lisbon mutation Leiden mutation Antiphospholipid syndrome Inducible thrombocytopenia syndrome

Q460

Most common inherited thrombotic disorder is : *(PGI Dec. 05) Protein C deficiency

Q461

Predisposing factor for arterial thrombosis: *(PGI Dec. 04) AT III deficiency Protein S deficiency Homocystenemia

Q462

All of the following are aquired causes of Hypercoagulability, Except *(AI 2009) Inflammatory Bowel disease Myeloproliferative disorders Prolonged surgery > 1 hour

Q463

Causes of Deep venous thrombosis include all of the following, Except. *(AI 2009) Diabetes Mellitus Oral contraceptives Prolonged surgery

Q464

A patient is admitted with 3rd episode of deep venous thrombosis. There is no history of any associated medical illness. All of the following investigations are required for establishing the diagnosis except : *(AIIMS Nov 2004)* Antithrombin III deficiency Antibodies to factor VIII Antibodies to cardiolipin

Anti Phospholipid Syndrome (APS) is associated with all of the following Except: *(AI 2008) Recurrent abortions

Q466

Antiphospholipid Antibody (APLA) syndrome is associated with all of the following except: *(AI Bleeding disorders Thrombotic disorders Recurrent fetal loss

Q467

All of the following statements about Antiphospholipid Antibody Syndrome (APLAb) are true, Except : *(NBE)* Single titre of Anticardiolipin is diagnostic May cause pulmonary hypertension May cause pulmonary hypertension with recurrent fetal loss Warfarin is given as treatment

Q468

The following condition is not associated with an Anti-phospholipid syndrome: *(AI 2002) Recurrent foetal loss Neurological manifestations

Q469

Patients with Antiphospholipid antibodies may manifest with any of the following, except: *(NBE) Arterial Thrombosis Hemolytic Anemia

Q470

All are true regarding lupus anticoagulant except- *(AIIMS Sep 96) Increased abortions Rashes Arterial thrombosis

Q471

All are true regarding Lupus anticoagulant, EXCEPT- *(AIIMS Nov 93)

Q472

All of the following statements about Lupus Anticoagulant are true, Except. *(AI 2009) May present with an isolated prolongation of APTT May present with Recurrent Abortions May occur with minimal clinical manifestations Thrombotic spells can be followed by severe life threatening haemorrhage

Q473

Lupus anticoagulants may cause all of the following except: *(NBE) Recurrent abortion False+ve VDRL results Increase prothrombin time

Q474

Q475

Commonest cause of Budd Chiari syndrome is- *(AI 97) Valve in the IVC

Q476

Most common cause of Budd Chiari syndrome is: *(AIIMS May 94) Idiopathic Valves in hepatic veins Thrombosis of hepatic veins

Q477

In Budd Chiari syndrome, the site of venous thrombosis is- *(PGI June 04) Infrahepatic inferior vena cava Infrarenal inferior vena cava Hepatic veins Portal veins

Q478

What is the most common cause for Budd chiary syndrome- *(AIIMS Nov 2000) Right ventricular failure Valve in hepatic veins

Q479

All of the following about Methemoglobinemia are true, except: *(NBE) Formed when iron in Hb is converted from Fe2+ to the Fe2+ state Presents with cyanosis unresponsive to supplemental oxygen Muddy appearance of freshly drawn blood may be seen All of the above are true

Which of the following is the treatment of choice for Acquired Methemoglobinemia *(NBE) Ascorbic Acid Crystal Violet Methylene Blue Red Cell Exchange

Blood transfusion should be completed within how many hours of initiation: *(NBE) 4 hours 6 hours 8 hours 12 hours

Q483

Shelf life of blood in a blood bank in CPDA buffer: *(NBE) 21 days 30 days 35 days 42 days

Q486

Q487

TRALI occurs within how many hours of transfusion? *(NBE) 6 Hrs 12 Hrs 48 Hrs 72 Hrs

Q488

MC blood transfusion reaction is- *(AI 2008) Febrile nonhemolytic transfusion reaction Transmission of infections Electrolyte imbalance

Q489

All of the following infections may be transmitted via blood transfusion, except: *(AI 2002) Parvo B-19 Dengue virus Cytomegalo virus Hepatitis G virus

Q490

Massive transfusion is defined as transfusionof *(NBE) 10 or more Red Cell Products in 24 Hours 10 or more Red Cell Products in 48 Hours 5 or more Red Cell Products in 24 Hours

Q491

Which of the following is the least likely complication after massive blood transfusion: *(AIIMS May 09)* Hyperkalemia Citrate toxicity Hypothermia Metabolic Acidosis

All of the following are major complications of massive transfusion, except: *(AI 2006) Hypokalemia Hypomagnesemia

Q493

Which of the following is/are complications of massive blood transfusion? *(NBE)

Q494

Which of the following investigations should be done immediately to best confirm a non matched blood transfusion reaction: *(AI 2010)* Indirect Coomb's test Direct Coomb's test Antibody in patient's serum Antibody in donor serum

Q495

Platelets transfusion must be completed in how many hours after entering the bag: *(NBE) 1 hour 2 hour 3 hour 4 hour

Q496

Shelf life of platelets in blood bank is: *(NBE) 5 days 7 days 10 days

Q497

Half life transfused platelets is: *(NBE) 1 day 2 days 4 days 8 days

Q498

Storage temperature of platelets is: *(NBE) -4 degrees Celsius + 4 degrees Celsius -20 degrees Celsius + 20-24 degrees Celsius

Q499

Incorrect about platlet transfusion: *(NBE) Platelets carry the Rh antigen Platelets bear the antigens of ABO system Platelet products contain donor plasma

Q500

Cryoprecipiate contains all except- *(AIIMS Nov 07) Fibrinogen VWF

Q501

Arterial blood Gas analysis in a bottle containing heparin causes a decrease in value of : *(NBE) pCO2 HCO3- pH

Q1

  • A. Werner's Syndrome
  • B. Sipple Syndrome
  • C. Weber's Syndrome
  • D. Morrison Syndrome

  • A. MEN 1
  • B. MEN 2a
  • C. MEN 2b
  • D. None of the Above
Q3

A 20 year old male presented with chronic constipation, headache and palpitations. On examination he had marfanoid habitus, neuromas of tongue, medullated corneal nerve fibers and nodule of 2x2 cm size in the left lobe of thyroid gland. This patient is a case of: *(AI 2004)

  • A. Sporadic medullary carcinoma of thyroid
  • B. Familial medullary carcinoma of thyroid
  • C. MEN IIA
  • D. MEN IIB

  • A. Insulinoma
  • B. Gastrinoma
  • C. Glucagonoma
  • D. Somatostatinoma

  • C. Pituitary
  • D. Parathyroid

  • A. Gastrinoma
  • B. Insulinoma
  • D. Somatistatinoma

  • B. Pheochromocytoma

  • A. MEN - 1
  • B. MEN - 2
  • C. MEN - 3
  • D. MEN - 4

  • B. Urine VMA and aspiration of the thyroid nodule
  • C. Ultrasound abdomen
Q19

The physical examination reveals a thyroid nodule but no clinical sign of thyrotoxicosis. Before performing thyroid surgery, the surgeon should order: *(AIIMS Nov 02)

  • A. Measurement of thyroid hormones.
  • B. Serial determinations of serum calcium, phosphorus protein and alkaline phosphatase.
  • C. 24-hours urine test for 5 hydroxyindoleacetic acid excretion.
  • D. Serial 24 hours test for catecholamines, metanephrines and vanillylmandelic acid excretion.

  • D. Type 4

A common cause of Cushing syndrome is: *(AIIMS May 93)

  • A. Cancer producing ectopic ACTH
  • B. Pituitary adenoma

  • A. Addison's disease
  • B. Cushing's syndrome

  • A. Adrenal carcinoma
  • B. Oat cell carcinoma of lung
  • C. Medulloblastoma
  • D. Pituitary adenoma

All of the following are clinical features of Cushing's syndrome except: *(NBE)

  • A. Insulin resistance
  • B. Menorrhagia
  • C. Violaceous striae
  • D. Centripetal obesity

  • A. Conn's syndrome

  • A. Red striae
  • B. Increased adrenaline

Cushing syndrome is characterized by all except: *(PGI June 98)

  • A. DM
  • B. HT

Features not seen in Cushing's Syndrome is: *(AI 1997)

  • C. Frank psychosis

All are features of Cushing's disease except: *(AIIMS Nov 07)

  • A. Central obesity
  • B. Episodic hypertension
  • C. Easy bruising
  • D. Glucose intolerance

  • A. Increased ACTH and increased catecholamines
  • B. Increased ACTH and increased cortisol
  • C. Increased urinary Catecholamines
  • D. Increased ADH

About Cushing syndrome, true is: *(PGI June 2000)

  • A. Low dose dexamethasone suppresses corticosone secretion
  • B. CA of adrenal is more common than adenoma
  • C. Pituitary adenoma size > 2 cm (usually)
  • D. ↑ ACTH secretion is the commonest endogenous cause

A patient with cushinoid features presents with hemoptysis; he shows no response to dexamethasone suppression test; most likely diagnosis here is: *(AI 2001)

  • A. Adrenal hyperplasia
Q35

Lalloo, 50 years old, a chronic smoker, presents with history of hemoptysis. He was having truncal obesity and hypertension. He had an elevated ACTH level which was not suppressible with high dose dexamethasone. What would be the most probable diagnosis: *(AIIMS Nov. 2000)

  • A. Bilateral adrenal hyperplasia
  • C. Pituitary tumour
  • D. Ectopic ACTH producing lung cancer
Q36

A chronic smoker presented with mild haemoptysis. He also gave a history of hypertension and obesity. The Lab data showed raised ACTH levels, which were not suppressed by high dose dexamethasone. The cause for the Cushing's syndrome in the patient is: *(AIIMS May 02)

  • A. MEN I
  • C. Adrenal cortical adenoma
  • D. Ectopic ACTH secreting tumor

  • A. Hypokalemic alkalosis
  • B. Clinical features of cushing syndrome
  • C. Hyperpigmentation
  • D. Hypertension
Q38

Which of the following is the earliest manifestation of Cushing's syndrome? *(AI 2004)

  • A. Loss of diurnal variation
  • B. Increased ACTH
  • C. Increased plasma Cortisol
  • D. Increased urinary metabolites of Cortisol
Q39

  • A. Serum electrolytes
  • B. Plasma cortisol
  • C. Plasma testosterone and ultrasound evaluation of pelvis
  • D. T3, T4 and TSH

Conn's syndrome is most commonly associated with: *(AI 2008)

  • A. Cortical adenoma
  • B. Cortical hyperplasia
  • C. Cortical carcinoma
  • D. Pheochromocytoma

All of the following may be associated with primary aldosteronism, Except: *(PGI June 05)

  • A. Adrenal adenoma
  • B. Adrenal hyperplasia
  • C. Von-Hippel-Lindau syndrome
  • D. Adrenal carcinoma
Q42

Conn's syndrome is associated with all, except: *(AI 2002)

  • B. Muscle weakness
  • C. Hypokalemia
Q43

Increased Aldosterone leads to all except: *(AI 2009)

  • B. Hypokalemia
Q44

NOT a feature of primary hyperaldosteronism is *(AIIMS Dec 04/AI-1998)

  • A. Pedal edema
  • B. Diastolic hypertension
  • C. Polyuria

  • A. Diastolic HTN without oedema
  • B. Metabolic Alkalosis
  • C. Increased aldosterone
  • D. High plasma renin

  • C. Hypernatremia
  • D. Sodium retention

  • D. Increased renin level
Q49

The following are feature of primary hyperaldosteronism: *(PGI June 97)

True about Conn's syndrome (select two best options): *(PGI Dec 02)

  • A. ↑K+
  • B. ↓ K+
  • D. ↑ed plasma rennin activity
  • E. Edema
Q51

True about primary aldosteronism (select three options): *(PGI June 05)

  • A. Pedal oedema

  • B. Ectopic ACTH syndrome
  • C. Diuretic therapy
  • E. Liddle's syndrome

In primary adrenal hyperplasia, incorrect is: *(AIIMS May 94)

  • B. Hypernatremia
  • D. Water retention

In primary adrenal hyperplasia, incorrect is *(AIIMS May 94)

  • A. Autoimmune
  • B. Surgery
  • C. Steroid withdrawal
  • D. Tuberculosis

  • B. Postpartum
  • C. HIV

  • B. Cryptococcus
  • C. Pneumocystis

  • D. Fludrocortisones

Which type diabetes is HLA associated: *(AI 2002)

  • A. Type I diabetes
  • B. Type II diabetes
  • C. Malnutrition related type disease
  • D. Pregnancy related type diabetes

  • A. It is an autoimmune disease
  • B. It is non-insulin-dependent
  • C. It is not associated with thyroid disorder
  • D. Insulin resistance is the hallmark of the disease pathogenesis

Major susceptible gene for type 1 DM located on chromosome *(NBE)

  • A. 1
  • B. 5
  • C. 6
  • D. 12

  • A. 10 and 30
  • B. 20 and 60
  • C. 30 and 70
  • D. 50 and 90
Q76

What is the percentage of concordance of type 2 DM in identical twins *(NBE)

  • B. 30 and 50
  • C. 50 and 70
  • D. 70 and 90

Risk of developing DM in individual having both parents with type 2 DM *(NBE)

  • A. ~10%
  • B. ~20%
  • C. ~30%
  • D. ~40%

In type 2 DM increased fasting plasma glucose is predominantly due to *(NBE)

  • A. Increased hepatic glucose output
Q79

  • B. Decreased peripheral utilization of glucose

Q80

A 45-year old woman visited her physician with complaints of increased appetite and thirst with increased frequency of urination. She also had the symptoms of diminished or impalpable pulses in the feet, besides gangrene of the feet. Her laboratory findings on the oral glucose tolerance test are as follows: *(AI | Parameters | Fasting | 1 hr | 2 hr | | Blood glucose (mg/dL) | 155 | 270 | 205 | | Urine glucose | -ve | +++ | ++ | | Ketone bodies | -ve | -ve | -ve | Which of the following statements is not correct for the above mentioned case:

  • A. She was suffering from insulin dependent diabetes mellitus
  • B. She was suffering from non-insulin dependent diabetes mellitus
  • C. She was treated with oral hypoglycemic drugs only when diet and exercise could not control the
  • D. Knowledge of family history of diabetes mellitus is useful in predicting the nature of the diabetes
Q81

  • A. MODY
  • B. DM type I
  • C. DM type II
  • D. Pancreatic diabetes

All of the following statements about Type I Diabetes Mellitus are true, Except (select two options): *(PGI Dec 01)* Family history is present in 90% cases Dependent on insulin to prevent ketoacidosis Time of onset is usually predictable Autoimmune destruction of beta cells occur Often occurs in children

Q85

All of the following are true about Type 1 DM, Except: *(AIIMS May 02) Family history is present in 90% of cases Antibodies against β cells Prone to Diabetic Ketoacidosis (DKA) Insulin is required for management of DKA

In a patient with NIDDM which of the following condition is seen : *(AIIMS May 02) Ketosis commonly occurs on stopping treatment. Hypertriglyceridemia never occurs Pancreatic beta cells stop producing insulin Increased levels of insulin in blood, may be seen

In diabetes all of the following statement are correct; except: *(PGI) Patients with type 2 diabetes never require insulin treatment Maturity Onset Diabetes of the Young (MODY) is an autosomal recessive condition The majority of type 2 diabetes patients die from cardiovascular disease Weight loss is a common symptom of type 1 diseases Recognized endocrine causes of diabetes include Acromegaly and Cushing's syndrome

Which of the following is not a test for diabetes mellitus *(AIIMS Nov 2010) Fasting blood glucose Random blood glucose D-Xylose test Oral Glucose tolerance test

Q92

Which of the following indicates Prediabetes: *(NBE) Fasting Glucose <126 mg/dl 2h PG Glucose <200 mg/dl HbA1C < 7

Q93

Q94

For diagnosis of diabetes mellitus, fasting blood glucose level should be more than: *(NBE) 126 mg/dl 140 mg/dl 100 mg/dl 200 mg/dl

Q95

A 42 year old male has strong positive Benedict's test, random blood sugar is > 163 mg%, fasting blood sugar is > 200mg%. Next line of investigation is: *(AIIMS June 99)* Urine glucose charting 5 hourly Oral GTT Repeat benedict's test 24 hr urine sugar estimation

At what value for one hour glucose challenge test will you recommend a standard glucose tolerance test: *(AIIMS May 05)* 120 mg/dl 150 mg/dl 160 mg/dl

Q97

HBA1C level in blood explains: *(AI 2004) Acute rise of sugar Long terms status of blood sugar Hepatorenal syndrome Chronic pancreatitis

Q98

Diabetes control is best monitored by: *(PGI Dec 98) Serum glucose Post prandial blood glucose HbA1C HbA2C

Q99

Glycaemic control in diabetes is best assessed by: *(NBE) HbAIC Urinary glucose Fasting glucose Post prandial glucose

The most common dyslipidaemia associated Diabetes mellitus is: *(NBE) ↑ HDL ↑ Triglycerides ↓ triglycerides ↓ cholesterol

Q101

Dyslipidemia is more commonly associated with: *(NBE) Type 1 Diabetes Type 2 Diabetes Both None

Necrobiosis lipoidica is classically associated with *(NBE) Scurvy. Diabetes mellitus. Sickle cell anemia.

All are seen in DKA except: *(AIIMS Nov 07) Tachycardia Dehydration Bradycardia Abdominal pain/tenderness

The expected level of blood glucose for this lady is: *(AIIMS May 03)* < 45 mg/dl. < 120 mg/dl. > 180 mg/dl. > 75 mg/dl.

The drug of choice for managing hyperglycemia in diabetic ketoacidosis is: *(NBE) Regular insulin Lente insulin Glyburide 70/30 insulin

All of the following statements about Diabetes Ketoacidosis are true, Except: *(NBE) DKA is commoner in type I Anion gap is increased Serum PH <7.4 Serum bicarbonate in DKA is >15 meq/L

In diabetic ketoacidosis which of the following is the initial iv fluid choice? *(NBE) 0.9% sodium chloride solution Ringer's lactate solution 5-10% dextrose

Which of the following is recommended in patients of Diabetic Ketoacidosis presenting with abdominal Pain to exclude Acute Pancreatitis *(NBE)* Lipase Amylase

If a patient with severe hyperglycemia is given IV insulin, which of the following can occur? *(AI Hypernatremia

Which of the following is the most likely diagnosis in this case : *(AIIMS Nov 2004)* Pituitary macroadenoma Ophthalmic ICA aneurysm Chiasmal glioma

All are associated with pituitary apoplexy except: *(AIIMS May 07)

Which drug is essential in Sheehan's syndrome: *(AI 1996) Cortisone Thyroxin Growth hormone

A person sustains head trauma leading to transaction of pituitary stalk. All can be consequence of this, except: *(AIIMS Dec 97)* SIADH

A patient meets with an accident with resultant transaction of the pituitary stalk; what will NOT occur: *(AI 01)* Hypothyroidism

SIADH was first described in patients with *(NBE) Head trauma Bronchogenic carcinoma Schizophrenia

SIADH secretion is seen in all except : *(AI 1994) Lung abscess Interstitial Nephritis Vinka alkaloids Bronchial adenoma

Q171

SIADH is associated with the following drug : *(AI 2007) Vincristine Erythromycin 5 – FU Methotrexate

Q172

SIADH is associated with : *(AIIMS Dec 97) Small cell carcinoma lung Adeno carcinoma lung Squamous cell carcinoma lung Mixed cell tumor lung

Q174

Inappropriate ADH secretion is characterised by the following except : *(AI 2000) Hypo-osmolar urine Water intoxication Expanded fluid volume

Q177

Q178

All are true regarding SIADH except: *(AIIMS Nov 93) Increased level of ADH Urine hyposmolar Adequate hydration status

Q183

All of the following conditions are known to cause diabetes insipidus, except : *(AIIMS May 04) Multiple sclerosis Head injury Histiocytosis Viral encephalitis

Q184

Nephrogenic diabetes insipidus refers to *(NBE) ADH deficiency. Insulin insensitivity. Renal tabular unresponsiveness to ADH. Increased renal reabsorption of salt and water.

Q185

In a patient if administration of exogenous vasopressin does not increase the osmolality of urine the likely cause is *(NBE)* Psychogenic polydipsia Renal Hyposensitivity to ADH ADH Deficiency

Q186

A 33 year old lady presents with polydipsia and polyuria. Her symptoms started soon after a road traffic accident 6 months ago Further investigations revealed low plasma osmolality, low urine osmolality and a decreased serum sodium. The most likely diagnosis is: *(AIIMS Nov 02)* Central diabetes insipidus Nephrogenic diabetes insipidus Resolving acute tubular necrosis

Q187

Which of the following statements about Diabetes Insipidus is true: *(AI 2011) Urine osmolality should be > 300 mosm/L Plasma osmolality should be < 280 mmol/L Water deprivation test is required Plasma osmolality should be > 300 mosm/L prior to H2O Deprivation

Q188

Choose the best Lab value for a patient with diabetes insipidus : *(AI 2000) | Urinary Osmolality | Serum Osmolality | | :--- | :--- | | A. 50 | 300 | | B. 500 | 260 | | C. 50 | 260 | | D. 500 | 100 |

Q189

A patient is observed to have urine output of 3.5 litres, serum sodium 156 mEq/l and urine osmolality of 316 mOsm/kg. The most likely diagnosis based on these parameters is *(NBE)* Solute Diuresis Nephrogenic Diabetes insipidus Central Diabetes Insipidus Primary Polydipsia

Q190

Mainstay of treatment of Nephrogenic Diabetes Insipidus is: *(NBE) Thiazide / Amiloride diuretics and salt restriction Desmopressin and salt restriction Vasopressin and salt restriction

Q191

Mainstay of treatment of Neurogenic (central) Diabetes Insipidus is *(NBE) Vasopressin Terlipressin Amiodarone

Q192

Treatment of choice for polyuria in lithium induced diabetes insipidus is: *(NBE) Mineralocorticoid antagonist Loop diuretic

Q193

Most common cause of hyperparathyroidism: *(AIIMS Nov 93) Iatrogenic Medullary carcinoma thyroid Parathyroid adenoma Parathyroid hyperplasia

Q194

Most common cause of primary hyperparathyroidism is: *(AI 1997) Solitary adenoma Chief cell hyperplasia Multiple adenoma

Q195

The commonest cause of primary hyperparathyroidism is : *(AIIMS May 95) Carcinoma parathyroid Solitary adenoma of the parathyroid Chronic renal failure Hyperplasia of the parathyroid

Q196

A common cause of Primary hyperparathyroidism is: *(AI 1995) Multiple parathyroid adenomas Solitary parathyroid adenoma Adrenal hyperplasia Ectopic PTH production

Q197

Asymptomatic hypercalcemia in a 30 year old young male is due to : *(AI 2000; AIIMS June 99) Occult primary malignancy Primary Hyperparathyroidism Familial hypocalciuria Hyper-nephroma

Q198

All are TRUE about hyperparathyroidism, Except: *(NBE) Commonly occurs after thyroidectomy May cause hypercalcemia Solitary adenoma is the most common cause

Q199

Subtle presentation of hyperparathyroidism is: *(AIIMS Dec 97) Psychiatric manifestation Nephrocalcinosis Asymptomatic hypercalcemia

Q200

Which of the following is associated with secondary hyperparathyroidism *(AIIMS Dec 94) Marked hypercalcemia Parathyroidectomy relieves the symptoms

Q201

Secondary Hyperparathyroidism is seen in all of the following, Except *(AIIMS Nov 2010) Rickets

Q202

Increased Calcium with Decreased Phosphate levels are seen in: *(NBE) Primary hyperparathyroidism Secondary hyperparathyroidism due to Vitamin D deficiency

Q203

Secondary Hyperparathyroidism is seen in all of the following, Except *(NBE) Chronic Renal Failure Vitamin D Deficiency Medullary Carcinoma Thyroid

Q204

Secondary hyperparathyroidism may be caused by *(NBE) Acute renal insufficiency. Chronic renal insufficiency. Aluminium intoxication

Q205

A 45 year old man, known case of chronic renal failure develops rugger jersey spine. The probable cause is: *(AI 2000)* Secondary hyperparathyroidism

Q207

Rugger jersey spine is seen in: *(PGI June 98) Myeloma CRF Ankylosing spondylitis

Q208

In hyperparathyroidism all are seen except: *(PGI Dec 98) Osteopetrosis Cysts Brown tumor

Q209

  • B. Involvement of small joints

  • C. Hyperlipidemia

  • C. Thiazide diuretics
  • D. Digitalis

  • A. Thiazides
  • D. High Dose Salicylates

  • A. 90% of cases are caused by over production of uric acid
  • B. Uric acid levels may be normal at the time of an acute attack
  • C. Men are more commonly affected than women (Male > Females)
  • D. Definitive diagnosis requires aspiration of synovial fluid
Q220

  • A. Aspirate of joint fluid for microscopy to identify the needle shaped crystals is confirmatory
  • B. Raised Serum levels of uric acid are diagnostic of acute gout
  • C. 24 hour urine for uric acid levels is used for assessing the risk of uric acid stones
  • D. Synovial fluid leukocyte counts are elevated during the acute attack

  • A. Occurs due to accumulation of urate crystals in joint
  • B. Can be pptd by pyrazinamide
  • C. Birefringement crystals are present in joint
  • D. Occurs more in females
  • E. Due to decreased excretion of uric acid

  • B. HLA-B 5801 gene marker.
  • C. HLA-B 27 gene marker.

  • B. Benzbromarone
  • C. Pegloticase

  • B. Colchicine
  • C. Allopurinol
  • D. Glucocorticoids

  • B. Allopurinol should be started immediately
  • C. Colchicine is known to provide relief
  • D. Serum uric acid levels may be normal

  • A. Uric acid nephrolithiasis
  • B. Deficiency of enzyme Xanthine oxidase
  • C. Increase in serum urate concentration
  • D. Renal disease involving interstitial tissues
Q228

What is not true about gout: *(AIIMS May 95)

  • A. Abrupt increase in serum urate levels is more common a cause for acute gout than an abrupt fall in urate
  • B. Patient may be asymptomatic with high serum uric acid for years
  • C. Development of arthritis correlates with level of serum uric acid
  • D. Uric acid crystals are best seen by polarising light microscope
Q229

False regarding gouty arthritis is: *(AIIMS Nov 93)

  • A. Synovial fluid analysis is diagnostic
  • B. Allopurinol is the treatment of choice in acute gout
  • C. Arthritis occurs after long attack of hyperuricemia
  • D. Levels of uric acid in blood and severity of gout has good correlation
Q230

Specific test for gout is: *(AIIMS Sept 96)

  • A. Raised serum uric acid level
  • B. Raised uric acid in synovial fluid of joint
  • C. Raised urea level
  • D. Raised urease enzyme level

Rhomboidal Crystals with Weakly positive birefringence on compensated polarized light microscopy suggest *(NBE)

  • C. Infection with a crystal-producing organism
Q232

All are used in treatment of acute gout, EXCEPT: *(AIIMS May 93)

  • B. Naproxen
  • D. Colchime

  • A. Charcot's joint
  • B. Clutton's joint

A 60-year old man with diabetes mellitus presents with painless, swollen right ankle joint.

  • D. Arthrogryposis multiple congenital

  • A. Fabry's disease
  • B. Wilson's disease

  • B. Testicular atrophy
  • D. Chronic active hepatitis

  • A. It has autosomal dominant inheritance
  • B. Kayser-Fleischer ring is diagnostic of Neurologic Wilson's disease
  • C. Low ceruloplasmin can be found in asymptomatic carriers
  • D. Treatment with D-Penicillamine can initially worsen neurological status

  • B. Serum ceruloplasmin level is < 20 mg/dl
  • C. Urinary copper excretion is <100 µg/dl
  • D. Zinc acetate is effective as maintenance therapy

  • A. Total Serum Copper is increased
  • B. Ceruloplasmin levels are reduced
  • C. Liver Copper is increased
  • D. Urinary copper is increased

  • B. Low serum ceruloplasmin and high urinary copper
  • C. High serum ceruloplasmin and low urinary copper
  • D. High serum ceruloplasmin and high urinary copper
